tasks.title: Core Models and Abstract Interfaces Setup
tasks.description: Define the core normalized UsageEvent Pydantic model and abstract interfaces (UsageSource, LimitsSource, PricingStrategy) for the shared core package (tokemetry_core). Implement the FakeProvider test double to validate interfaces without Claude-specific assumptions.
tasks.details: Core package implemented: `models.py` includes `UsageEvent`, `DailyAggregate`, `LimitSnapshot`, `SourceFile`, `ParseResult`, `PriceRow`, and `Provenance` enum; all models are frozen Pydantic v2 with tz-aware timestamp validation and non-negative counts. `interfaces.py` defines `UsageSource`, `LimitsSource`, `PricingStrategy` ABCs plus `LimitsUnavailableError` for graceful degradation. `registry.py` implements `ProviderRegistry` with factories for sources and instances for pricing. `providers/fake.py` contains deterministic `FakeProvider` as reference implementation. Design deviations documented in `docs/architecture/core-package.md`: `parse` returns `ParseResult` (events + new_offset atomically) instead of iterator; `poll` returns list of snapshots (multi-window). Gates: 31 tests pass, coverage 99%, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Run pytest on `packages/core` with 100% pass rate. Verify `UsageEvent` serialization/deserialization with fixtures. Confirm `FakeProvider` implements all abstract methods without errors. Check coverage >= 99% line, 99% branch. Validate ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues. Confirm 31 tests pass.

tasks.title: Anthropic Pricing Strategy and LiteLLM Sync
tasks.description: Implement the Anthropic pricing strategy with date-versioned per-MTok rates per provider/model, synced from LiteLLM's model_prices_and_context_window.json. Add manual override support and handle dated/undated model IDs.
tasks.details: The pricing subpackage is now implemented: `litellm.py` (pure transform of LiteLLM price db to per-MTok PriceRows, skips platform-prefixed aliases, fallback multipliers 1.25x/2x/0.1x when cache rates missing), `table.py` (PricingTable with date-versioned resolution, dated/undated model id matching both directions, apply_overrides for TOML manual overrides with field validation, UnknownModelError), and `anthropic.py` (AnthropicPricingStrategy linear dot product quantized to micro-USD, DEFAULT_ANTHROPIC_PRICE_ROWS verified snapshot). `providers/anthropic.py` registers the strategy. HTTP fetching is deliberately kept out of core - the server owns it. Docs: `docs/architecture/pricing.md`. Gates: 51 tests, 98.85% coverage, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean. Create `packages/core/tokemetry_core/pricing/anthropic.py` implementing `PricingStrategy` (or verify the existing implementation matches the spec). Fetch `model_prices_and_context_window.json` from LiteLLM via the server, parse into a date-versioned pricing table using `table.py`. Implement logic to resolve price for dated (e.g., `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) and undated (e.g., `claude-fable-5`) model IDs using `table.py`'s matching. Add `manual_override` config in TOML to adjust prices using `table.py`'s `apply_overrides`. Compute cost using Anthropic formula: uncached input * input_price + 5m_cache * 1.25x + 1h_cache * 2x + cache_read * 0.1x + output * output_price. Add unit tests for price resolution and cost calculation.
tasks.testStrategy: Test price resolution for dated and undated models with fixtures. Verify cost calculation matches Anthropic formula. Confirm manual override updates pricing table. Run pytest with coverage >= 80%. Validate ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues.

tasks.title: Claude Code JSONL Parser with Keep-Max Dedup
tasks.description: Implement the Claude Code JSONL parser (ClaudeCodeJsonlSource) to parse ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl and subagent transcripts. Extract usage fields and implement keep-max deduplication by requestId.
tasks.details: Created `providers/claude_code.py` implementing `UsageSource`. discover() uses rglobs to find projects/**/*.jsonl including subagents, honoring CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR. parse() performs binary-reads from byte offset, implements keep-max dedup by requestId (largest output_tokens wins per pass), splits cache TTL into 5m/1h (short/long) with legacy fallback, aggregates server_tool_use counters into extra, skips non-assistant/synthetic/no-usage records, counts malformed lines (not silent drop), and leaves incomplete trailing line unconsumed. bootstrap() imports stats-cache.json dailyModelTokens as DailyAggregate. Added DailyAggregate.total_tokens with validate_default derivation and ParseResult.malformed_lines to core models. Registered usage source in providers/anthropic.py. VERIFIED against real local data: 525 transcripts, 12571 dedup events, 0 malformed, dated+undated model ids, 108 bootstrap aggregates. Gates: 67 tests, 95.31% coverage, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Test parsing with fixtures from multiple Claude Code versions. Verify keep-max deduplication by requestId with regression tests. Confirm extraction of all required fields. Run pytest with coverage >= 80% (actual: 95.31%). Validate ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues (actual: clean).

tasks.title: Database Schema and Alembic Migrations
tasks.description: Define the Postgres database schema using SQLAlchemy 2 and Alembic migrations. Create tables: machines, usage_events, limit_snapshots, sessions, daily_rollups, pricing, alert_rules, alert_events, api_tokens.
tasks.details: Server DB layer implemented: `apps/server/tokemetry_server/db/config.py` (pydantic-settings, TOKEMETRY_ prefix, sync_database_url derivation asyncpg->psycopg / aiosqlite->sqlite for Alembic), `db/base.py` (DeclarativeBase with naming convention, JSONType JSONB-on-postgres variant), `db/models.py` (9 SQLAlchemy 2 Mapped models: machines, usage_events PK(provider,event_id), limit_snapshots, sessions, daily_rollups unique grain with '' sentinels, pricing unique(provider,model,date), alert_rules, alert_events FK, api_tokens; BigInteger tokens, Numeric(20,10) money, DateTime tz-aware), `db/session.py` (async engine+session_scope). Alembic configured: `alembic.ini`, `env.py` (sync migrations), hand-authored `0001_initial_schema`, `db/migrate.py` (upgrade/downgrade helpers). Cross-dialect: runs on SQLite for dev/tests, Postgres in prod. Docs: `docs/architecture/database.md`. Gates: 75 tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.

tasks.title: Idempotent Ingest Endpoints with Validation
tasks.description: Implement idempotent ingest endpoints (POST /api/v1/ingest/events, /ingest/limits, /ingest/bootstrap) with batch all-or-nothing logic, upsert keeping maximum-usage row per event_id, and sanity validation rejecting garbage.
tasks.details: The ingest layer is already implemented. Update `apps/server/tokemetry_server/api/ingest.py` to integrate with the existing FastAPI app factory (app.py) where lifespan runs Alembic upgrade and wires engine/session_factory/dialect/cost_fn on app.state. Ensure injectable cost_fn defaults to no-op per task split. Wire models in `api/schemas.py` (EventsIngest/LimitsIngest/BootstrapIngest) with to_core converters, extra=forbid, and batch size bounds. Integrate bearer auth from `api/auth.py` (bootstrap token constant-time + hashed api_tokens lookup refreshing last_used). Use `api/deps.py` for session + ingest service deps. The 3 idempotent routes in `api/ingest.py` should return 400 on validation errors and 422 on schema errors. In `services/ingest.py`, ensure dedup keeps max usage before upsert, includes machine touch, handles events keep-max upsert, limits append, and bootstrap rollup upsert. Use `services/validation.py` for viberank-style sanity checks (token max 10B, clock skew 2h). Implement dialect-aware ON CONFLICT keep-max in `db/upsert.py` for PostgreSQL+SQLite via DialectInsert union. Ensure `security.py` uses SHA-256 token hash + generate. Add daily_rollups.total_tokens+provenance columns (amended migration+ORM). Store cost_usd as NULL pending cost engine. Update docs in `docs/api/ingest.md`. Ensure 91 tests pass, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean, and no warnings.
tasks.testStrategy: Test idempotent ingest with duplicate batches. Verify upsert keeps max usage. Confirm sanity validation rejects garbage. Run pytest with coverage >= 80%. Validate ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues and no warnings.

tasks.details: Cost engine + unknown model handling implemented: `services/cost.py` (CostEngine resolves price by provider/model/date, delegates to PricingStrategy, returns None for unknown model/provider and records the pair + logs), `services/pricing_repo.py` (seed_default_pricing idempotent, load_pricing_table DB->PricingTable, upsert_price_rows, recompute_costs with only_missing), `services/litellm_sync.py` (fetch_litellm_prices via httpx mockable + sync_anthropic_pricing), `providers.py` (build_registry), `db/upsert.py` pricing_upsert. Wired CostEngine.cost as ingest cost_fn in app lifespan (seeds+loads at startup; create_app cost_fn param still overrides for tests). Known models now priced at ingest, unknown stored null. Docs updated docs/architecture/pricing.md. Gates: 104 tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean. Add CLI command `reprice-history` to recompute costs when pricing table changes. Add unit tests for cost calculation and unknown model handling.

tasks.title: Collector Daemon with Offset-Tracked Tailing
tasks.description: Implement the collector daemon (apps/collector) as a cross-platform Python daemon running as Windows Scheduled Task, systemd unit, or launchd agent. Add source-plugin host with watchdog file events and periodic rescan for incremental, byte-offset-tracked tailing.
tasks.details: Implemented collector daemon (Task 8): config.py (TOML via tomllib+pydantic, per-source enable flags, extra=allow for source opts), state.py (SQLite: file_offsets, upload_queue, meta; crash/offline-safe), uploader.py (httpx.Client POST to ingest endpoints, network errors -> False not raised), wire.py (core->wire JSON serializers, machine per-batch), runner.py (Collector.collect_once: tail sources with offset tracking + truncation reset + batch chunking, poll limits with graceful degradation, drain queue oldest-first stopping on failure; run() daemon loop with separate limits cadence + injectable clock; run_bootstrap idempotent via meta flag; dry_run changes nothing), sources.py (provider-agnostic builder registry, filled by Task 9/10), cli.py (--config/--once/--dry-run/--bootstrap, signal-based stop). Runner tested against FakeUsageSource/FakeLimitsSource + httpx MockTransport fake server: offset advance/resume, offline requeue+recovery, truncation reparse, chunking, limits degrade, bootstrap once. Docs: docs/collector/overview.md + deploy/collector.example.toml. Gates: 136 tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Test file event detection and incremental tailing. Verify offset persistence in SQLite. Confirm operational modes work. Run pytest with coverage >= 80%. Validate ruff/mypy strict zero issues. Gates: 136 tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.

tasks.title: AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource Poller with Graceful Degradation
tasks.description: Implement the AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource poller to poll the undocumented endpoint GET https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage with OAuth token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Add graceful degradation to local estimates on failure.
tasks.details: AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource is implemented (collector-side to keep core httpx-free). Reads OAuth token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json using defensive recursive accessToken search. GET /api/oauth/usage with Bearer + `anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20` + Claude-Code UA. Maps five_hour/seven_day/seven_day_opus/seven_day_sonnet windows to normalized LimitSnapshots (provenance=official, resets_at parses ISO or epoch). Skips null windows. Token never leaves machine. All failure modes (missing token, network, non-2xx, bad body) -> LimitsUnavailableError for graceful degradation. Registered `anthropic_oauth` limits builder in sources.py. Tests: token read variations, window mapping, epoch reset, all error->unavailable paths, builder registration. Docs updated. Gates: 148 tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.

Added PriceRowIn + RecomputeResult schemas. tasks.title: Live Feed Throttle + Polling Fallback
tasks.description: Decouple live feed from fetch to eliminate WebSocket-driven refetch storm.
tasks.details: Frontend (NowView): Implemented lib/throttle.ts with trailing throttle (leading + guaranteed trailing, coalesces burst into one call per window; -Infinity init for correct leading edge), unit-tested with fake timers. WS onmessage now appends to feed (keyed by stable incrementing id, not array index) and calls throttledLoad (max one /summary/now refetch per 10s) instead of load() per event, killing per-event refetch storm. Added 30s polling fallback to prevent numbers freezing on idle/dropped socket. Added 'updated Xs ago' indicator (1s tick). All timers cleared on unmount. Gates: 28 dashboard tests, build + eslint/prettier clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Frontend: Verify 'updated Xs ago' indicator, feed append-only behavior (keyed by stable id), and throttledLoad limiting (max one refetch per 10s). tasks.title: Limits Time-Series + Exhaustion Forecast
tasks.description: Implement Limits time-series with reset markers and projected exhaustion.
tasks.details: Frontend (Now page): Use GET /limits/history. Draw utilization sparkline/area inside each GaugeCard with dashed slope-projected exhaustion segment (from prediction.slope) and reset marker. Compute most-at-risk window and promote to headline banner ('You will hit your 5-hour block in ~40m - 87%, climbing 1.4%/min', or 'X at N% - resets in Yh'). Replace one redundant Trends chart with utilization-over-time per window_kind. Test with vitest. Implementation: lib/sparkline.ts sparkGeometry() pure SVG-polyline geometry (clamps to max, optional dashed projection past last point), unit-tested; components/Sparkline.vue inline-SVG sparkline (history polyline + dashed projection + end marker, no chart lib). GaugeCard renders utilization sparkline colored by status with dashed projected-exhaustion segment. NowView fetches /limits/history (24h) per window in parallel and passes to each gauge; computes most-at-risk window and promotes to headline banner colored by status.
tasks.testStrategy: vitest: Test utilization calculation and banner logic. Frontend: Verify sparkline rendering, exhaustion segment, reset marker, and banner display. Ensure quality gates pass (42 dashboard tests, build + eslint/prettier clean).

Events show relative time (timeAgo) + absolute on hover, and expand to reveal body + context JSON. 'Evaluate now' surfaces its {fired} result ('N rule(s) fired' / 'nothing fired'). window_kind selector shown only for window-scoped kinds. Adopted AsyncState. tasks.title: Settings Pricing UI + LiteLLM Sync
tasks.description: Settings pricing management UI plus a LiteLLM price-sync endpoint
tasks.details: Backend: POST /api/v1/pricing/sync-litellm (fetch_litellm_prices via app.state.http_client, sync_anthropic_pricing, effective_date fixed to 2025-01-01 so synced prices cover ALL retained history not just today); stored http_client on app.state; SyncResult schema; monkeypatched test. Frontend client: createPrice/syncLitellm/recomputeCosts + PriceRowInput. SettingsView rewritten: full 5-column price table (input/output/cache read/write 5m/write 1h) + provider + effective_date in an overflow-x scroll, an add/override price form (all fields), an unpriced-model banner (diff usage group_by=model coverage) with click-to-prefill chips, and Sync-from-LiteLLM + Recompute buttons that report events repriced. Fixed modelLabel bedrock/date strip order (strip -v1:0 before -YYYYMMDD). VERIFIED on real data: LiteLLM sync priced 23 models, recomputed 11373 events; cost went from 0.79 (Haiku only) to ~$ equivalent with Opus 4.7 = $ revealed. Gates: 102 server tests + 25 dashboard tests, ruff/mypy/trivy/build/lint clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Backend: pytest with SQLite to verify fetch_litellm_prices, sync_anthropic_pricing, and transactional integrity of SyncResult. Verify effective_date=2025-01-01 covers all retained history. Frontend: test createPrice/syncLitellm/recomputeCosts flow and PriceRowInput rendering. SettingsView: validate 5-column table layout, overflow-x scroll, add/override form, unpriced-model banner with click-to-prefill chips, and Sync-from-LiteLLM + Recompute button event reporting. Verify modelLabel strip order (-v1:0 before -YYYYMMDD). Gates: 102 server tests + 25 dashboard tests, ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues, build/lint clean.

tasks.title: Fix naive datetime serialization across the API
tasks.description: Add a shared UtcDatetime Pydantic type with a field serializer stamping UTC on naive values; apply to every response schema so all datetime fields are offset-aware.
tasks.details: Added shared UtcDatetime annotated type (PlainSerializer, when_used=json) in api/serialization.py. Applied to all response schemas in schemas_query.py (LimitOut, PredictionOut, SummaryNow, BlockOut, SessionOut, MachineOut, token schemas) and schemas_alerts.py (AlertEventOut). Naive DB datetimes now serialize offset-aware so browsers stop parsing them as local time. New test: test_response_datetimes_are_offset_aware. 103 server tests pass; ruff+mypy+trivy clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Run test_response_datetimes_are_offset_aware to verify all datetime fields in response schemas are offset-aware. Validate that naive DB datetimes serialize with UTC offset. Confirm 103 server tests pass and code quality tools (ruff, mypy, trivy) report zero issues.

tasks.title: Limits freshness, reset rollover, weekly absolute time
tasks.description: Attach snapshot age to limits payload; roll a past five_hour resets_at forward in 5h steps (mark derived); GaugeCard shows staleness badge and weekly absolute reset time.
tasks.details: Backend: added analytics.roll_reset_forward(window_kind, resets_at, now) advancing a stale reset in whole periods (5h for five_hour, 7d for weekly) and flagging it derived; LimitOut gains age_seconds + derived_reset; _limit_out(now, roll_reset) computes both, history rows pass roll_reset=False; prediction resets_at also rolled. Frontend: formatDateTime + formatDuration in format.ts; Limit type extended; GaugeCard shows a stale badge (>=600s old) and weekly windows render the absolute reset date/time (countdown as tooltip) while five_hour keeps countdown primary. Tests: 7 rollover unit tests, 4 format tests; 110 server + 51 dashboard pass; all gates clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Backend: Verify analytics.roll_reset_forward advances stale resets in whole periods and flags derived. Confirm LimitOut includes age_seconds and derived_reset. Validate _limit_out computes both correctly and history rows pass roll_reset=False. Check prediction resets_at is rolled. Frontend: Verify formatDateTime and formatDuration work in format.ts. Confirm Limit type extension. Test GaugeCard stale badge (>=600s) and weekly absolute reset date/time with countdown tooltip. Ensure five_hour keeps countdown primary. Run 7 rollover unit tests and 4 format tests. Validate 110 server and 51 dashboard tests pass with clean gates.

tasks.title: Timezone setting
tasks.description: Frontend: useSettings composable persists an IANA timezone ('' = auto/browser via Intl.resolvedOptions), Settings page adds a timezone <select> (Intl.supportedValuesOf) with a live 'now' preview; formatDateTime now renders in activeTimezone() so all absolute displays re-render on change; routed the bare toLocaleString() tooltips in Blocks/Sessions/Alerts through it. Backend: config.timezone (TOKEMETRY_TIMEZONE), AlertEngine(timezone) resolves a ZoneInfo (UTC fallback) and _in_quiet_hours evaluates in it; added tzdata dep for Windows. Tests: quiet-hours + resolve-zone units; 114 server + 51 dashboard pass; gates clean.

tasks.title: Project grouping root-folder heuristic
tasks.description: New tokemetry_core/projects.py project_group(path, roots): strips .claude/worktrees/<name> tails, drops drive letters, folds case/subfolder variants by the segment after a root marker (TOKEMETRY_PROJECT_ROOTS default 'devel'); empty->'(unattributed)'. rollups._aggregate_day now re-groups raw cwd into project groups and sums colliding rows; new rebuild_all_rollups deletes derived rollups and rebuilds full span; POST /api/v1/admin/rebuild-rollups exposes it. list_sessions normalizes project at read; ingest/recompute thread settings.project_root_markers. usage_events.project stays raw. Settings gets a 'Rebuild project groups' button. Tests: 16 core unit cases across all messy forms + 2 integration (fold + rebuild). 195 server/core + 51 dashboard pass; gates clean.
tasks.testStrategy: 16 core unit cases across all messy forms + 2 integration (fold + rebuild). 195 server/core + 51 dashboard pass; gates clean.

tasks.title: Cache framing and plan-value polish
tasks.description: Frontend-only polish. Added pure cacheSavingsUsd(buckets, prices) to lib/coverage.ts summing cache_read x (input_rate - cache_read_rate)/1e6 over priced models (latest rate per model); unit-tested. Breakdowns cache card now shows 'Caching saved ~$X' (from pricing() + byModel) plus reassurance copy that a ~95% cache-read share is normal (Claude Code re-reads cached context each turn, billed at a fraction of input). Now-page Plan value tile labels its window ('last 30d'). 59 dashboard tests pass; build+lint clean.
tasks.details:
1. Implement `cacheSavingsUsd(buckets, prices)` in `lib/coverage.ts`:
   - Sum `cache_read * (input_rate - cache_read_rate) / 1e6` over priced models.
   - Use latest rate per model.
   - Unit-test the function.
2. Update Breakdowns cache card:
   - Display 'Caching saved ~$X' using `pricing()` + `byModel`.
   - Add reassurance copy: '~95% cache-read share is normal' (explain Claude Code re-reads cached context each turn, billed at a fraction of input).
3. Update Now-page Plan value tile:
   - Label its window as 'last 30d'.
4. Validation:
   - Ensure 59 dashboard tests pass.
   - Confirm build+lint are clean.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit-test `cacheSavingsUsd(buckets, prices)` with sample data covering multiple models and rate variations.
2. Verify Breakdowns cache card displays correct 'Caching saved ~$X' value and reassurance copy.
3. Confirm Now-page Plan value tile labels window as 'last 30d'.
4. Run 59 dashboard tests; ensure all pass.
5. Validate build+lint are clean.

tasks.title: Token reconciliation and unattributed components
tasks.description: Components dont sum to total_tokens (uncaptured reasoning tokens?) and bootstrap rows have zero components; capture/reconcile so stacks and table match total, and price models that show null cost despite price rows.
tasks.details: Investigation: derived per-event rollups reconcile EXACTLY (diff=0, fully priced); the entire component-vs-total gap is 108 bootstrap 'stats_cache' rows carrying total_tokens (60M) with zero components and null cost, and the 3 'unpriced-despite-price' models are bootstrap-only (no per-event detail to price) -- so no parser bug. Fix (frontend, honest reconciliation): added an 'other' TOKEN_COMPONENTS entry = max(0, total - sum of 5 tracked) + 'Other' table column, so stacks/table reconcile to total and the 60M unattributed/bootstrap bar renders instead of drawing 0px. Refined pricedCoverage: buckets with zero priceable components are excluded from the actionable unpricedKeys (adding a price cannot resolve bootstrap-only cost) while still counting against coverage ratio. 60 dashboard tests pass; build+lint clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify frontend reconciliation: 1) 'Other' TOKEN_COMPONENTS entry renders correctly as max(0, total - sum of 5 tracked); 2) 'Other' table column displays unattributed tokens; 3) 60M unattributed/bootstrap bar renders instead of 0px; 4) pricedCoverage excludes buckets with zero priceable components from actionable unpricedKeys while counting against coverage ratio; 5) 60 dashboard tests pass; 6) build+lint clean.

tasks.title: Cache-read off by default, selection-aware percent, sticky selection
tasks.description: EChart emits legendselectchanged (chart.on 'legendselectchanged') because notMerge wipes selection each render. stackedTokenBarOption/stackedAreaOption accept a selected map → set legend.selected AND compute the normalized denominator from only visible components, so hiding cache-read re-normalizes the rest to 100%. Views hold a selection ref (default {'cache read': false}), feed it to builders (auto-refresh preserves it), update from the legend event, and persist per-view via loadSelection/saveSelection in useSettings (localStorage). Cache-read is OFF by default on every composition chart (Now model, Trends daily+dimension, Breakdowns 3 stacks). Unit test covers the re-normalize math. 70 dashboard tests pass; build+lint clean.
tasks.details: Implementation: 1) Set cache-read OFF by default on all composition charts. 2) Wire EChart legendselectchanged event to update view selection ref. 3) Pass selected map to stacked builders; builders set legend.selected and compute normalized percent over visible components only. 4) Views persist selection per-view using loadSelection/saveSelection in useSettings (localStorage). 5) Auto-refresh feeds existing selection ref to builders. 6) Unit test validates re-normalize math. Verification: 70 dashboard tests pass; build+lint+format clean.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test: validate re-normalize math (hiding cache-read → rest re-normalizes to 100%). Dashboard: 70 tests pass. Build/lint: clean. Verify: cache-read OFF by default on all composition charts; legend event updates selection; selection persists via localStorage; auto-refresh preserves selection.

tasks.title: Alert channel settings backend (DB-stored, env fallback),
tasks.description: Alert channel settings now DB-editable with env fallback. Migration 0003 adds an app_settings KV table and AppSetting model. services/channel_config.py: resolve_channel_settings merges non-empty channel.* DB values over env Settings (model_copy), coercing port->int/tls->bool. channel_views returns per-channel configured state + masked secrets (last-4). save_channel_config upserts (absent field = unchanged, '' = clear to env). AlertEngine.reconfigure hot-swaps notifiers. app.py resolves channel settings at startup; PUT rebuilds+reconfigutes without restart. API: GET /api/v1/alerts/channels + PUT /channels/{name} beside the test endpoint. Updated secrets-in-env docstrings. Tests (7): precedence, clear-to-env, masking, port coercion, API get/put+hot-swap, unknown-channel 404. 145 server tests; ruff/mypy/trivy clean.
tasks.details:
Implementation complete:
- Migration 0003: app_settings KV table + AppSetting model
- services/channel_config.py: resolve_channel_settings (DB over env, model_copy, port->int/tls->bool coercion)

tasks.title: TOK-1: Provider-Neutral Baseline Audit and V1 Compatibility Contract
tasks.description: Audit current schemas, APIs, storage, dedupe, pricing, collector behavior, and dashboard assumptions; commit docs/architecture/provider-neutral-baseline.md; capture golden v1 wire fixtures that lock current behavior for the entire program. PRD-TOK-002 v1.1 Section 10 and Epic TOK-1.
tasks.details:
Objective: produce the authoritative baseline for the provider-neutral evolution before any schema change (PRD Section 10, Epic TOK-1). No production behavior changes in this epic (Migration Phase 0).

Scope of audit: UsageEvent, DailyAggregate, LimitSnapshot, PriceRow core models in packages/core/src/tokemetry_core/models.py; usage_events composite PK (provider, event_id) and keep-max-output dedupe in apps/server/src/tokemetry_server/db/upsert.py; ingest endpoints /api/v1/ingest/events|limits|bootstrap with batch limits 5000/1000/20000 and validation caps in services/validation.py; rollup grain (day, provider, machine, model, project) in services/rollups.py; collector byte-offset tailing and SQLite upload queue in apps/collector; ProviderRegistry, pricing strategies, and LiteLLM import in packages/core; all Anthropic-specific dashboard code paths (WINDOW_LABELS in format.ts, modelLabel parsing, AlertsView hardcoded window kinds, report recommendations in services/report.py).

Deliverables: docs/architecture/provider-neutral-baseline.md committed; explicit V1 compatibility requirements; database migration constraints (SQLite plus Postgres, Alembic policy, backup strategy, maintenance-window upgrade posture); golden v1 wire fixtures and snapshot tests running in CI for the whole program; mapping of the existing test suite to epics TOK-2 through TOK-12.

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-1): baseline document committed; V1 compatibility requirements explicit; migration constraints documented; existing tests mapped to future epics.

Test strategy: golden fixture snapshot tests must pass against the current server unchanged; the baseline document is reviewed against the live code, not from memory. All migration tasks in Tasks 61 through 71 must reference this baseline.

subtasks.title: Capture V1 compatibility contract and golden wire fixtures
subtasks.description: Freeze current v1 wire behavior as golden fixtures and snapshot tests that must pass for the whole program.
subtasks.details:
Create a compatibility test layer that locks v1 behavior before any schema work: golden JSON fixtures for representative v1 ingest payloads (events with duplicates exercising keep-max, limits snapshots for all four Anthropic windows, bootstrap aggregates) and for the key v1 query responses (summary/now shape, usage grouped by each dimension, sessions list and detail, limits history, blocks). Store fixtures under apps/server/tests/fixtures/v1_golden/ and add snapshot tests (apps/server/tests/integration/test_v1_golden.py) that ingest the fixtures into a fresh database and assert byte-stable normalized responses (volatile fields like timestamps and request ids masked deterministically). These tests become the executable form of AC-001 and run unchanged through every later epic; any diff is a compatibility break. Document the contract prose in docs/architecture/provider-neutral-baseline.md: exact v1 wire schemas, status codes, dedupe outcome semantics (accepted plus duplicates_merged), and the rule that v1 endpoints stay wire-identical until a formal deprecation policy exists (Migration Phase 7). Test strategy: run the new golden suite twice against the current master to prove determinism; mutate one response field intentionally to prove the suite fails loudly; then revert.

tasks.title: TOK-2: Provider and Model Registries
tasks.description: Introduce canonical provider registry with alias normalization, native model registry with lifecycle states and alias rules, unknown provider and model handling with data quality records, and the /api/v2/providers and /api/v2/models endpoints. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.1, 12.2; Epic TOK-2; FR-PROVIDER-001..010, FR-MODEL-001..010.
tasks.details:
Objective: canonical registries so every later epic resolves providers and models centrally (Migration Phase 1; no v1 behavior change).

Design: providers table (id lowercase stable, display_name, aliases JSON, pricing_strategy, limit_semantics, supported_dimensions, registered flag); models table with PK (provider, native_model_id), lifecycle active/deprecated/retired/unknown, capabilities JSON, first_seen/last_seen; model_aliases table (provider, alias) unique mapping to native_model_id with rule_version. Alias normalization is centralized in packages/core (z.ai and zai and legacy names normalize to zai; existing Claude Code provider id anthropic remains valid unchanged per FR-PROVIDER-009). Native model IDs are always retained exactly as reported (FR-MODEL-001); pricing lookups use provider plus native model plus effective date (FR-MODEL-003). Unknown providers may be ingested when policy permits but are marked unregistered and produce data_quality_events records (FR-PROVIDER-005, FR-MODEL-006). Provider registration must not require dashboard code changes (FR-PROVIDER-007). Seed anthropic, openai, zai (FR-PROVIDER-008) plus the fake test provider.

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-2): Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai registered; aliases normalize centrally; model IDs remain native and provider-scoped; unknown provider and model data quality records generated; existing Claude data remains queryable.

Test strategy: unit tests for alias normalization and model lookup; migration upgrade and downgrade tests on SQLite and Postgres; regression test proving existing Claude usage remains queryable through v1 endpoints; fixtures for all three providers (NFR-MAIN-007). Reference docs/architecture/provider-neutral-baseline.md.

subtasks.title: Core provider descriptors and alias normalization
subtasks.description: Extend packages/core with provider descriptors and central alias normalization.
subtasks.details:
In packages/core/src/tokemetry_core, add a ProviderDescriptor model (id, display_name, aliases, pricing_strategy id, limit_semantics, supported usage dimensions) and a normalization function that maps any alias to the canonical lowercase provider id (FR-PROVIDER-002/003): z.ai, zai, z-ai to zai; anthropic and claude legacy spellings to anthropic; openai and codex client names to openai. Extend the existing ProviderRegistry (registry.py) so descriptors are registered alongside usage/limits/pricing factories without breaking current register() call sites (anthropic.py, fake.py). Normalization is pure, case-insensitive, and versioned (FR-MODEL-009 pattern applied to providers); unknown ids pass through marked unregistered rather than raising, since ingest policy decides acceptance (FR-PROVIDER-005). Keep mypy strict and frozen pydantic conventions of models.py. Update docs/architecture/core-package.md. subtasks.title: data_quality_events table and recording service
subtasks.description: Create the data quality ledger that records unknown providers, unknown models, schema drift, conflicts, and unpriced usage.
subtasks.details:
Add data_quality_events table (id autoincrement, kind, subject, detail JSONType, source_id nullable, ts, resolved bool default false) with indexes on kind and ts, via Alembic migration. Kinds initially: unknown_provider, unknown_model, schema_drift, sequence_conflict, unpriced_usage, limit_source_failure, clock_skew. Add services/data_quality.py with a record() helper that deduplicates bursts (one open record per kind plus subject within a configurable window rather than one per event) and a query service for the future /api/v2/data-quality endpoint and alerts. Recording is fire-and-forget from ingest paths: a data quality failure must never reject otherwise valid ingest. This subtask provides the sink used by FR-MODEL-006, FR-IDEMP-008, FR-PRICE-022, FR-LIMIT-013, and Epic TOK-9. Test strategy: unit tests for dedup-window behavior, kind coverage, and resolution flagging; integration test that recording from inside an ingest transaction does not roll back accepted events (NFR-REL-008 pattern); migration tests both engines.
<info added on 2026-07-18T15:44:39.190Z>

Config: data_quality_dedup_window_seconds default 3600. Wired into IngestService (optional injected DataQualityService): records unknown_provider when resolve marks registered=False, unknown_model when observe.newly_observed (completes the deferred FR-MODEL-006 hook from 61.3), both via record_safe. deps.py injects it with the window from settings. tz: _as_utc coercion for window comparison (SQLite drops tzinfo). Tests (test_data_quality.py, 11): record/kinds/unknown-kind-rejected, dedup within/beyond window + distinct subjects, resolution flagging + reopen-after-resolve, open_events filter, record_safe swallow + persist, and HTTP ingest of a novel model records unknown_model while keeping the accepted event. subtasks.title: Backfill registries from historical usage data
subtasks.description: Populate models and data quality records from existing usage_events history.
subtasks.details:
Write an idempotent backfill routine (invoked once at startup after migration, guarded by an app_settings marker like registry_backfill_done) that scans distinct (provider, model) pairs from the existing usage_events table plus distinct providers from limit_snapshots, inserts registry rows (known Claude model families get lifecycle active; anything unrecognized gets lifecycle unknown plus an unknown_model data quality record), and populates first_seen/last_seen from min/max event timestamps per model. Never mutates usage rows (FR-MODEL-007). Provide a CLI or admin trigger for re-running with --force for recovery. Test strategy: unit test with a seeded history containing dated Claude ids, an unknown id, and a synthetic provider, asserting registry rows, lifecycle assignment, timestamps, and data quality records; idempotency test (second run is a no-op); guard-marker test.
<info added on 2026-07-18T16:05:28.417Z>
Added services/registry_backfill.py: RegistryBackfill.run(force=False) is idempotent, guarded by app_settings marker registry_backfill_done. Scans distinct providers from usage_events + limit_snapshots (registers each via ProviderRegistryService.resolve accept - seeds registered, unknowns registered=False) and distinct (provider,model) with func.min/max(ts) grouped from usage_events (ReturnTypeFromArgs propagates DateTime so aggregates parse back). Known Claude families (provider anthropic + id startswith claude, dated+undated) -> lifecycle active; else unknown + unknown_model DQ record via record_safe (source=backfill in detail). first_seen/last_seen from min/max ts. Model upsert is get-or-create: on existing extends window (min first_seen/max last_seen tz-robust) and upgrades unknown->active, never downgrades or mutates usage rows (FR-MODEL-007). Wired into app.py startup after provider seeding (guarded, no-op on empty/already-done DB). CLI: cli.py + __main__.py -> python -m tokemetry_server backfill-registries [--force]; migrates then runs; smoke-tested (first run migrates+backfills, --force re-runs, --help ok). Tests (test_registry_backfill.py, 8): seeded history (dated+undated claude, mystery id, synthetic acme provider via limit_snapshot) asserts lifecycle/timestamps/providers/DQ, never-mutates-usage, second-run-skipped, marker-set, force-reruns-without-duplicating (DQ dedup collapses), startup-marker via client. tasks.title: TOK-3: Usage Event v2 Ledger and Ingest API v2
tasks.description: Add the v2 attempt-event lifecycle (finality, sequence, revisions, corrections), the new usage_events_v2 ledger with v1 backfill and compatibility view, logical_requests, privacy validation, and the /api/v2/ingest endpoints while keeping v1 endpoints wire-identical. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 11, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5; Epic TOK-3; decision D-001.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral event lifecycle and ingest (Migration Phase 2, v2 behind the api surface only; existing collectors stay on v1).

Design decisions: new usage_events_v2 table rather than in-place evolution (D-001); usage_event_revisions for superseded and conflicting states and corrections; logical_requests dedicated table (D-003); event identity (provider, event_id) with event_kind attempt|logical_request|import|adjustment, finality snapshot|final, increasing sequence; final supersedes snapshot; final-to-final requires admin correction (D-002); same-sequence conflicts rejected to data_quality_events (FR-IDEMP-008); replay is a no-op (FR-IDEMP-007); batches transactional (FR-IDEMP-009). V1 ingest maps into v2 rows with event_kind attempt and finality final, applying legacy keep-max-output as documented compatibility conflict mode (FR-IDEMP-012, FR-EVENT-023). After verified backfill the physical usage_events table is replaced by a read-only v1-shaped compatibility view. Privacy: content fields rejected (FR-EVENT-021/022, FR-PRIV-001/002), dimensions allowlist with bounds 16 keys, 64-char keys, 256-char values (D-004), tool-name histogram accepted only behind server-side opt-in and default off (D-005), maximum event size and JSON depth enforced (FR-EVENT-028, NFR-SEC-004).

Endpoints: POST /api/v2/ingest/events, /api/v2/ingest/limits, /api/v2/ingest/aggregates, /api/v2/ingest/validate (schema plus semantic checks without persistence, FR-INGEST-007), GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event. Structured validation errors with event index, field path, code, message (FR-INGEST-006); server batch IDs and ingest_batches table (FR-INGEST-008); request IDs on every response (FR-INGEST-016); gzip support (FR-INGEST-010); OpenAPI for all v2 schemas (FR-INGEST-012).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-3): v2 schema and OpenAPI published; attempts, logical request IDs, finality, sequence, outcomes, source, routing, and reasoning supported; v1 events map into internal v2 representation; v1 endpoints retain behavior; validation endpoint exists; batch tests cover duplicates, snapshots, finality, and conflicts.

Test strategy: unit tests for v1-to-v2 conversion, validation, snapshot and finality resolution, conflict and correction behavior; integration tests for duplicate batches, snapshot followed by final, conflicting same-sequence events; golden v1 fixtures from Task 60 must pass unchanged; migration tests on SQLite and Postgres including the backfill count and sum verification; performance tests for 100-event batch p95 under 200 ms and a 5000-event compatibility batch.

subtasks.title: Core v2 wire model and published JSON schema
subtasks.description: Define the complete v2 usage event wire model in packages/core with a machine-readable JSON schema.
subtasks.details:
Create packages/core/src/tokemetry_core/models_v2.py (or usage_v2.py) with frozen extra=forbid pydantic models implementing the full PRD 12.3 wire shape: schema_version literal 2 (FR-EVENT-001); event_id; event_kind attempt|logical_request|import|adjustment (FR-EVENT-003); finality snapshot|final (FR-EVENT-005); sequence int >= 0 (FR-EVENT-006); logical_request_id, attempt_id, provider_request_id, provider_response_id (FR-EVENT-009..011); provider; requested_model, routed_model, native_model as separate fields (FR-EVENT-012); ts_started, ts_first_token, ts_completed all timezone-aware (FR-EVENT-013, reuse the tz validator pattern from models.py); machine, project, session_id, agent_id, environment; six non-negative token counters input, output, cache_read, cache_write_short, cache_write_long, reasoning (FR-EVENT-014/015); success bool plus outcome string kept separate (FR-EVENT-017); http_status, stop_reason, service_tier, streaming, latency_ms, time_to_first_token_ms, tool_call_count, optional tool histogram dict; provenance official|local_estimate|stats_cache|imported|adjusted (FR-EVENT-025, extending the existing Provenance enum); source object with type, name, version, optional instance_id (FR-EVENT-018); optional gateway-neutral routing object (FR-EVENT-019); bounded dimensions dict (FR-EVENT-020); namespaced extra dict (FR-PROVIDER-006); trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id (FR-OTEL-001). Export a JSON schema document for GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event. No content fields exist in the schema at all (FR-EVENT-021). Test strategy: unit tests for every field bound, tz enforcement, non-negative counters, kind and finality literals, zero-token failed attempts accepted (FR-EVENT-024), and JSON schema generation stability (snapshot).
<info added on 2026-07-18T20:21:37.479Z>
Implemented v2 wire model in `packages/core/src/tokemetry_core/usage_v2.py`: `UsageEventV2` (frozen, `extra=forbid`) plus `EventKind`/`Finality`/`SourceType` StrEnums, `SourceRef` and `Routing` nested models, and `usage_event_json_schema()` returning the JSON Schema 2020-12 document generated from the model. Extended the existing `Provenance` enum in `models.py` with `IMPORTED` and `ADJUSTED` (FR-EVENT-025). Key decisions: source is required per D-011 (every v2 event carries source identity); `native_model` required to match v1 and the PRD example; `success` defaults `False` and all token counters optional so zero-token failed/cancelled attempts are ingestible (FR-EVENT-024); dimension bounds and prohibited-key rejection deferred to 62.2 (this subtask is wire shape + invariants only); reused the v1 `_require_tz` validator via import to avoid duplication. Schema stability locked by a unit test pinning the property and required-field sets and asserting no content-like key exists (FR-EVENT-021). 36 unit tests in `test_usage_v2.py`. Doc: `docs/architecture/usage-event-v2.md`. Gates: pytest 427 passed/11 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (83 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T20:21:37.479Z>

subtasks.title: Privacy validation layer with prohibited-key fuzzing
subtasks.description: Server-side strict privacy enforcement for all v2 metadata.
subtasks.details:
Add services/privacy.py implementing FR-EVENT-022 and FR-PRIV-001/002: a prohibited-key detector applied recursively to extra, dimensions, routing, and the tool histogram, rejecting or stripping (strict server policy setting privacy_mode reject|strip, default reject) keys that look like content (prompt, response, message, content, text, input_text, output_text, arguments, file_path, path, code, snippet, reasoning_content and variants); dimension allowlist enforcement per decision D-004 (configurable, defaults team, cost_center, environment; bounds 16 keys, key length 64, value length 256 per FR-EVENT-020); tool-name histogram gate per D-005 (server setting tool_names_enabled default false; when disabled any histogram is rejected; when enabled entries bounded to 32 names of 64 chars with integer counts); extra namespace bounding (top-level keys must be provider ids or gateway, size-capped); maximum serialized event size (default 32 KiB, configurable per FR-EVENT-028) and JSON depth limit (NFR-SEC-004). Violations produce structured field-path errors for the ingest error format. Test strategy: unit tests per rule; property-based prohibited-key fuzz tests (hypothesis) generating nested payloads with forbidden keys at random depths asserting rejection (FR-PRIV-012, AC-011, AC-022); performance sanity that validation of a 100-event batch stays well under the ingest budget.
<info added on 2026-07-18T20:37:35.436Z>
Implemented privacy validation layer in `apps/server/src/tokemetry_server/services/privacy.py`: added `PrivacyValidator` and `PrivacyPolicy` (frozen dataclass with defaults matching decisions D-004/D-005) returning `PrivacyResult` containing the possibly-cleaned event, a tuple of fatal `ValidationIssue` records (with `field_path`, `code`, `message` per FR-INGEST-006), and stripped paths. Controls (one per open container): recursive content-key scan on `extra` only (rejects/strips keys whose alphanumeric-lowercased form contains `prompt`, `response`, `message`, `content`, `text`, `arguments`, `path`, `code`, `snippet`, `completion`, `body`) plus top-level namespace bounding to `provider` and `gateway`; dimension allowlist enforcement (`team`, `cost_center`, `environment`) with 16-key/64-char-key/256-char-value bounds; `tool_histogram` gated by `tool_names_enabled` (default `false`) with 32-name/64-char/non-negative integer bounds; whole-event max serialized size 32 KiB and JSON depth limit 8. Design decisions documented: content scan targets `extra` only (the sole free-form nested container); `dimensions` covered by allowlist, `routing` by fixed schema, `tool names` by the D-005 gate (scanning tool names for content tokens would defeat the gate). Default `mode=reject`; `mode=strip` removes content keys and rebuilds via `model_copy`. Settings-to-policy wiring deferred to subtask 62.6. Tests added in `apps/server/tests/unit/test_privacy.py`: 21 tests including a seeded `stdlib-random` fuzz (300 iterations injecting forbidden keys at random depths; `hypothesis` not installed, used deterministic seeded generation) and a 100-event performance sanity check. Fixed mypy issue by casting the recursive scan result to `dict[str, Any]`. Gates passed: full `pytest` suite, `ruff` clean, `mypy` strict clean, `trivy` no HIGH/CRITICAL issues.
</info added on 2026-07-18T20:37:35.436Z>

subtasks.title: Ledger tables migration: usage_events_v2, usage_event_revisions, logical_requests
subtasks.description: Create the three lifecycle tables with indexes on both engines.
subtasks.details:
Alembic migration creating: usage_events_v2 with composite PK (provider, event_id) and all wire fields flattened per the design spec section 3.1 (six BigInteger token counters, Numeric latencies as integers ms, JSONType for routing, dimensions, extra, tool_histogram; schema_version int; source_id FK-less integer reference until Task 63 lands, then FK; trace/span/parent ids as indexed strings); usage_event_revisions (id autoincrement, provider, event_id, sequence, finality, payload JSONType snapshot of the superseded state, reason superseded|conflict|correction, actor, ts) with index on (provider, event_id); logical_requests with PK (provider, logical_request_id), requested_model, session_id, routing_policy, routing_reason, attempt_count, fallback_count, winning_attempt_id, ts_first, ts_last. Indexes on usage_events_v2: ts_started, machine, session_id, native_model, logical_request_id, source_id, provider_request_id (FR-EVENT-011), outcome. Follow db/base.py conventions; keep the existing usage_events table untouched in this migration (the swap happens in subtask 10). Test strategy: upgrade and downgrade tests on SQLite and Postgres; index existence assertions; insert round-trip for every column including JSON payloads on both engines.
<info added on 2026-07-18T20:51:01.225Z>
Added migration 0006 (down_revision 0005) creating `usage_events_v2`, `usage_event_revisions`, and `logical_requests` tables with matching ORM models in `db/models.py`. `usage_events_v2` uses a composite primary key `(provider, event_id)` and flattens all v2 wire fields: `schema_version` (int), `event_kind`/`finality` (strings), `sequence` (int), six `BigInteger` token counters plus `reasoning_tokens`, `success` (bool), `outcome`, latencies as `Integer` ms, `tool_histogram`/`routing`/`dimensions`/`extra` as `JSONType`, `provenance`, `source_id` as plain `Integer` (FK deferred to Task 63), and `trace_id`/`span_id`/`parent_span_id`. It includes 11 single-column indexes named `ix_usage_events_v2_<col>` for `ts_started`, `machine`, `session_id`, `native_model`, `logical_request_id`, `provider_request_id`, `outcome`, `source_id`, `trace_id`, `span_id`, and `parent_span_id`. `usage_event_revisions` has `id` as PK, columns `(provider, event_id, sequence, finality, payload JSON, reason, actor, ts)`, and a composite index `ix_usage_event_revisions_provider_event`. `logical_requests` has PK `(provider, logical_request_id)` with fields for `routing_policy`, `attempt_count`, `fallback_count`, `winning_attempt_id`, `ts_first`, and `ts_last`. The physical `usage_events` table remains untouched (view swap occurs in subtask 62.10). Tests include `test_usage_events_v2_tables.py` covering full-column round-trips (including JSON), nullability checks, composite-PK duplicate rejection, same-id-different-provider cases, index existence assertions, revisions round-trip+index, and logical_requests round-trip+PK; all tests are engine-parametrized via `migrated_engine`. The three tables were added to `test_migrations.py` `_EXPECTED_TABLES` to ensure upgrade/downgrade and ORM-drift coverage. Postgres tests run in CI (skipped locally due to missing `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL` and Docker), while SQLite tests pass green, including `test_migration_matches_orm_metadata` confirming ORM matches migration columns. Documentation updated in `docs/architecture/database.md`. Quality gates: 455 pytest passed/18 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (85 files), and trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL issues.
</info added on 2026-07-18T20:51:01.225Z>

subtasks.title: Revision engine: finality, sequence, conflicts, corrections
subtasks.description: Implement the event lifecycle resolution rules that decide whether an incoming event inserts, supersedes, duplicates, conflicts, or corrects.
subtasks.details:
Add services/revisions.py implementing the PRD 12.4 state machine for (provider, event_id): new id inserts; snapshot over snapshot with higher sequence supersedes (previous state archived to usage_event_revisions with reason superseded, FR-IDEMP-002/003); final supersedes any snapshot (FR-IDEMP-004); identical replay is a byte-equal no-op returning duplicate (FR-IDEMP-007); same sequence with differing payload rejects and records a sequence_conflict data quality event (FR-IDEMP-008); later snapshot never supersedes a final (FR-EVENT-008); final over final requires the correction flag plus admin:corrections scope and archives the previous state with reason correction, actor, timestamp, and reason text (FR-IDEMP-005/006, FR-EVENT-026, decision D-002). Legacy keep-max compatibility mode: events entering through the v1 mapper resolve conflicts by output_tokens >= comparison exactly as db/upsert.py does today (FR-IDEMP-012), implemented as an explicit conflict-mode enum so the behavior is documented and testable rather than accidental. Returns a per-event outcome (accepted, updated, duplicate, rejected, corrected) consumed by the ingest service. Test strategy: exhaustive unit matrix over (existing state x incoming kind/finality/sequence) including replay determinism; correction audit content assertions; keep-max mode equivalence tests against fixtures derived from the current upsert behavior.
<info added on 2026-07-18T21:07:04.599Z>
Added services/revisions.py implementing the PRD 12.4 state machine. Pure resolve(incoming, existing, mode, correction) -> RevisionDecision (outcome, write, archive_reason, conflict) so the full existing-x-incoming matrix is unit-tested without a DB. Outcomes accepted/updated/duplicate/rejected/corrected. Rules: new id -> accepted; identical fingerprint -> duplicate (FR-IDEMP-007); over snapshot: final or higher-sequence snapshot supersedes (archive superseded), same-sequence differing -> rejected+sequence_conflict DQ (FR-IDEMP-008), older snapshot -> duplicate; over final: later snapshot ignored -> duplicate (FR-EVENT-008), final-over-final without correction -> rejected+DQ (FR-IDEMP-005), with correction -> corrected (archive correction with actor+reason_text, FR-IDEMP-006/D-002). ConflictMode.KEEP_MAX reproduces v1 keep-max exactly (archives nothing, FR-IDEMP-012). Shared usage_event_v2_row() projection (reused by 62.5 ingest and 62.9 v1 mapper) normalizes timestamps to UTC; row_fingerprint() is canonical key-sorted JSON with UTC-normalized datetimes so tz-aware incoming and SQLite naive readback compare equal. RevisionEngine.apply() reads active row, resolves, archives prior state to usage_event_revisions (payload envelopes previous row JSON-safe + reason_text), writes new active state, records sequence_conflict via injected DataQualityService; admin:corrections scope enforced upstream at API. Tests: unit test_revisions.py (resolve matrix, projection column-completeness, UTC normalization, fingerprint order/tz invariance) + integration test_revision_engine.py (accept, replay duplicate, snapshot supersede+archive, final supersede, same-seq conflict+DQ, snapshot-after-final ignored, final-over-final rejected+DQ, correction audited, keep-max equivalence). 29 new tests. Doc: usage-event-v2.md revision-engine section. Gates: pytest 484 passed/18 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (86 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T21:07:04.599Z>

subtasks.title: Ingest service v2 with transactional batches and ingest_batches ledger
subtasks.description: Batch orchestration, per-event outcome counts, batch IDs, request IDs.
subtasks.details:
Add services/ingest_v2.py: accepts a validated batch, runs privacy validation (subtask 2) then the revision engine (subtask 4) per event inside one transaction (FR-IDEMP-009, NFR-REL-001); on any structural failure the batch rolls back atomically; response carries a server-generated batch id (FR-INGEST-008), counts for accepted, updated, duplicate, rejected, corrected (FR-IDEMP-011), and optionally the accepted and updated event ids when the client requests them subject to response-size limits (FR-INGEST-009). Create ingest_batches table via migration (batch_id, source identity, token label, schema_version, counts, received_at, request_id) retained 30 days per retention defaults. Add request-id middleware stamping every API response (FR-INGEST-016) if not present. WebSocket publication after commit reuses the existing broadcaster and must never roll back accepted ingest (NFR-REL-008). Ingest logs never contain bearer tokens or event metadata values (FR-INGEST-017). Test strategy: integration tests for transactional all-or-nothing behavior, count correctness across mixed batches (new, replay, snapshot upgrade, conflict), batch ledger rows, and WS publish failure isolation; unit tests for response-size limiting of returned id lists.
<info added on 2026-07-18T21:20:10.222Z>
Added services/ingest_v2.py (IngestV2Service): validated batch → privacy validation over every event (reject-mode violation is structural → raise BatchValidationError with per-event BatchIssue index/field_path/code/message, FR-INGEST-006, nothing persisted; strip-mode uses cleaned events) → revision engine per event within the caller's transaction (FR-IDEMP-009), accumulating five outcome counts (accepted/updated/duplicate/rejected/corrected, FR-IDEMP-011). Per-event sequence_conflict counts as rejected + recorded via DataQualityService but does NOT fail the batch; only structural failure or DB error rolls back. Writes one ingest_batches row per batch with server-generated uuid4 batch_id (FR-INGEST-008), source_id, token_label, counts, schema_version=2, received_at, request_id. Optional return_ids echoes accepted/updated ids capped to max_returned_ids (default 1000) with ids_truncated flag (FR-INGEST-009). Service is transport-agnostic and never commits (route owns txn + post-commit WS publish, deferred to 62.6). Added migration 0007 + IngestBatch ORM (batch_id PK, index received_at); added to test_migrations _EXPECTED_TABLES. Added request-id middleware in app.py stamping X-Request-ID on every response, honoring client-supplied (FR-INGEST-016). Tests: test_ingest_v2.py (new batch + ledger row, mixed-batch counts incl in-batch replay/snapshot-upgrade, conflict rejected-but-commits + DQ, privacy failure atomic all-or-nothing, return_ids optional, id capping, request-id header present + echoed). Postgres migration path in CI. Docs: database.md, usage-event-v2.md. Gates: pytest 492 passed/18 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (88 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T21:20:10.222Z>

subtasks.title: Ingest endpoints: POST /api/v2/ingest/events and /api/v2/ingest/validate
subtasks.description: The v2 ingest HTTP surface with structured errors, gzip, and configurable limits.
subtasks.details:
Add api/v2/ingest.py: POST /api/v2/ingest/events accepting a batch envelope (schema_version, source object, events list) with configurable maximum event count and byte size (FR-INGEST-005, defaults 1000 events and 5 MiB, settings-driven); gzip request body support (FR-INGEST-010); bearer auth (FR-INGEST-001) using require_token now and ingest:events scope once Task 63 lands; validation failures return the structured error list with event index, field path, code, and message (FR-INGEST-006) and reject the batch. POST /api/v2/ingest/validate runs schema plus semantic plus privacy checks and returns the same error structure without persisting anything (FR-INGEST-007, FR-IDEMP-010) so exporters can pre-flight batches. Health and readiness endpoints stay unauthenticated and readiness reports database plus migration status without secrets (FR-INGEST-018/019). Rate limiting distinguishes ingest from query traffic (FR-INGEST-015) via a simple in-process token bucket configurable per class, hardened further in Task 70. Test strategy: integration tests for happy path, oversized batch, malformed event index reporting, gzip round trip, validate-endpoint non-persistence (row counts unchanged), unauthenticated health, and rate-limit classes; error-format snapshot test used later by client generators.
<info added on 2026-07-18T21:39:17.723Z>
Added api/v2/ingest.py with POST /api/v2/ingest/events, POST /api/v2/ingest/validate, and GET /api/v2/ready. The events endpoint reads the raw body (supporting gzip via Content-Encoding, FR-INGEST-010), enforces ingest_max_bytes (returns 413) and ingest_max_events (returns 413, FR-INGEST-005, settings-driven defaults 1000 events/5 MiB), validates the envelope {schema_version, events, return_ids, correction}, then validates each event as UsageEventV2 while collecting structured errors {index, field_path, code, message} (FR-INGEST-006); on failure it returns 422 with detail {errors, request_id} and rejects the batch. Successful ingestion calls IngestV2Service in a single transaction with an explicit commit before a best-effort WebSocket publish (publish errors are swallowed so they cannot roll back the ingest, NFR-REL-008) and returns batch_id, counts, and optional capped IDs. The validate endpoint performs the same schema and privacy checks but returns 200 {valid, errors, request_id} without persisting anything (FR-INGEST-007, FR-IDEMP-010). The readiness endpoint is unauthenticated, reports database connectivity and Alembic migration revision without secrets (FR-INGEST-018/019), and returns 503 when the database is down. Source remains per-event (wire-canonical per PRD 12.3/62.1) rather than a batch-envelope field; token_label is derived from auth; source_id resolution is deferred to Task 63; correction is allowed now (bootstrap=full-admin) but gated by admin:corrections scope in Task 63. Request-ID middleware (from 62.5) stamps X-Request-ID. A new services/rate_limit.py implements a token-bucket RateLimiter with separate ingest and query buckets on app.state (FR-INGEST-015), enforced via the enforce_ingest_rate dependency returning 429. Configuration includes ingest_max_events/bytes, privacy_* knobs, a dimension allowlist (CSV/set property), and ingest/query rate capacity and rate. Response schemas (ValidationErrorItem, IngestEventsResponse, ValidateResponse) are in api/v2/schemas.py. Non-deprecated Starlette status constants are used (422 UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT, 413 CONTENT_TOO_LARGE). Tests include unit test_rate_limit.py (bucket capacity/refill/cap/key-isolation) and integration test_ingest_v2_api.py (happy path, return_ids, malformed-index reporting, privacy content_key, gzip round trip, validate non-persistence, validate reports-without-raising, unauthenticated readiness=0007, oversized 413, ingest-vs-query rate-limit separation). Documentation is in docs/api/ingest.md v2 section. Gates: pytest 506 passed/18 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (90 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T21:39:17.723Z>

subtasks.title: V2 limits and aggregates ingest endpoints
subtasks.description: POST /api/v2/ingest/limits and /api/v2/ingest/aggregates with source identity.
subtasks.details:
Add POST /api/v2/ingest/limits accepting provider-neutral limit snapshots carrying the v2 dimensions (provider, window_kind opaque, account, organization, machine, source identity, utilization, remaining, limit_amount, unit, resets_at, provenance official|estimated per FR-LIMIT-002..004) and POST /api/v2/ingest/aggregates for historical importer bootstraps (daily aggregate rows with provenance imported, mapping onto the rollup upsert path the way v1 bootstrap does today). The limit_snapshots dimension columns themselves are added in Task 69 subtask 2; until that lands the endpoint validates the wire shape and stores what the current table supports, feature-flagging the extended dimensions (document the flag in the task log). subtasks.title: V1-to-v2 backfill migration with count and sum verification
subtasks.description: Copy all historical v1 events into the v2 ledger and prove equality before any cutover.
subtasks.details:
Write the backfill as an Alembic data migration plus a standalone verification tool. Backfill: every usage_events row becomes a usage_events_v2 row with event_kind attempt, finality final, sequence 0, provenance preserved, native_model from the v1 model column, requested_model and routed_model null, source_id null (attributed later by Task 63 subtask 6), all six token counters copied (reasoning 0), extra JSON preserved, machine, project, session_id, ts columns mapped onto ts_started (v1 has one timestamp; ts_completed set equal, latency null) per FR-EVENT-023. Batched copy (10000 rows per chunk) so SQLite and Postgres both survive production-scale tables; resumable via high-water mark; never mutates source rows. Verification tool (CLI, also runnable via admin endpoint): per day, provider, and machine compare row counts and sums of all five v1 token columns plus cost between usage_events and usage_events_v2, emitting a machine-readable report; any mismatch blocks subtasks 9 and 10. Downgrade path drops v2 rows originating from backfill (marked via provenance-preserving batch tag in extra or a backfill marker column). Test strategy: migration tests seeding representative v1 data (including duplicates already collapsed, null projects, unknown-model rows with null cost) then asserting the verification report is all-equal; interrupted-backfill resume test; downgrade test restoring the pre-backfill state.
<info added on 2026-07-18T22:08:58.278Z>
Added db/backfill.py + migration 0008 (data migration, down_revision 0007) + CLI commands. backfill_usage_events_v2(connection, chunk_size=10000): chunked, keyset-paginated by (provider,event_id), idempotent via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (resumable, never mutates source). Maps each v1 usage_events row -> usage_events_v2 with event_kind=attempt, finality=final, sequence=0, native_model=v1.model, requested/routed null, source_id null (Task 63), five token counters copied + reasoning_tokens=0, single v1 ts -> ts_started AND ts_completed, success=True (FR-EVENT-023). V1-only columns with no v2 home (git_branch, client_version, entrypoint, is_sidechain, session_kind, speed, source, cost_usd) preserved in extra['_v1'] so the 62.10 compatibility view reproduces them; backfilled rows carry extra['_backfill']=True marker. remove_backfilled_rows: downgrade deletes only marked rows (keyset-scanned, natively-ingested v2 rows survive). verify_backfill: dialect-agnostic Python aggregation (no date/JSON SQL functions) grouping by day/provider/machine, compares row count + five token sums + cost (v1 cost from cost_usd, v2 cost from extra['_v1']['cost_usd']); returns machine-readable BackfillReport (ok/groups_checked/mismatches); any mismatch blocks subtasks 9/10. CLI: python -m tokemetry_server backfill-usage-events and verify-backfill (verify exits 1 on mismatch, prints JSON report). mypy fixes: _after() keyset helper wrapping literals in sa.literal, sa.delete on ORM class, stmt: Any in _insert_ignore. Tests: test_backfill_v2.py (column mapping incl _v1 namespace + cost precision + marker, verify all-equal over representative data incl null project + unpriced unknown model, idempotent+resumable with chunk_size=1 no dups, downgrade removes only backfilled preserving native row, verify detects a corrupted-token mismatch), engine-parametrized (Postgres in CI). Updated the 62.6 readiness test to assert migration present (robust vs future migrations). Docs: database.md backfill section. Gates: pytest 528 passed/18 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (93 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T22:08:58.278Z>

subtasks.title: Repoint v1 ingest and server services to the v2 ledger
subtasks.description: All reads and writes go through usage_events_v2; v1 endpoints stay wire-identical.
subtasks.details:
Switch the server to the v2 ledger: the v1 ingest path (api/ingest.py, services/ingest.py) maps EventIn to a v2 event (event_kind attempt, finality final, keep-max compatibility conflict mode) and writes through the revision engine; queries in services/queries.py, analytics.py, insights.py, session_stats.py, report.py, and rollups.py read from usage_events_v2; cost stamping moves to the computed_costs flow only when Task 64 lands, so in this subtask the existing cost_usd semantics are preserved by writing cost into the v2 row-compatible location the view exposes (transitional column, removed in Task 64 subtask 4 cutover; document in task log). The golden v1 fixture suite from 60.4 is the acceptance gate: every v1 ingest and query response must be byte-identical. In-batch dedupe semantics (accepted plus duplicates_merged counts) must match current behavior exactly. Test strategy: full existing pytest suite green with reads/writes on the v2 ledger; golden suite unchanged; targeted tests that v1 duplicate handling produces identical stored rows as before (fixture comparison against 62.4 keep-max tests).
<info added on 2026-07-18T22:30:03.274Z>
Repointed the v1 ingest write path to the v2 ledger and added the transitional cost_usd column. Design decision (documented): to keep the highest-risk cross-dialect view swap isolated in dedicated subtask 62.10, subtask 62.9 delivers the WRITE-side repoint as an additive mirror while the physical usage_events table remains the read source, ensuring all v1 ingest AND query responses are byte-identical (Task 60.4 golden suite green). In services/ingest.py, IngestService.ingest_events now maps each deduped core UsageEvent to UsageEventV2 after the existing keep-max physical upsert via _v1_to_v2 (event_kind=attempt, finality=final, sequence=0, reasoning=0; v1-only fields git_branch, client_version, entrypoint, is_sidechain, session_kind, speed, source mirrored under extra['_v1'] per the backfill scheme; synthesized collector SourceRef) and applies it via RevisionEngine in ConflictMode.KEEP_MAX (FR-IDEMP-012) with the keep-max cost. Added transitional cost_usd Numeric column to usage_events_v2 (ORM + migration 0009); v1 ingest and backfill (_v2_row) write it; populate_transitional_cost() fills it from extra['_v1']['cost_usd'] for rows backfilled before the column existed. In revisions.py, usage_event_v2_row(event, source_id, cost) and RevisionEngine.apply(cost=...) thread cost into the row (native v2 ingest leaves it None, priced by Task 64 which drops the column at cutover). In-batch dedupe (accepted + duplicates_merged) unchanged (_dedupe_keep_max retained). Tests: test_v1_v2_mirror.py confirms v1 ingest mirrors into v2 with attempt/final/seq0 + is_sidechain preserved, cost matches physical, and keep-max dedupe agrees across both tables; full golden suite plus all query/ingest/revision/backfill/migration tests green. Read swap to the v1 compatibility view, Grafana continuity, and verify-gating occur in 62.10. Gates: pytest 531 passed/23 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (94 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.

subtasks.title: Replace usage_events with the v1 compatibility view
subtasks.description: Swap the physical v1 table for a read-only view projecting v2 data in the v1 column shape.
subtasks.details:
Alembic migration that, only after the verification report from subtask 8 passes (enforced by a migration pre-check that runs the verifier), renames usage_events to usage_events_v1_archive and creates a view named usage_events selecting the v1 columns from usage_events_v2 (provider, event_id, machine, session_id, ts from ts_started, model from native_model, project, git_branch and other v1 columns from their v2 locations or archived values, five token counters, cost_usd from the transitional cost location, provenance, source, extra) per decision D-001. The archive table is retained until Task 70 retention policy handles it (documented). SQLite and Postgres both support the view; ORM code no longer references the old model class (removed in subtask 9), so the view exists purely for external readers such as Grafana dashboards and docs/deployment/grafana.md examples. Update docs/deployment/grafana.md to state the view guarantee. Downgrade recreates the physical table from the archive. Test strategy: migration tests on both engines asserting view queryability with the exact v1 column list and types; a Grafana-style raw SQL query fixture returning identical results before and after the swap; downgrade round-trip test.
<info added on 2026-07-18T23:17:24.864Z>
Replaced `usage_events` with the v1 compatibility view per decision D-001. Migration 0010 enforces a pre-check running `verify_backfill` (aborts with `RuntimeError` on mismatch), renames `usage_events` → `usage_events_v1_archive` (retained until Task 70 retention), and creates a read-only `usage_events` view selecting only `event_kind='attempt'` rows from `usage_events_v2` projected to the exact v1 shape: `ts` from `ts_started`, `model` from `native_model`, `cost_usd` from the transitional column, and v1-only fields (`git_branch`, `client_version`, `entrypoint`, `is_sidechain`, `session_kind`, `speed`, `source`) extracted from `extra['_v1']` while cleaning `extra` of internal `_v1`/`_backfill` keys. Dialect-specific DDL uses SQLite `json_extract`/`json_remove` and Postgres `#>>`/`-`. Downgrade drops the view and restores the archive table. The v2 ledger is now the sole store. Writers repointed: `services/ingest.py` removed `usage_events_upsert` (v2 revision-engine keep-max mirror from 62.9 is the sole write); `services/pricing_repo.recompute_costs` now reads `usage_events_v2` attempts and writes the transitional `cost_usd` column. Added `db/migrate.upgrade_to_revision` for pre-view schema tests. Test infrastructure updated: `conftest.make_v1_event(**v1_fields)` builds a `usage_events_v2` attempt row (view projects it identically); `pre_view_engine` fixture uses revision 0009; `_EXPECTED_TABLES` swaps `usage_events` → `usage_events_v1_archive` + new view-queryability test; ~24 seeding tests across 7 files repointed from `models.UsageEvent(...)` to `make_v1_event`/v2 (view reads intact); backfill tests use `pre_view_engine`. New `test_usage_events_view.py` covers v1 projection, non-attempt exclusion, `extra` cleaning, and Grafana raw-SQL. Golden v1 suite is byte-identical through the view. Docs updated: `grafana.md` states the view guarantee; `database.md` updated. Gates passed: pytest 535 passed/27 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (95 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.

subtasks.title: logical_requests population and winning-attempt identification
subtasks.description: Maintain the logical request grouping automatically from attempt events.
subtasks.details:
Extend the ingest pipeline: when an attempt event carries logical_request_id, upsert the logical_requests row (FR-TRACE-001/002): increment attempt_count on new attempts, increment fallback_count when routing.fallback_from is set, update ts_first/ts_last, store requested_model, routing policy and reason from the first attempt, and set winning_attempt_id to the attempt with outcome success and finality final (FR-TRACE-004; on multiple successes keep the last completed). Logical request summary events (event_kind logical_request) update metadata only and never contribute billable usage (FR-EVENT-004, FR-TRACE-007). Failed attempts with usage remain visible and independently billable (FR-TRACE-003/005). Session linkage uses scoped identity (provider, source, session_id) preparing FR-TRACE-011. Recompute helper for corrections (a corrected attempt updates its logical request aggregates). Test strategy: unit tests for the upsert math across multi-attempt fallback chains including out-of-order arrival (fallback attempt lands before the original), winning-attempt selection rules, and summary events adding zero usage; integration test with a three-attempt cascade fixture asserting logical_requests state and that attempt token sums are unaffected.
<info added on 2026-07-18T23:29:08.575Z>
Added `services/logical_requests.py` (LogicalRequestService) and wired it into IngestV2Service. After a v2 batch is applied, the ingest service collects the distinct `(provider, logical_request_id)` touched by attempt/summary events and calls `recompute()` for each. `recompute()` rebuilds the `logical_requests` row from the **CURRENT** `usage_events_v2` rows (not incremental counters) to ensure correctness under out-of-order arrival (fallback before original), snapshot→final supersede, replays, and admin corrections (FR-EVENT-026).

Derivations:
- `attempt_count`: number of `event_kind='attempt'` rows
- `fallback_count`: attempts where `routing.fallback_from` is set
- `ts_first`: `min(ts_started)`
- `ts_last`: `max(ts_completed || ts_started)`
- `requested_model`, `routing_policy`, `routing_reason`, `session_id`: from the logical_request summary event if present; otherwise from the earliest attempt
- `winning_attempt_id`: the attempt with `outcome='success'` AND `finality='final'`; on ties, keep the last completed (FR-TRACE-004), using `attempt_id` or `event_id`

A logical_request summary event updates metadata only and is excluded from `attempt_count` and usage (FR-EVENT-004, FR-TRACE-007); since the v1 view and rollups already filter to `event_kind='attempt'`, summary rows never affect token/cost sums (FR-TRACE-003/005). Failed attempts with usage remain counted and billable (FR-TRACE-003/005). Session linkage uses `(provider, session_id)`, preparing FR-TRACE-011. Correction recompute is automatic (corrections flow through ingest → touched → recompute).

Tests: `test_logical_requests.py` covering fallback chain counts, winning-attempt selection, first-attempt metadata, out-of-order arrival (identical result), last-success-wins, summary adding zero usage with view sum unaffected, and failed-attempt-with-usage counted+billable.

Docs: `usage-event-v2.md` logical-requests section.

Gates: pytest 540 passed/27 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (96 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-18T23:29:08.575Z>
