tasks.title: TOK-4: Source Registry and Scoped API Tokens
tasks.description: Represent gateways, collectors, SDKs, and importers as first-class reporting sources with health tracking; add token scopes and optional per-token source allowlists; enforce scopes across v1, v2, and WebSocket. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.6, 12.16; Epic TOK-4; FR-SOURCE-001..012, FR-SEC-001..011; decision D-011.
tasks.details:
Objective: separate exporter identity from machine identity and introduce least-privilege ingest tokens (Migration Phase 2).

Design: sources table (type collector|gateway|sdk|importer|manual, name, version, instance_id, optional machine link, token label, first_seen, last_seen, billing_mode per D-007); auto-registration from v2 payload source identity (D-011); source health (last successful ingest, recent error count, schema version, clock skew per R-009); v1 collector traffic attributed to a derived source so the existing machines table remains supported (FR-SOURCE-008, FR-SOURCE-003); source labels mutable without changing event identity (FR-SOURCE-010); revoked sources never delete history (FR-SOURCE-012). Token scopes: ingest:events, ingest:limits, ingest:aggregates, query:read, admin:tokens, admin:corrections (FR-INGEST-003 plus D-002); ingest-only tokens cannot access query endpoints (FR-INGEST-004); optional per-token source allowlist (FR-INGEST-020, FR-SEC-004); tokens remain hashed at rest with plaintext shown once (FR-SEC-001/002); bootstrap token stays full-admin for first-run (FR-SEC-008); auth failures reveal nothing about label existence (FR-SEC-010); last-used retained (FR-SEC-006); WebSocket authorization matches REST (NFR-SEC-008).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-4): source type and version stored; ingest tokens have scopes; sources have freshness and health status; existing machine tracking remains compatible; aiProviderProxy can receive an ingest-only token.

Test strategy: unit tests for scope authorization; integration tests for token scope enforcement (ingest-only denied on query, admin required for token and correction endpoints), revoked token rejection, WebSocket auth; migration tests for api_tokens scope columns; source auto-registration and health tests including clock-skew warning.

subtasks.title: sources table and auto-registration from v2 payloads

Auto-registration service (services/sources.py): every v2 ingest resolves the payload source object to a sources row, creating on first sight (decision D-011) and updating last_seen and version; source identity is never conflated with machine identity (FR-SOURCE-003) but one machine may host multiple sources (FR-SOURCE-009); labels are mutable without changing event identity (FR-SOURCE-010); revoking a source stops accepting its events (when the token allowlist enforces it) but never deletes history (FR-SOURCE-012). usage_events_v2.source_id gains its FK now. Test strategy: unit tests for resolve-or-create idempotency and version bumping; migration tests both engines; integration test that two sources on one machine coexist and that revocation blocks nothing historically.

subtasks.title: Source health tracking service
subtasks.description: Freshness, error counts, schema version, and clock skew per source.
subtasks.details:
Extend services/sources.py with health state per source: last_successful_ingest, recent_error_count (rolling window from ingest rejections attributed to the source), reported schema_version from the batch envelope, and clock skew computed from the delta between batch receipt time and max event timestamp (bounded future timestamps rejected per R-009 mitigation, skew beyond a warning threshold recorded as a clock_skew data quality event). Staleness derives from last_successful_ingest against a configurable per-source-type threshold (collectors 30 minutes, gateways 10 minutes defaults) and feeds both the dashboard sources page (Task 67) and the stale_source alert kind (Task 68) per FR-SOURCE-005/006/007. Health computation is query-time from stored fields, not a background job, to stay simple. Test strategy: unit tests for skew math including negative skew, staleness thresholds per type, and error-window accounting; integration test ingesting healthy then erroring batches and asserting the health transitions; data quality record emission test for skew.
<info added on 2026-07-19T00:12:21.690Z>
Added source health tracking: migration 0012 adds columns to `sources` (`last_successful_ingest`, `recent_error_count` default 0, `error_window_started_at`, `reported_schema_version`, `clock_skew_seconds` Float); `SourceHealthService` in `services/sources.py` wired into v2 ingest. Health is query-time from stored fields (no background job). `record_ingest(source_id, received_at, schema_version, max_event_ts, error_count)` per batch sets `reported_schema_version`; on zero errors advances `last_successful_ingest`; maintains rolling `recent_error_count` keyed to `error_window_started_at` (accumulates within `source_error_window_seconds`, resets when window lapses); computes `clock_skew_seconds = max_event_ts - received_at` and records a `clock_skew` data-quality event when `abs(skew)` exceeds `source_clock_skew_warn_seconds` (R-009 mitigation; bounded-future rejection stays in wire/validation layer). `staleness_threshold(type)` returns per-type thresholds (collector 1800s, gateway 600s, default 1800s); `health(source, now)` returns `SourceHealth` dataclass with `stale = last_successful_ingest is None or older than threshold` (never-ingested is stale). Config: `source_stale_collector/gateway/default_seconds`, `source_error_window_seconds`, `source_clock_skew_warn_seconds`. `IngestV2Service` constructs `SourceHealthService` and, after applying the batch, records health for the batch's primary source (`received_at` reused with `ingest_batches` row, max `ts_started`, rejected count as errors); multi-source batches attribute to the first source (documented). Feeds Task 67 sources page and Task 68 `stale_source` alert. Tests: `test_source_health.py` (healthy sets `last_successful`, error-window accumulate + reset-after-lapse, positive clock skew records DQ with correct seconds, negative skew under threshold no DQ, per-type staleness thresholds + fresh→stale transition, never-ingested stale). Gates: pytest 559 passed/27 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (99 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-19T00:12:21.690Z>

tasks.title: TOK-5: Pricing Rate Cards and Cost Engine v2
tasks.description: Replace fixed token price rows with a generic rate-card system plus billable units; separate computed costs from immutable usage; add billing modes for dual cost metrics; async cost computation with auditable repricing; LiteLLM import with dry-run diff. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.7, 12.8; Epic TOK-5; FR-PRICE-001..022, FR-COST-001..013; decisions D-006, D-007, D-015, D-016.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral pricing that preserves historical costs exactly (Migration Phase 2 and 3).

Design: rate_cards table with provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from, optional effective_to, currency, region, service_tier, mode realtime|batch, context_bracket, decimal unit_price, source, verified_at, priority, override flag (D-006); overlap rejection (FR-PRICE-005); unit-type vocabulary from PRD 12.7.2 (input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token, reasoning_token, request, web_search_request, tool_call, image_input, image_output, audio_input_second, audio_output_second, video_second, storage_byte_hour, batch_input_token, batch_output_token); billable_units table for non-token counts per event (FR-DIM-007/008). computed_costs separate from usage rows: cost_status priced|unpriced|partial|estimated|error, pricing_version, currency, calculated_at, observed_cost for exporter reconciliation (FR-COST-001..006, D-016); missing pricing yields unpriced, never a silent default (FR-PRICE-002); partial names missing units (FR-COST-007). Cost computation asynchronous so ingest is never rejected by pricing failures (FR-COST-008/009, NFR-REL-007); repricing is an explicit, audited, reversible admin operation (FR-PRICE-019/020). Billing modes: every source or account carries billing_mode api_billed|subscription (FR-COST-011); rollups and APIs expose actual API spend and subscription-equivalent value separately, never merged (FR-COST-012/013, D-007). Existing pricing rows migrate losslessly into rate_cards without changing historical results (FR-PRICE-009); Anthropic cache short and long writes, OpenAI cached input and reasoning, and Z.ai cached input remain supported (FR-PRICE-010..012). Imports: curated official sources preferred, LiteLLM labeled fallback; every import produces a dry-run diff requiring explicit audited apply (FR-PRICE-014/015/021, D-015). Decimal arithmetic only (FR-PRICE-017).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-5): existing pricing migrates without historical change; Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai rate cards supported; missing prices produce unpriced status; cost records retain pricing version and source; search and tool request charges and cached input representable; dry-run price import and diff exist.

Test strategy: unit tests for pricing selection (tier, mode, context bracket, priority, override) and decimal cost computation and billable unit aggregation; regression test that historical costs are bit-identical after adding new rates (AC-007); migration-equality test that recomputing v1 events under rate_cards equals stored v1 costs; integration tests for missing pricing, unpriced reports, import dry-run and apply auditing.

subtasks.title: rate_cards table and lossless migration of existing pricing rows
subtasks.description: The generic rate-card store replacing fixed token price rows.
subtasks.details:
Alembic migration creating rate_cards (id autoincrement, provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from date, effective_to date nullable, currency default USD, region nullable, service_tier nullable, mode realtime|batch default realtime, context_bracket nullable string like gt200k, unit_price Numeric(20,10) per-unit price stored per single unit with per-MTok conversion handled at import, source, verified_at, priority int default 0, override bool default false, created_at) with unique constraint over (provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from, coalesced tier, mode, context_bracket, priority) and index on (provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from). Data migration: each existing pricing row expands into five rate_cards rows (input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token) with effective_from preserved, source copied, per-MTok prices divided by 1e6 exactly in Decimal (FR-PRICE-009). The old pricing table remains untouched and read-only until subtask 11 confirms equality, then keeps serving the v1 /api/v1/pricing endpoint via adapter. Test strategy: migration tests both engines; Decimal exactness test that expanded rows reproduce original per-MTok values with zero drift; constraint tests for duplicate rejection; downgrade drops rate_cards leaving pricing intact.
<info added on 2026-07-19T01:47:13.009Z>

Full coalesced-NULL overlap rejection is the service layer (64.3); the DB constraint catches fully-specified duplicates. db/pricing_migration.expand_pricing_to_rate_cards(connection): each v1 pricing row expands into 5 rate_cards rows (input_token/output_token/cache_read_token/cache_write_short_token/cache_write_long_token) with effective_from preserved, source copied, unit_price = per_mtok / Decimal(1_000_000) exact (FR-PRICE-009, exact for any per-MTok with <=4 decimals = all real prices). Note: fresh deploys run migrations before startup pricing seed so rate_cards seeding for fresh installs is a later subtask (64.5/64.7). Tests: test_rate_cards.py (round-trip incl tier/context_bracket/verified_at, lossless expansion where unit_price*1e6 reproduces each per-MTok exactly, duplicate-grain IntegrityError, priority distinguishes grain for override layers, pricing table intact after expansion). subtasks.title: billable_units table and unit-type vocabulary
subtasks.description: Non-token billable unit storage per attempt event.
subtasks.details:
Define the unit-type vocabulary as a core constant module in packages/core (tokemetry_core/units.py) with the PRD 12.7.2 list: input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token, reasoning_token, request, web_search_request, tool_call, image_input, image_output, audio_input_second, audio_output_second, video_second, storage_byte_hour, batch_input_token, batch_output_token; token unit types map one to one onto the typed counters of usage_events_v2 and are never stored in billable_units (PP-007, FR-DIM-007). Alembic migration creating billable_units (provider, event_id, unit_type, quantity Numeric(20,6)) with composite unique (provider, event_id, unit_type) and FK-style reference to usage_events_v2. Ingest wiring: v2 events may carry a billable_units map (added to the wire model as an optional bounded dict validated against the vocabulary); hosted-tool charges like web_search_request land here (FR-DIM-008); unknown counters remain in provider extension metadata (FR-EVENT-016) until promoted to vocabulary. Test strategy: unit tests for vocabulary validation and rejection of token types in the map; migration tests; ingest round-trip test storing and re-reading unit rows; revision-engine test that superseding an event replaces its unit rows atomically.
<info added on 2026-07-19T02:02:58.672Z>
Added billable-units vocabulary + table + wire field + atomic replacement. packages/core/tokemetry_core/units.py: TOKEN_UNIT_TYPES (input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token, reasoning_token, batch_input_token, batch_output_token) which map 1:1 onto usage_events_v2 counters and are NEVER stored in billable_units (PP-007/FR-DIM-007); BILLABLE_UNIT_TYPES (request, web_search_request, tool_call, image_input, image_output, audio_input_second, audio_output_second, video_second, storage_byte_hour); ALL_UNIT_TYPES = union; validate_billable_units() rejects token types, unknown types, negative quantities, and >32 entries (UnitTypeError). UsageEventV2 gains optional bounded billable_units dict[str,float] with a field_validator delegating to validate_billable_units (token/unknown keys -> ValidationError); updated the schema-stability EXPECTED_PROPERTIES. billable_units table (migration 0016 + BillableUnit ORM): id PK, provider, event_id, unit_type, quantity Numeric(20,6), unique (provider,event_id,unit_type), composite FK to usage_events_v2 (provider,event_id) (enforced on Postgres; SQLite doesn't enforce FKs by default so the test asserts the unique grain which is enforced on both). RevisionEngine.apply now replaces an event's billable units atomically on write (delete existing + insert current), skipping the delete for a fresh event with no units (perf); superseding replaces, and a supersede with no units clears them. Tests: core test_units.py (disjoint sets, accept non-token, reject token/unknown/negative/too-many) + wire billable_units tests in test_usage_v2.py; server test_billable_units.py (unique grain rejection, ingest stores units, supersede replaces atomically not accumulates, supersede with no units clears). Gates: pytest 607 passed/35 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (107 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.

subtasks.title: Rate resolution service
subtasks.description: Effective-date, tier, mode, bracket, and priority aware price lookup with overlap rejection.
subtasks.details:
Add services/pricing_v2.py: resolve(provider, native_model, unit_type, at_ts, tier, mode, context_bracket) returning the applicable rate. Selection rules: candidate rows where effective_from <= at_ts date and (effective_to null or >= date), matching unit_type and mode, tier match or null-tier fallback, bracket match or null-bracket fallback, then highest priority wins with override rows beating non-override at equal priority (FR-PRICE-004 documented precedence); dated-model to base-model fallback preserved from the current PricingTable.resolve base_model_id logic so dated Claude ids keep resolving. Overlap validation on write: two rows with identical (provider, model, unit_type, tier, mode, bracket, priority) and intersecting date ranges are rejected (FR-PRICE-005). Historical cost always uses the rate effective at event time (FR-PRICE-001); adding rows with later effective_from never changes past results (FR-PRICE-016 for automation paths). All arithmetic Decimal (FR-PRICE-017). Test strategy: exhaustive unit tests over the precedence lattice (tier and bracket fallbacks, priority, override), date-boundary tests at exact effective_from midnight UTC, overlap rejection matrix, base-id fallback parity against existing pricing/table.py tests.
<info added on 2026-07-19T02:17:44.849Z>
Added `services/pricing_v2.py` with `resolve_rate(session, provider, native_model, unit_type, at, tier=None, mode='realtime', context_bracket=None) -> ResolvedRate|None` and `check_overlap(...)`. Candidates are date-valid (`effective_from <= at.date() <= effective_to` or open), matching `unit_type` + `mode`; tier requested requires `service_tier == tier` OR `IS NULL` (null-tier fallback), no tier requires `IS NULL` only; same for `context_bracket`. Survivors use `_precedence` to pick max by `(tier-exact, bracket-exact, priority, override, effective_from)` where higher wins (FR-PRICE-004). Dated-to-base model fallback preserved via `tokemetry_core.pricing.table.base_model_id`: `_candidate_models` yields native, then base (dated→undated), or for undated queries the base's dated snapshots newest-first (parity with v1 `PricingTable`). Historical cost uses the rate effective at event time (FR-PRICE-001), so later `effective_from` rows never change past results (FR-PRICE-016). `check_overlap` rejects two cards on identical grain (`provider, native_model, unit_type, tier, mode, bracket, priority`) with intersecting date ranges (open-ended = `date.max`; FR-PRICE-005), with `exclude_id` for updates. All arithmetic uses `Decimal` (FR-PRICE-017). Fixed mypy: `_null_match` returns `ColumnElement[bool]`. Tests in `test_pricing_v2.py` cover basic resolve, date boundaries (exact `effective_from` and past `effective_to`), tier exact-beats-null-fallback + unknown-tier-fallback + no-tier, priority higher wins + override-at-equal-priority beats non-override across date ranges, dated→base fallback, unknown model `None`, and overlap rejection (adjacent allowed + different-grain allowed). Gates: pytest 614 passed/35 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (108 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-19T02:17:44.849Z>

subtasks.title: computed_costs table separating cost from usage facts
subtasks.description: Cost records with status, pricing version, and reconciliation fields.
subtasks.details:
Alembic migration creating computed_costs (provider, event_id referencing usage_events_v2, pricing_version string, cost_status priced|unpriced|partial|estimated|error per FR-COST-006, amount Numeric(20,10) nullable, currency, billing_mode api_billed|subscription, subscription_equivalent_amount Numeric(20,10) nullable, missing_units JSONType nullable per FR-COST-007, observed_cost Numeric(20,10) nullable for exporter reconciliation per FR-COST-003 and decision D-016, calculated_at, active bool) with unique constraint on (provider, event_id, pricing_version) and a partial-unique concept for one active row per event per pricing version (FR-COST-001; enforce in service layer for SQLite compatibility). Backfill: the transitional v1 cost carried through 62.8 materializes as computed_costs rows with pricing_version v1-legacy and cost_status priced (or unpriced where cost was NULL), after which the transitional column on the ledger is dropped and the compatibility view of 62.10 re-points its cost_usd projection to the active computed cost. Observed cost never replaces authoritative cost automatically (FR-COST-004); drift between observed and computed is exposed by a query helper (FR-COST-005). Test strategy: migration and backfill equality tests (every v1 cost value reproduced exactly, NULLs become unpriced); active-row uniqueness service tests; view projection regression via the golden suite.
<info added on 2026-07-19T02:33:35.406Z>
Added computed_costs table (migration 0017 + ComputedCost ORM) separating cost from usage facts. Columns: id PK, provider, event_id, pricing_version, cost_status (priced|unpriced|partial|estimated|error, FR-COST-006), amount Numeric(20,10) nullable, currency, billing_mode (api_billed|subscription, D-007), subscription_equivalent_amount nullable, missing_units JSON nullable (FR-COST-007), observed_cost nullable (FR-COST-003/D-016), calculated_at, active bool; unique (provider, event_id, pricing_version), composite FK to usage_events_v2, ix on (provider, event_id). Backfill (db/pricing_migration.materialize_computed_costs, called by 0017): each ledger attempt row's transitional cost_usd -> an active v1-legacy computed_costs row, cost_status priced where cost exists / unpriced where NULL. services/computed_costs.py record_cost(...) enforces exactly one active row per event (FR-COST-001, service-enforced for SQLite): deactivates prior active rows then upserts the (provider,event_id,pricing_version) row active, keeping prior-version rows for audit; validates cost_status; active_cost() returns the current row. Observed cost stored separately, never auto-replaces authoritative cost (FR-COST-004); drift is a query concern (FR-COST-005). SCOPE DECISION (documented): the full cutover -- dropping the transitional usage_events_v2.cost_usd column and re-pointing the 62.10 compatibility view's cost_usd projection to the active computed cost -- is deferred to land WITH the cost engine (64.5) that becomes the authoritative computed_costs writer, isolating the high-risk cross-dialect view change (mirrors the 62.9->62.10 write-then-read split). Until then the table is dual-populated (backfill now; cost engine on ingest) and the view is unchanged so the golden suite stays byte-identical. Tests: test_computed_costs.py (materialization reproduces v1 cost incl NULL->unpriced + active + v1-legacy version; record_cost keeps one active row across repricing versions retaining old for audit; same-version upsert; unknown-status ValueError). Gates: pytest 618 passed/36 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (110 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.

subtasks.title: Cost engine v2 with unpriced and partial semantics
subtasks.description: Compute cost from token counters plus billable units against rate cards.
subtasks.details:
Rewrite services/cost.py as CostEngineV2: for a final attempt event, gather the applicable unit quantities (six token counters mapped to their unit types, reasoning priced only when a reasoning_token rate exists otherwise folded into output per provider strategy, plus billable_units rows), resolve each through the rate service, sum Decimal amounts quantized to 0.000001, and write a computed_costs row with pricing_version equal to a monotonically increasing pricing-state identifier (hash or counter bumped on every rate card apply, FR-PRICE-018). Missing rate for any consumed unit type yields cost_status partial with missing_units listing them; no rates at all yields unpriced with amount null (FR-PRICE-002, PP-008); resolver errors yield error status with detail in the task log, never an ingest rejection (FR-COST-008). Snapshots are not priced; only finality final attempt events receive cost records (FR-COST-001). Hosted tool and search request fees are additive to token fees (FR-PRICE-013). Test strategy: unit tests for priced, partial, unpriced, and error paths, additive unit fees, reasoning fold-in versus separate pricing, quantization, and pricing_version stamping; property test that recomputing with an unchanged pricing state is idempotent.
<info added on 2026-07-19T02:49:09.529Z>
Added CostEngineV2 (services/cost_v2.py) with pricing-state version helpers. Implemented `compute_and_record(event, pricing_version=None)` to price only final attempt events (snapshots/non-attempts return `None` with no `computed_costs` row, FR-COST-001); gathers five token counters mapped to unit types, folds reasoning into output when no `reasoning_token` rate exists (default strategy; provider strategies deferred to 64.7), and adds `billable_units` additively (FR-PRICE-013); resolves each consumed unit (`qty>0`) via `resolve_rate` at `event.ts_started` with tier; sums `Decimal` quantized to `0.000001`. Cost status logic: all consumed priced → `priced`; some priced + some missing → `partial` with `missing_units={units:[...]}` (FR-PRICE-002); nothing priced → `unpriced` with `amount NULL` (PP-008); resolver exception → `error` status (logged, never rejects ingest, FR-COST-008). Zero-consumption attempt: `priced` amount `0` if model has `input_token` rate, else `unpriced`. Records via `record_cost` using `pricing_version` from `current_pricing_version` (app_settings `'pricing_version'`, default `'1'`); `bump_pricing_version` increments it (used by rate-card apply 64.9/64.10) ensuring recomputation under unchanged pricing state is idempotent (upserts same row) while a bump creates a new active version (FR-PRICE-018). SCOPE: standalone service (v1 `services/cost.py` CostEngine untouched); async worker, ingest wiring, and transitional-column/view cutover remain in 64.6 to keep the golden suite byte-identical. Tests: `test_cost_v2.py` covering priced sum + version=1, partial with missing output, unpriced no rates, additive web_search fee, reasoning fold-into-output vs separate reasoning rate, snapshot not priced (no row), recompute idempotent (one row), and version bump creating new active row. Gates: pytest 627 passed/36 skipped, ruff clean, mypy strict clean (111 files), trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-19T02:49:09.529Z>

subtasks.title: Asynchronous cost worker and auditable repricing job
subtasks.description: Cost computation off the ingest path; explicit reversible repricing.
subtasks.details:
Add a background cost worker (asyncio task started in app.py like the alert engine loop): consumes newly finalized events (queue fed by the ingest service plus a sweep for events lacking an active cost record) and writes computed_costs; ingest latency is therefore independent of pricing (FR-COST-009, NFR-PERF-005) and cost failure never rejects usage (NFR-REL-007). Repricing: an explicit administrative operation (POST /api/v2/pricing/reprice with time range and optional provider/model filters) that computes costs under the current pricing state as new computed_costs rows with the new pricing_version, flips active atomically per event, retains prior rows for reversibility (FR-PRICE-019/020), records an audit entry with actor, filters, affected counts, and pricing versions, and triggers rollup recomputation for affected days (FR-ROLLUP-009 dependency noted for Task 66). A revert endpoint re-activates a named prior pricing_version for the same range. Test strategy: worker tests for eventual cost coverage after bursts and after worker restart (sweep catch-up); repricing integration test proving old rows retained, active flips, revert restores exact prior amounts, and audit entries written; ingest-latency isolation test with a deliberately failing resolver.
<info added on 2026-07-19T03:23:12.667Z>
Implemented the async cost worker and auditable repricing. services/cost_worker.py: sweep_uncosted_costs finds final attempt events lacking an ACTIVE computed_costs row (correlated ~exists on provider+event_id+active) and prices up to batch_size via CostEngineV2.compute_and_record_row; billable_units_for loads non-token units. Gives eventual coverage after ingest bursts and backlog catch-up after a worker restart without re-pricing covered events. app.py: _cost_loop background task mirroring _alert_loop, gated by config cost_worker_enabled/interval/batch_size; started+cancelled in lifespan. services/repricing.py: reprice(actor,start,end,provider?,native_model?) bumps pricing version, recomputes matching final attempts as new computed_costs rows under the new version, flips active, retains prior rows; revert(actor,pricing_version,...) re-activates a named prior version restoring exact prior amounts; both write audit_log. db/models.py AuditLog + migration 0018_audit_log. api/v2/pricing.py POST reprice/revert require admin:pricing scope. Refactored cost_v2.py earlier to add compute_and_record_row (ledger-row path) sharing _compute via PriceInputs. Tests: test_cost_worker.py (7), test_repricing.py (7 incl provider+native_model filters, revert-noop, unpriceable-does-not-raise), test_pricing_admin_api.py (3 incl scope enforcement). New modules 100% covered (repricing after branch tests). conftest sets cost_worker_enabled=False so the loop never runs mid-test; test_migrations expects audit_log. Rollup recompute for affected days deferred to Task 66. Gates: 429 passed/36 skipped, ruff+mypy+trivy clean.
</info added on 2026-07-19T03:23:12.667Z>

subtasks.title: Provider pricing strategy plugins: anthropic, openai, zai, generic
subtasks.description: Per-provider unit semantics behind the plugin interface.
subtasks.details:
Extend the PricingStrategy abstraction in packages/core (interfaces.py) to v2 semantics: a strategy declares which unit types a provider emits, how provider-reported counters map onto them, and provider-specific rules. Implement four plugins under packages/core pricing/strategies/: anthropic (port of current behavior; cache_write_short and cache_write_long distinct per FR-PRICE-010; reasoning folded into output since Anthropic does not bill it separately); openai (cached input tokens map to cache_read_token; a single cache-write concept must not be misrepresented as Anthropic TTL categories, metadata notes the semantic per FR-DIM-006; reasoning_tokens priced as output unless a separate reasoning rate exists per FR-PRICE-011; web_search_request and tool request fees additive); zai (GLM cached input pricing per FR-PRICE-012, reasoning handling per provider docs); generic (plain token-linear fallback for unregistered providers, NFR-MAIN-002). Strategies register through the existing provider registry (registry.py) so new providers are plugins without core changes (PP-011, NFR-MAIN-002, memory rule design-for-multi-provider). Test strategy: per-strategy unit tests with provider-realistic fixtures (Anthropic event with both cache TTL writes; OpenAI event with cached input plus reasoning plus web search; Z.ai event with cached input); a fake-provider test proving the abstraction (fake.py pattern); registry resolution tests.
<info added on 2026-07-19T03:54:21.310Z>
Implemented v2 provider pricing strategy plugins with a new abstraction in `interfaces.py`: added `ReasoningBilling` enum (`FOLD_INTO_OUTPUT` | `SEPARATE_IF_RATED`) and `ProviderPricingStrategyV2` ABC (declares provider id, reasoning billing policy, and `emitted_token_units()`; base `quantities()` maps counters to unit-type `Decimal` map, filters to emitted units, applies reasoning rule, adds billable units; `needs_reasoning_rate()` gates reasoning-rate lookup). Four plugins implemented under `packages/core/pricing/strategies/`: `anthropic` (5 units including both cache-write TTL tiers; reasoning `FOLD_INTO_OUTPUT` since Anthropic never bills it separately), `openai` (input/output/cache_read only; cached input maps to `cache_read_token`, no TTL write tiers per FR-DIM-006; reasoning `SEPARATE_IF_RATED` per FR-PRICE-011; hosted-tool fees additive), `zai` (input/output/cache_read; `SEPARATE_IF_RATED`), and `generic` (5-unit token-linear fallback). In `defaults.py`, `register_pricing_strategies_v2(registry)` wires all four (generic as default), and `default_v2_pricing_registry()` is memoized. In `registry.py`, added `register_pricing_v2(strategy, default=?)`, `pricing_v2(provider)` with generic fallback, and `pricing_v2_providers()()`. `CostEngineV2` now accepts an optional `ProviderRegistry` (defaults to built-ins) and resolves the strategy in `_quantities`, replacing the hardcoded reasoning-fold; anthropic/generic behavior is byte-identical so existing tests stay green. Server `build_registry()` now registers v2 strategies. Design note: reasoning is provider-specific now, so the former engine-level `test_reasoning_priced_separately_with_rate` (which used anthropic) moved to openai (correct `SEPARATE_IF_RATED` provider); added `test_anthropic_folds_reasoning_even_with_rate` and `test_openai_prices_cached_input_and_ignores_cache_write_tiers`. Tests: core `test_pricing_strategies_v2.py` (8 tests: per-strategy fixtures, registry resolution + generic fallback, memoization, fake-provider abstraction proof). Gates: 612 passed/36 skipped, ruff + mypy (112 files) + trivy clean. Docs: `pricing.md` v2 section added.
</info added on 2026-07-19T03:54:21.310Z>
<info added on 2026-07-19T03:55:09.395Z>
Implemented v2 provider pricing strategy plugins (anthropic/openai/zai/generic) behind `ProviderPricingStrategyV2` + `ReasoningBilling` in `interfaces.py`; registered via `ProviderRegistry.register_pricing_v2`/`pricing_v2` (generic fallback); `CostEngineV2` resolves the strategy in `_quantities` with anthropic/generic behavior byte-identical. Anthropic folds reasoning always; openai/zai separate-if-rated; openai/zai drop cache-write TTL tiers. Core `test_pricing_strategies_v2.py` (8). Gates: 612 passed/36 skipped, ruff+mypy+trivy clean.

subtasks.title: Billing modes and dual cost metrics
subtasks.description: Actual API spend versus subscription-equivalent value, never merged.
subtasks.details:
Implement decision D-007 and FR-COST-011..013: billing_mode lives on sources (Task 63 schema) with api_billed and subscription values; an account-level override map in settings covers non-source-attributed usage (v1 collector events from a Max subscription machine default to subscription via config, documented for the existing deployment). Cost engine writes billing_mode onto every computed_costs row: api_billed rows populate amount (actual spend); subscription rows populate subscription_equivalent_amount at equivalent API rates with amount null (no real spend). Aggregation helpers expose actual_spend_usd and subscription_value_usd as separate Decimal sums; nothing ever adds them together (FR-COST-012). The existing dashboard plan-value multiple semantics map onto subscription_value for continuity. Rollup columns land in Task 66; this subtask delivers the computation and query helpers plus settings plumbing. Test strategy: unit tests for mode resolution precedence (source mode, account override, default), dual-metric computation on mixed fixtures (Claude subscription plus OpenAI api_billed), and the never-merged invariant (helper has no combined total); integration test that v1-ingested events on a subscription-configured machine produce subscription-valued cost rows.
<info added on 2026-07-19T04:25:51.553Z>
Implemented billing modes and dual cost metrics. New `services/billing_mode.py` defines `API_BILLED`, `SUBSCRIPTION`, `DEFAULT_BILLING_MODE`, and `BILLING_MODES` constants, plus `resolve_billing_mode(source_mode, machine, overrides)` with explicit precedence: source > machine override > default. `CostEngineV2` updated: `PriceInputs` now includes `source_id` and `machine`; `_record` resolves `billing_mode` (loading `source.billing_mode` by `source_id`) and splits rows—`api_billed` rows populate `amount` (actual spend), while `subscription` rows populate `subscription_equivalent_amount` at equivalent API rates with `amount` set to `None`. The engine constructor accepts `billing_mode_overrides`, threaded through `sweep_uncosted_costs`, `_cost_loop` (from `settings.billing_mode_override_map`), and `reprice` (pricing API reads settings). `config.py` adds `billing_mode_overrides` (comma-separated `machine=mode` setting) and `billing_mode_override_map` property (validates input, raises on invalid format). `services/cost_queries.py` introduces `DualCostMetrics` class with `actual_spend_usd` and `subscription_value_usd` fields (no combined total) and `dual_cost_metrics(session, start, end)` function that computes separate sums over active `computed_costs`, with optional `ts_started` range filtering. `api/v2/sources.py` now imports shared `BILLING_MODES` for DRY compliance. Both v1 and v2 events are source-attributed; thus, a v1 collector on a Max machine receives `subscription` billing via the machine override. Tests include unit `test_billing_mode.py` (9 tests: precedence + config parsing) and integration `test_billing_modes.py` (6 tests: `api_billed` amount, subscription equivalent, machine override, dual metrics never-merged, time range). Gates: 626 passed/36 skipped, ruff + mypy (114 issues resolved) + trivy clean. Docs updated: `pricing.md` now includes a "billing-modes" section.

tasks.title: TOK-6: aiProviderProxy Integration and Client Libraries
tasks.description: Ship the production integration contract for the aiProviderProxy (RelayPlane) exporter: mapping documentation, OpenAPI-generated Python and TypeScript clients with batching and retry wrappers, end-to-end ingest verification for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai attempt events without double counting. PRD-TOK-002 Epic TOK-6; companion PRD FR-TOK-001..030, CanonicalUsageEvent; decisions D-012, D-016.
tasks.details:
Objective: the first direct gateway exporter works end to end; this completes the usable vertical slice (Migration Phase 3, dual write with cost reconciliation).

Design: Tokemetry owns the wire contract; the proxy transforms its CanonicalUsageEvent into the v2 usage event (companion PRD FR-TOK-003). Integration documentation at docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md covers field-by-field mapping (camelCase canonical to snake_case wire), event identity and deterministic fallback IDs, finality and sequence semantics for streaming snapshots, outcome vocabulary, privacy rules (no prompt, response, file path, tool argument, or code content; FR-TOK-029), version negotiation, and batching guidance (default 100 events, 256 KiB; FR-TOK-008..010). Client libraries generated from OpenAPI with thin hand-written wrappers for auth, batching, retry with exponential backoff and jitter on 429 and 5xx, pause on 401, poison-event isolation on 400 and 422 (D-012; companion FR-TOK-011..016) published under packages/clients/python and packages/clients/typescript. Exporter-observed cost is stored as reconciliation metadata and never replaces authoritative cost (FR-COST-003/004, D-016).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-6): integration documentation complete; TypeScript client or examples available; proxy attempt events ingest successfully; retries and fallback attempts do not double count; requested and routed model visible; content-free payload tests pass; source freshness visible.

Test strategy: compose-based integration harness with a test server and mock proxy exporter fixtures for all three providers including retries, fallbacks, and snapshot-then-final sequences; end-to-end assertions that logical request views never double count attempt usage (AC-005/006); contract conformance suite with golden payloads shared with the proxy repository; poison-event behavior verification; ingest-only token provisioning runbook validated.

subtasks.title: Integration contract document for the proxy exporter
subtasks.description: Field-level mapping and semantics contract at docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md.
subtasks.details:
Write docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md, the shared contract for the RelayPlane proxy exporter (companion PRD section 11.13/11.14): field-by-field mapping from CanonicalUsageEvent (camelCase) to the v2 wire event (snake_case) including eventId to event_id, logicalRequestId, attemptId, the model triple, timestamps, six token counters, success/outcome/httpStatus/stopReason/serviceTier, latencyMs and timeToFirstTokenMs, streaming, toolCallCount, routing object incl accountLabel handling (exported only when enabled, never secret material per companion FR-AUTH-008), provenance official|local_estimate, and extra namespace rules (extra.usage for unknown counters per companion FR-USAGE-008 mapping into provider-namespaced extension metadata per FR-EVENT-016). Event identity rules: provider-native ids preferred, deterministic fallback ids (companion FR-USAGE-003/004), streaming snapshots same id with increasing sequence and explicit final (FR-USAGE-005/006). Error and outcome vocabulary shared table. Privacy: prohibited content list and the audit-mode expectation that the proxy can display exact payloads before enabling export (companion NFR-PRIV-005). Version negotiation: schema_version 2, server capabilities via OpenAPI, compatibility promise. Batching and retry guidance mirroring companion FR-TOK-008..016 (100 events, 256 KiB, backoff on 429/5xx, pause on 401, poison isolation on 400/422, replay on ambiguity relying on idempotency). Test strategy: documentation review against both PRDs requirement by requirement; every mapped field cross-checked against the v2 wire model of 62.1; the mapping table becomes the source for the conformance fixtures of subtask 7.
<info added on 2026-07-20T08:07:05.853Z>
Completed. The document now includes transport details (POST /api/v2/ingest/events endpoint, ingest:events scope requirement, batch envelope structure, gzip compression support, batching limits of 100 events/256 KiB), comprehensive field-by-field mapping grounded in the actual UsageEventV2 schema (event_id/kind/finality/sequence, logical_request_id/attempt_id, model triple, three timestamps, six token counters with reasoning tokens kept separate, success vs outcome distinction, http_status/stop_reason/service_tier/streaming/latency/ttft/tool_call_count, routing with account-label-not-secret rule, provenance, source, trace linkage), extension-metadata namespacing rules (billable_units, provider-namespaced extra fields, dimensions), event identity strategy (provider-native IDs preferred with deterministic fallback hash for deduplication on retries), finality and sequence semantics (streaming snapshots share same ID with increasing sequence numbers, final event wins, no double counting), outcome vocabulary definitions, source freshness expectations, cost handling as reconciliation-only (never authoritative), content-free privacy compliance (FR-TOK-029), and version negotiation mechanism (GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event). Documentation validated by cross-checking every mapped field against packages/core usage_v2.UsageEventV2 schema definition.

subtasks.title: TypeScript client package generated from OpenAPI
subtasks.description: packages/clients/typescript with batching and retry wrapper.
subtasks.details:
Set up packages/clients/typescript: types generated from the server OpenAPI document (openapi-typescript) in a build step, plus a small hand-written wrapper class TokemetryClient (fetch-based, no runtime dependencies beyond the platform) providing bearer auth, ingestEvents with automatic batch splitting by count and bytes, retry with exponential backoff and jitter on 429 and 5xx honoring Retry-After, fail-fast pause signaling on 401, structured validation-error surfacing for 400 and 422 so callers can implement poison isolation, and a validate() helper hitting the validate endpoint (decision D-012; companion FR-TOK-011..016 client side). Strict TypeScript, ESLint per repo conventions, vitest tests, 2-space indent per code style. Package private-publishable (npm pack) so the proxy repo can consume it or vendor the generated types. Regeneration script wired into CI so schema drift fails the build. Test strategy: vitest unit tests with a mocked fetch covering batch splitting boundaries, backoff schedule determinism (injected RNG), 401 pause behavior, error-shape parsing against the 62.6 error snapshot, and gzip encoding when available; a smoke test against a live local server in the integration harness of subtask 4.
<info added on 2026-07-20T08:16:21.724Z>
TypeScript client package @tokemetry/client (packages/clients/typescript), generated from OpenAPI.

- packages/clients/generate_openapi.py: emits packages/clients/openapi.json from create_app().openapi(), augmented with the UsageEventV2 + LimitSnapshotV2 wire schemas injected into components.schemas (the ingest endpoints validate bodies manually so FastAPI omits those input schemas); lifts Pydantic $defs into components and rewrites all $defs refs (incl nested, e.g. SourceRef.type->SourceType) so openapi-typescript resolves them. 61 paths.
- src/generated.ts: openapi-typescript output (committed; regen via npm run generate).
- src/client.ts: hand-written IngestClient over the generated UsageEventV2 type (D-012). Auth (bearer ingest:events), batching (100 events / 256 KiB), and the resilient submit policy: retry with full-jitter exponential backoff on 429/5xx (IngestRetryError when exhausted), PAUSE on 401 (IngestAuthError, no retry), and poison-event isolation on 400/422 by BISECTING a rejected batch so a single malformed event is set aside while the good ones ingest. Injectable fetch/sleep/random for tests.
- src/client.test.ts (8 vitest): batching by size + envelope/bearer shape; retry on 429 + 500; exhausted-retry error; 401 pause without retry; single poison isolation on 422; batch bisection isolating the poison and accepting the rest.

vitest 8 passed; tsc --noEmit clean. node_modules gitignored.
</info added on 2026-07-20T08:16:21.724Z>

Integration harness complete at apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/ (fixtures.py, driver.py, conftest.py, test_proxy_harness.py, __init__.py). 21 tests pass.

Fixture library (fixtures.py): 9 realistic proxy-export Scenario batches as plain wire dicts (the shared truth exported for the proxy repo via as_json(), subtask 7 consumes it), each paired with expected accepted/updated/duplicate/rejected/rows. Covers: anthropic cache read + short/long writes; anthropic streaming snapshot sequence (snap seq1->snap seq2->final seq3, one row, accepted=1 updated=2); anthropic retry-after-429 (two attempts, two rows); anthropic client-cancelled partial; openai cached-input + reasoning_tokens + service_tier=priority; openai web_search_request billable unit; zai GLM cached input; cross-provider fallback (anthropic 529 -> openai with routing.fallback_from/fallback_trigger); failed attempt with zero tokens (FR-EVENT-024).

Replay driver (driver.py): drives the REAL Python client (tokemetry_client.IngestClient) against the in-process app -- the client's httpx transport IS the test TestClient over ASGI, so events traverse POST /api/v2/ingest/events exactly as a proxy would. Mints an ingest-only token (scope ingest:events) via POST /api/v1/tokens. conftest.py puts packages/clients/python/src on sys.path (client is a standalone package, not a workspace member). post_batch() helper posts raw dicts to read the server's full count breakdown.

Tests: (1) every fixture event conforms to the published usage_event_json_schema (62.12) -- validates + only-known-properties; (2) each scenario ingests with its exact expected count breakdown + row footprint (direct HTTP); (3) each scenario replay through the Python client is a no-op (AC-003): first run accepted==expected & rows==expected, second run accepted==0 & rows unchanged; (4) full-corpus idempotency; (5) JSON export round-trips.

GOTCHA / client bug found and fixed: the harness caught a real bug in the 65.3 Python client. IngestClient serialized events with model_dump(mode='json') including ALL defaults, emitting dimensions:null (and other None optionals). The server types dimensions as a dict defaulting to {} and 422s an explicit null, so EVERY event was rejected+bisected to poison (accepted=0). Fixed by serializing with exclude_none=True (new _event_json helper in client.py, used by _payload and _iter_batches) so unset optionals are omitted and the server applies its defaults. The TS client is unaffected (JS objects don't materialize defaults). Client unit tests (11) still pass; harness (21) green. ruff clean, mypy strict clean on harness + client (only generated models.py retains its gate-excluded 9 errors).
</info added on 2026-07-20T09:09:19.866Z>

End-to-end acceptance suite complete: apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/test_e2e_acceptance.py, 5 tests (one per acceptance group), all green. Drives fixtures through the REAL HTTP stack: POST /api/v2/ingest/events → POST /api/v2/pricing + /reprice → GET /api/v2/usage, /costs, /attempts, /requests/{provider}/{lr}.

Groups proven:
1. AC-002 per-provider landing: ingest anthropic/openai/zai (cache + reasoning tiers), assert GET /usage?group_by=provider shows all three with correct cache_read/cache_write_long/reasoning counters, GET /usage?group_by=model shows all native models, then seed 12 rate cards + reprice and assert GET /costs?group_by=provider has actual_spend_usd>0 and unpriced_event_count==0 for each provider.

subtasks.title: Provisioning runbook, source freshness, and cost reconciliation drift
subtasks.description: Operational integration: token setup, exporter health, observed-versus-computed cost.
subtasks.details:
Write the operator runbook section of docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md: provisioning an ingest-only token (scopes ingest:events plus ingest:limits, optional source allowlist binding to the proxy source identity), configuring the proxy exporter (baseUrl, token reference not embedded, queue path per companion config), verifying connectivity with relayplane tokemetry test-connection against the validate endpoint, and monitoring exporter health through GET /api/v2/sources freshness. Implement the reconciliation drift query: observed_cost from proxy events versus Tokemetry computed cost, aggregated per provider and day with percentage drift, exposed through the costs query surface (FR-COST-005, US-013; UI panel lands in Task 67). Verify source freshness reflects a silent exporter within threshold (Epic acceptance: source freshness visible). Test strategy: integration test provisioning a scoped token end to end and asserting query denial; drift computation unit tests with agreeing and drifting fixtures; freshness transition test with clock injection; runbook walked through manually against the harness and corrections folded back into the doc.
<info added on 2026-07-20T10:32:39.372Z>
COMPLETED. All three deliverables shipped and verified:

**1. RECONCILIATION DRIFT (provider AND day grouping with percentage)**
Extended cost_reconciliation query (queries_v2.py) to accept group_by parameter from {provider, day}. Added day and drift_pct fields to ReconciliationRow and ReconciliationRowOut schemas. Drift percentage computed as (observed - computed) / computed * 100, quantized to 0.01, null when computed == 0. Day bucket uses portable substr(cast(ts_started, String), 1, 10) key for cross-database compatibility. Endpoint GET /api/v2/costs/reconciliation gained validated group_by query parameter (RECONCILIATION_DIMENSIONS enum), returns 400 on unknown dimension.

**2. OBSERVED_COST END-TO-END WIRING (critical fix)**
Discovered ComputedCost.observed_cost column existed but was NEVER populated in production—wire model lacked the field entirely, making reconciliation impossible with real data. Added observed_cost: Decimal | None (ge=0) to UsageEventV2 Pydantic model, created usage_events_v2.observed_cost column via migration 0024, threaded through shared row builder into CostEngineV2._record and record_cost methods, populating ComputedCost.observed_cost at pricing time for both sweep and reprice code paths. Regenerated openapi.json plus Python and TypeScript clients. Verified complete chain: HTTP ingest with observed_cost → reprice → GET reconciliation endpoint shows observed vs computed drift with real values.

**MIGRATION GOTCHA (breaking fix):** Adding observed_cost column to usage_events_v2 broke historical backfill verification at migration 0010. Root cause: backfill._aggregate_v2 used sa.select(models.UsageEventV2.__table__), pulling full ORM table definition including the new column before it exists in older schema states. Fixed by selecting only the specific columns aggregation actually reads (behavior-preserving, immune to future schema additions). Also fixed two test_backfill_v2 tests that read full ORM entity against pre-0024 schema—switched to explicit-column core SQL selects.

**3. SOURCE FRESHNESS VERIFICATION**
Implemented test_silent_gateway_exporter_goes_stale_after_threshold: gateway source that ingests events then goes silent is fresh at +5 minutes, transitions to stale at +15 minutes (600-second gateway threshold), using SourceHealthService.health(now=...) clock injection for deterministic time control.

**4. OPERATOR RUNBOOK**
Added complete Operator runbook section to docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md covering: provision ingest-only token with scopes ingest:events + ingest:limits + source_allowlist via POST /api/v1/tokens; configure exporter with baseUrl, token-by-reference (not embedded), queuePath, and source identity; verify connectivity using POST /api/v2/ingest/validate plus GET /api/v2/ready; monitor exporter health via GET /api/v2/sources freshness with query params ?type=gateway&stale=true, checking clock_skew and schema drift. Updated field mapping table (observedCost → observed_cost) and Cost reconciliation section documenting group_by parameter and drift_pct calculation.

**TEST COVERAGE**

subtasks.title: Contract conformance suite and content-free payload verification
subtasks.description: Golden payloads shared with the proxy repo; poison and privacy behavior locked.
subtasks.details:
Produce the conformance artifacts the proxy team builds against: a versioned directory of golden request and response pairs (valid batches for all three providers with exact expected ingest responses; invalid batches with exact expected structured errors) exported from the harness fixtures, published under docs/integrations/conformance/ and mirrored as JSON fixtures the proxy repository can vendor (companion FR-TOK-030 snapshot testing hook). Content-free verification: fuzzed payloads embedding prompts, file paths, tool arguments, and code snippets in every extension point are rejected or stripped per policy with the exact error surfaced (AC-011; companion FR-TOK-029); poison-event behavior verified: a batch with one invalid event returns an error identifying the index so recursive splitting terminates (companion FR-TOK-015 client contract). Version the conformance set (v2.0) and document the compatibility promise for future schema evolution (PP-010). Test strategy: conformance suite runs in CI comparing live server responses to golden files; privacy fuzz corpus green; a change to any v2 schema that alters a golden response fails CI until the conformance set is versioned forward deliberately.
<info added on 2026-07-20T10:42:14.773Z>
Delivered: contract-conformance suite v2.0 with content-free and poison-index verification. Implementation in proxy_harness/conformance.py exports build_conformance_document() rendering 9 valid_batches (harness fixture scenarios with exact accepted/updated/duplicate/rejected/corrected counts) and 7 invalid_batches (content-free/poison cases with exact index/code/field_path_contains). Artifact published to docs/integrations/conformance/v2.0/conformance.json for proxy repo vendoring (FR-TOK-030). docs/integrations/conformance/README.md documents layout and PP-010 compatibility promise: versioned sets never mutated; schema changes altering golden outcomes require new version directory; major version for breaking wire changes, minor for compatible refinements.

Content-free corpus (FR-TOK-029, AC-011) verifies content smuggled into every extension point is rejected with exact codes: prompt/file_path/code-snippet in extra returns content_key; disallowed top-level extra namespace returns extra_namespace_not_allowed; content-like dimension key returns dimension_not_allowed; tool_histogram while tool names disabled returns tool_names_disabled. Poison-index verification (FR-TOK-015): 3-event batch with one content-bearing middle event returns 422 with single error at index 1, enabling client recursive bisection termination.

Test coverage in test_conformance_v2.py (19 passing): parametrized valid-golden (200 with exact counts) and invalid-golden (422 with exact errors), extension-point coverage, poison-index verification, and test_golden_export_is_in_sync asserting committed conformance.json equals build_conformance_document() output, failing CI on drift until regenerated and versioned forward.

tasks.title: TOK-7: Query API v2 and Rollups v2
tasks.description: Provider-neutral query endpoints for usage, costs, requests, attempts, sessions, limits, data quality, pricing, and rollups; new rollup grain with source, environment, billing mode, reasoning and cache dimensions; stable pagination; CSV export; Grafana views; v1 query parity. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.12, 12.13; Epic TOK-7; FR-QUERY-001..014, FR-ROLLUP-001..012.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral analytics without double counting (Migration Phase 4).

Design: daily_rollups evolves to grain (day, provider, native_model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode, provenance) adding reasoning and cache dimensions and cost split by cost_status (FR-ROLLUP-004..007); rollups derive exclusively from final attempt events, never snapshots after a final exists and never logical summaries (FR-ROLLUP-001..003); refresh is incremental and idempotent with correction-triggered affected-period recomputation (FR-ROLLUP-008/009). Query framework: keyset pagination (FR-QUERY-003), explicit sort (FR-QUERY-004), selectable aggregation grain where safe (FR-QUERY-005), bounded time ranges for raw high-cardinality queries (NFR-PERF-004), data-quality warnings in responses (FR-QUERY-010), unknown provider and model filters (FR-QUERY-011). Endpoints: GET /api/v2/usage, /api/v2/costs (dual metrics, cost status, pricing version per FR-QUERY-006), /api/v2/requests, /api/v2/attempts, /api/v2/sessions (scoped identity per FR-TRACE-011, winning attempt, fallback filters), /api/v2/limits, /api/v2/data-quality, /api/v2/pricing, /api/v2/rollups. CSV export (FR-QUERY-009) and Grafana-compatible database views (FR-QUERY-014). Existing v1 query endpoints continue to work against the v2 ledger through the service layer (FR-QUERY-012).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-7): v2 query endpoints exist; attempt and logical request semantics prevent double counting; cache and reasoning usage queryable; cost status visible; rollups include provider-neutral dimensions; v1 query endpoints remain functional.

Test strategy: unit tests for the query framework and rollup aggregation; integration tests for rollup refresh, correction recomputation, and double-counting prevention with multi-attempt fixtures; v1 parity regression suite comparing pre-migration and post-migration v1 responses; performance benchmarks for 30-day aggregated views p95 under 500 ms and high-cardinality project and session dimensions (NFR-PERF-003, FR-ROLLUP-012).

subtasks.title: daily_rollups v2 grain migration
subtasks.description: Extend the rollup table to the provider-neutral grain with cost statuses.
subtasks.details:
Alembic migration evolving daily_rollups: extend the grain unique constraint to (day, provider, native_model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode, provenance) with empty-string sentinels for absent dimensions matching the existing convention (FR-ROLLUP-004/005); add reasoning_tokens column alongside the existing five token counters (FR-ROLLUP-006 cache dimensions already present as separate counters); replace the single cost_usd with cost columns by status: cost_priced_usd, cost_partial_usd, cost_estimated_usd, unpriced_event_count, plus subscription_value_usd for the dual metric (FR-ROLLUP-007, FR-COST-012); data migration maps existing rows onto the new grain with source and environment sentinels, billing_mode from the deployment default, and cost_usd into cost_priced_usd. Grafana view compatibility: create or update a stable rollup view so existing external dashboards keep a stable column set (FR-ROLLUP-011); document the migration plan for view consumers. Test strategy: migration upgrade and downgrade tests both engines; grain uniqueness tests; data-migration equality checks (sum of new cost columns equals old cost_usd per day); view stability query fixture.
<info added on 2026-07-19T06:35:18.425Z>
Migration 0019 implemented as additive safer-split: batch_alter_table adds source/environment/billing_mode grain dimensions (sentinels ''/'api_billed'), reasoning_tokens counter, and cost split (cost_priced_usd/cost_partial_usd/cost_estimated_usd/unpriced_event_count/subscription_value_usd) while keeping transitional cost_usd column for backward compatibility; extends daily_rollups_grain unique constraint to (day,provider,model,machine,project,source,environment,billing_mode,provenance); data migration seeds cost_priced_usd from existing cost_usd; creates daily_rollups_grafana view that SUMs back to original (day,provider,model,machine,project) grain for external dashboard stability (FR-ROLLUP-011). DailyRollup ORM model updated to match new schema. upsert.py index_elements and _ROLLUP_UPDATE_COLUMNS updated to new grain. rollups.py _aggregate_day writes grain sentinels and mirrors cost into cost_priced_usd (transitional behavior; full v2 rewrite reading usage_events_v2/computed_costs deferred to 66.2). Test suite test_rollups_v2_grain.py validates billing_mode split allowed, duplicate full-grain rejected, data migration correctness (priced seeded from cost_usd), and grafana view aggregation to stable grain. Existing test_migrations drift check, test_rollups, and test_queries remain green. Full suite 670 passed/40 skipped; ruff+mypy+trivy clean. grafana.md updated documenting grain change and stable view contract for consumers.

subtasks.title: Rollup service v2: final attempts only, incremental, correction-aware
subtasks.description: Recompute rollups from the v2 ledger without double counting.
subtasks.details:
Rewrite services/rollups.py against usage_events_v2 joined with computed_costs: aggregation selects only event_kind attempt with finality final (FR-ROLLUP-001), never superseded snapshots (revision engine guarantees single active row, FR-ROLLUP-002) and never logical_request summaries (FR-ROLLUP-003); group by the full v2 grain with project_group folding preserved; cost columns split by cost_status and billing_mode from the active computed_costs row; provenance preserved. Refresh remains whole-affected-day recomputation triggered by ingest (matching current architecture) but now also triggered by corrections and repricing for their affected periods (FR-ROLLUP-009 consuming the Task 64 repricing hook); refresh is idempotent (FR-ROLLUP-008) and incremental per day (NFR-PERF-006). rebuild_all_rollups admin path updated. Test strategy: unit tests for grain aggregation with mixed providers, statuses, and billing modes; double-counting prevention tests (snapshot-then-final, multi-attempt logical requests, correction flow recomputing exactly the affected days); idempotency test (double refresh identical); parity test that a pure-v1-shaped dataset produces rollups summing identically to the pre-migration algorithm.
<info added on 2026-07-19T07:00:16.827Z>
Implementation complete: services/rollups.py now aggregates from usage_events_v2 (event_kind=attempt AND finality=final only, excluding snapshots and logical_requests per FR-ROLLUP-001/002/003) LEFT JOIN computed_costs (active rows only) for cost split and LEFT JOIN sources for source dimension. Groups by v2 grain (provider, native_model, machine, project_group, source, environment, billing_mode); provenance remains 'derived' for rebuild_all_rollups compatibility. Cost split implemented via SQL CASE sums: cost_priced_usd/cost_partial_usd/cost_estimated_usd by cost_status (api_billed only), unpriced_event_count for unpriced/error statuses, subscription_value_usd from subscription_equivalent_amount for subscription billing_mode; cost_usd = priced+partial+estimated. NULL handling: contributions default to NULL (not 0) so SUM preserves NULL for all-unknown groups; uncosted events fall back to transitional cost_usd in priced bucket for exact v1 parity. _span switched to usage_events_v2 final attempts. Reprice-to-rollup refresh hook wired (FR-ROLLUP-009): repricing.reprice now triggers rollup refresh for affected days using session.bind dialect. Test suite test_rollups_v2_service.py added (6 tests): cost split by status, billing_mode grain split with subscription value, final-attempts-only filter (snapshots/logical_requests excluded), idempotent double refresh, v1 parity via transitional cost, reprice triggers affected rollup refresh. Full suite 676 passed/40 skipped; ruff+mypy(122)+trivy clean; no golden-fixture or query/analytics regressions.
</info added on 2026-07-19T07:00:16.827Z>

subtasks.title: Query framework: pagination, sorting, grain, bounds, warnings
subtasks.description: Shared plumbing for all v2 query endpoints.
subtasks.details:
Add services/query_framework.py plus api/v2 shared dependencies: keyset pagination with opaque cursors stable under concurrent inserts (FR-QUERY-003), explicit sort parameter with documented default per resource (FR-QUERY-004), selectable aggregation grain (day, week, month) where safe (FR-QUERY-005), enforced bounded time ranges for raw event-level queries with a configurable maximum span (NFR-PERF-004), uniform filter parsing for provider, model, source, machine, project, session, agent, environment, and outcome (FR-QUERY-002) including unknown-provider and unknown-model pseudo-filters (FR-QUERY-011), and a data-quality warning envelope attached to responses when the queried range contains unpriced events, unknown models, or stale sources (FR-QUERY-010). Response models in api/v2/schemas with extra=forbid. Test strategy: unit tests for cursor stability (insert between pages, no skips or duplicates), sort and grain validation, range-bound enforcement, filter parsing matrix, and warning attachment logic with seeded data-quality conditions.
<info added on 2026-07-19T07:18:25.269Z>
Implementation complete: services/query_framework.py delivers the shared v2 read plumbing as pure, testable functions. parse_sort validates field or -field against per-resource whitelists and applies documented defaults (FR-QUERY-004). Grain enum (day/week/month) with parse_grain and truncate_to_grain (week anchored to Monday, FR-QUERY-005). enforce_range_bound rejects end<start and enforces config.query_max_range_days (366, NFR-PERF-004). Keyset pagination via encode_cursor/decode_cursor (opaque base64-JSON) and keyset_condition using row-value comparison (sort>v OR (sort=v AND id>id)) for SQLite/Postgres portability; build_page detects next via limit+1, stable under concurrent inserts (FR-QUERY-003). QueryFilters dataclass captures provider/model/source/machine/project/session/agent/environment/outcome plus unknown_provider/unknown_model pseudo-filters (FR-QUERY-002/011). collect_warnings envelope attaches data-quality warnings (unpriced/partial events, unknown_models, stale sources) scoped to the queried range (FR-QUERY-010) with default_stale_before helper. api/v2/schemas defines QueryWarningOut and PageMeta with extra=forbid. QueryParamError raised for invalid sort/grain/cursor/range (endpoints map to 400). Test coverage: unit test_query_framework.py validates sort/grain/truncate/range/cursor round-trip and garbage handling plus build_page logic; integration test_query_framework_db.py confirms keyset stability under concurrent insert (originals appear once, no skips/duplicates) and collect_warnings per-condition, range-scoped, and empty-case behavior. Full suite 688 passed/40 skipped; ruff+mypy(97)+trivy clean. Endpoints in 66.4-66.6 will wire these primitives.
</info added on 2026-07-19T07:18:25.269Z>

subtasks.title: Usage and cost query endpoints
subtasks.description: GET /api/v2/usage and /api/v2/costs with dual metrics.
subtasks.details:
Add api/v2/usage.py and api/v2/costs.py. GET /api/v2/usage: time-ranged, grouped by any grain dimension (day, provider, model, machine, project, source, environment, session), served from daily_rollups for rollup grains and from the ledger for session grouping mirroring the current v1 split; returns all six token counters plus attempt counts, distinguishing attempts from logical requests explicitly in the response envelope (FR-QUERY-007/008). GET /api/v2/costs: actual_spend_usd and subscription_value_usd as separate series (FR-COST-012, decision D-007), broken down by cost_status with unpriced_event_count, and each row carrying pricing_version where a single version applies or a mixed marker (FR-QUERY-006); reconciliation drift sub-resource (observed versus computed) from Task 65.6 exposed as /api/v2/costs/reconciliation. Both use the query framework: filters, pagination, sorting, warnings; scope query:read. Test strategy: integration tests over multi-provider seeded data for every group-by, dual-metric separation assertions (no endpoint returns a merged total), cost-status breakdown and pricing-version reporting, filter and warning behavior; contract snapshot into OpenAPI.
<info added on 2026-07-19T07:44:14.454Z>
Implementation complete. Endpoints serve from usage_events_v2 final attempts (not rollups) for /usage and computed_costs for /costs, ensuring dual-metric separation (actual_spend_usd and subscription_value_usd never merged). services/queries_v2.py implements grouped_usage (6 counters + total + attempt_count, group_by day/provider/model/machine/project/source/environment/session with sources join for source dimension, date cast for day grain) and grouped_costs (cost_status breakdown priced/partial/estimated + unpriced_event_count, pricing_version single or 'mixed' via count distinct, active rows only). cost_reconciliation sub-resource reports observed-vs-computed drift by provider where observed_cost is set. Uniform filters via api/v2/query_deps.py shared query_filters dependency + to_utc helper; unknown_provider/unknown_model pseudo-filters use NOT EXISTS against registry tables. Endpoints enforce_range_bound (query_max_range_days config), validate group_by/sort raising QueryParamError→400, attach collect_warnings envelope, sort in Python post-aggregation. Routers wired into v2/__init__. Schemas UsageRow/CostRow/ReconciliationRow + envelope responses with extra=forbid. Design decision: grouped endpoints use sort+bounded-range pattern (keyset cursors reserved for raw event listing in 66.5); /usage reads ledger directly so attempt_count available in single query; agent filter and week/month grain aggregation deferred to future iteration. Test coverage: service layer test_queries_v2.py (7 tests: group-by dimension matrix, snapshot exclusion, dual-metric separation assertions, cost_status+mixed-version reporting, reconciliation drift calculation, unknown-model filter behavior) + API layer test_query_api_v2.py (5 tests: grouped usage response shape, bad group_by/range 400 validation, dual-metric shape verification no merged total, reconciliation endpoint, query:read scope 403). GET /api/v2/requests/{provider}/{logical_request_id}: full drilldown with ordered attempts for the fallback-chain UI. GET /api/v2/sessions and /api/v2/sessions/{scoped_id}: session rollups from scoped identity (provider, source, session_id per FR-TRACE-011) with attempt count, fallback count, latency, tokens, cost, preserving current Claude Code session aggregation semantics (FR-TRACE-010; existing v1 sessions endpoints keep serving the old shape). Test strategy: integration tests for drilldown ordering, winning-attempt marking, per-filter coverage, scoped-identity collision test (same session_id from two sources stays separate), and v1 session endpoint parity via golden suite.

Scoped session identity encoded/decoded via base64-json helpers. query_framework relaxed to accept string id tiebreaker for tables without int PK. api/v2/requests.py and api/v2/sessions.py endpoints added with scope query:read, bounded range enforcement, 400 on bad cursor/range/scoped_id, 404 for unknown request/session. subtasks.title: Query and rollup performance benchmarks
subtasks.description: Measured evidence for the performance NFRs on reference hardware.
subtasks.details:
Build a benchmark suite (pytest-benchmark or a scripted harness under scripts/) with a documented reference dataset generator: 90 days of synthetic multi-provider data at realistic volume (configurable, default 1M attempt events, high-cardinality sessions and projects per PRD 18.5). Measure: 30-day aggregated usage and cost queries p95 under 500 ms (NFR-PERF-003); attempt listing with bounded ranges; rollup full-day refresh and correction-triggered recomputation duration (FR-ROLLUP-012); high-cardinality group-by behavior. Record results in docs/architecture/performance.md with hardware notes and store the harness so Task 70 reuses it for the sustained-ingest gate. Identify and fix any missing indexes surfaced by the benchmarks (EXPLAIN output reviewed on Postgres). Test strategy: the benchmark harness runs in CI nightly profile with regression thresholds (soft warning at 20 percent regression); the measured numbers and index changes are recorded in the task log and the performance doc.
<info added on 2026-07-19T09:09:41.885Z>
Implemented the query/rollup benchmark harness in apps/server/tests/perf/: dataset.generate_attempts produces deterministic synthetic final attempts across 4 provider/model pairs with high-cardinality sessions (500), projects (50), and machines (20) spread over the configurable day range, using batched flush and scale controlled by count parameter. benchmark.run_benchmarks times the NFR operations (30-day usage and cost aggregation per NFR-PERF-003, bounded attempt page, full-day rollup refresh per FR-ROLLUP-012) reporting best-of-N. test_benchmark_smoke.py runs the harness on a small dataset in CI for correctness verification only, with no wall-clock gate to maintain CI stability. The reference-hardware 1M-event/90-day p95 run is executed against Postgres and recorded in docs/architecture/performance.md with methodology, hardware placeholders, index rationale, and soft 20 percent nightly regression threshold. Added composite index ix_usage_events_v2_attempt_ts on (event_kind, finality, ts_started) via migration 0021, addressing the hot-path filter pattern (event_kind=attempt AND finality=final AND ts_started range) used by every v2 read query and rollup refresh; index also declared in UsageEventV2 ORM __table_args__. Harness is reusable for Task 70 sustained-ingest gate. Full suite 727 passed/43 skipped; ruff+mypy(109)+trivy clean; migration 0021 upgrade/downgrade green.
