tasks.title: TOK-8: Dashboard Generalization
tasks.description: Replace Claude-only dashboard assumptions with provider-neutral views: global provider and model filters, dual cost metrics, request and fallback-chain visualization, source health, data quality, and pricing administration pages, while preserving the limit-centric Claude views. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.14; Epic TOK-8; FR-UI-001..015; decisions D-007, D-017.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral UI on top of the v2 query APIs (Migration Phase 5). Dashboard work never combines with core ingest changes (PRD Section 23 rule 11).

Design: global provider and model filter store applied across views (FR-UI-002); v2 typed API client layer generated from OpenAPI; overview covers all providers (FR-UI-001); token and cost charts distinguish cache, reasoning, input, and output (FR-UI-003); cost views expose actual API spend and subscription-equivalent value separately (D-007, FR-COST-012) plus cost status and reconciliation drift (FR-UI-008/013); request detail shows the logical request with its attempts and visualizes fallback chains (FR-UI-004/005); latency and time-to-first-token chartable (FR-UI-006); failure rate by provider and model chartable (FR-UI-007); sources page shows freshness and schema-version drift (FR-UI-009); data quality page shows unknown models and unpriced events; pricing administration page manages rate cards with dry-run diff review and apply; labels distinguish zero, unavailable, and unsupported values (FR-DIM-010) and official versus estimated (FR-UI-012); limits pages read window labels from the provider window registry instead of hardcoded WINDOW_LABELS while preserving the existing Claude block and prediction views (FR-UI-010/014); no routes removed (D-017); components must not hardcode provider sets (NFR-MAIN-006).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-8): global provider and model filters exist; requests and fallback chains visible; source health and data quality visible; unknown and unpriced usage visible; cache, reasoning, input, and output distinguishable; existing Claude limit views remain useful.

Test strategy: vitest unit tests for the filter store, client layer, formatting, and label logic; component tests for the fallback-chain visualization and dual-metric cost views; manual end-to-end verification against a server seeded with three-provider fixtures; regression check that all pre-existing routes still render.

subtasks.title: Global provider and model filter store with v2 API client layer
subtasks.description: Foundation: typed v2 client and cross-view filters.
subtasks.details:
In apps/dashboard: generate v2 API types from the server OpenAPI document into src/api/types-v2.ts (build script, drift-checked in CI) and extend src/api/client.ts with the v2 endpoints (usage, costs, requests, attempts, sessions, sources, limits, data-quality, pricing, rollups, providers, models). Add a Pinia-style or composable filter store (src/composables/useGlobalFilters.ts) holding provider and model selections sourced from GET /api/v2/providers and /api/v2/models, persisted in the URL query and localStorage, applied by every view (FR-UI-002); components must never hardcode provider sets (NFR-MAIN-006) — provider lists, colors, and labels derive from registry data through a palette assignment helper extending src/lib/palette.ts. FilterBar component gains the provider and model selectors. Test strategy: vitest tests for the store (persistence, URL sync, multi-select), client layer request shaping, and palette assignment stability; type-generation drift test in CI.
<info added on 2026-07-19T09:50:38.395Z>
Delivered: hand-authored types-v2.ts (Money as string, ISO datetimes), 15 v2 client methods + query builders (V2Filters, appendV2Filters, appendV2Page, buildV2RangeParams, buildV2GroupedParams) on ApiClient, useGlobalFilters composable (single-select provider/model, localStorage + URL query persistence via toQuery/applyFromQuery/FILTER_QUERY_KEYS, setProvider clears model), stableColorMap/keyColor in palette.ts (dedupe+sort for stable hue assignment per NFR-MAIN-006), FilterBar provider/model selects wired to global store (rendered conditionally, emits provider/model in filter event), UsageFilter extended with provider/model fields. Tests: client.test.ts (v2 param shaping + endpoint calls), useGlobalFilters.test.ts (persistence, URL round-trip, model-clear on provider change), palette.test.ts (color stability). 92 dashboard tests pass; vue-tsc/eslint/prettier clean. Decision: hand-authored types over codegen (no existing toolchain, server as source of truth in header comment). Decision: single-select provider/model (v2 API takes one provider + one model per call; multi-select would require fan-out, out of scope). Decision: deferred vue-router URL wiring to 67.2 (first consuming view supplies option lists). Security: npm audit shows 5 pre-existing dev-only advisories (vite, vitest, esbuild, launch-editor); no new deps introduced; Task 72 opened (priority high) to upgrade toolchain.
</info added on 2026-07-19T09:50:38.395Z>

tasks.title: TOK-9: Alerts and Data Quality
tasks.description: Operational alerts for reporting and accounting failures: stale sources, unpriced events, unknown models, exporter schema drift, failure rate, latency, and fallback rate, all filterable by provider, model, source, project, and environment, all content-free. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.15; Epic TOK-9; FR-ALERT-001..010.
tasks.details:
Objective: data quality and exporter health become alertable operational signals (PP-012).

Design: extend the existing rule engine (services/alerting/) with new rule kinds: stale_source (built on source health last-ingest timestamps, replacing machine-only collector_stale semantics while keeping that rule working), unpriced_events (computed_costs cost_status unpriced over a window), unknown_model (v2 data quality events), schema_drift (exporter schema version vs supported versions), failure_rate, latency_p95, and fallback_rate (from attempt outcomes, latency, and routing metadata). All rules gain optional provider, model, source, project, and environment filters (FR-ALERT-002). Existing spend and limit alerts continue to work unchanged (FR-ALERT-001); cooldown and quiet-hours behavior preserved (FR-ALERT-009); alert context stays content-free (FR-ALERT-010); delivery channels ntfy, Telegram, and SMTP unchanged.

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-9): stale source alerts work; unknown model and unpriced usage alerts work; failure, latency, and fallback-rate alerts work; alert context content-free; existing delivery channels supported.

Test strategy: unit tests per evaluator with synthetic v2 fixtures covering warning and critical thresholds, filters, cooldown, and quiet hours; integration test firing each new kind end to end into alert_events; regression tests that the four seeded default rules and existing channels behave identically; content-free context assertion tests.

subtasks.title: Alert rule dimension filters
subtasks.description: Provider, model, source, project, and environment filters on every rule.
subtasks.details:
Extend the alert rule model and engine (services/alerting/): AlertRule.config gains an optional filters object (provider, model, source, project, environment lists) applied by every evaluator when scoping its query window (FR-ALERT-002); rule CRUD API and validation updated; existing rules migrate with empty filters (behavior unchanged, FR-ALERT-001). Evaluators receive a shared filtered-query helper so filter semantics are uniform. Alert event context records the matched filter scope, staying content-free (FR-ALERT-010). Cooldown, dual thresholds, quiet hours, and the firing-state machine are untouched (FR-ALERT-009). Test strategy: unit tests per evaluator with filters applied and empty; migration test for existing rules; API validation tests for unknown filter keys; regression run of the full existing alert test suite.
<info added on 2026-07-20T11:09:47.536Z>
Implemented alert-rule dimension filters. New services/alerting/filters.py: AlertFilters (frozen dataclass, list/IN semantics for provider/model/source/project/environment), filters_from_config(config) reading config['filters'] (absent/empty = unscoped, preserving existing rule behavior per FR-ALERT-001), apply_ledger_filters(stmt, filters) scoping a usage_events_v2 query (source matched by name via sources registry subquery), and scoped_dimensions() returning dimension NAMES only (content-free per FR-ALERT-010).

Evaluator wiring (rules.py): ledger-scanning kinds honor all five dimensions—unknown_model now queries usage_events_v2 (attempt/final, cost_usd IS NULL) with apply_ledger_filters; burn_rate uses new _ledger_burn_rate over usage_events_v2 when filters present (unfiltered rules retain exact v1-view analytics.token_burn_rate path for zero behavior change). limit_pct honors provider only (LimitSnapshot carries just provider); predicted_exhaustion and collector_stale have no applicable dimension (global limit/machine identity) and remain unfiltered. Each firing sets context['scoped_dimensions'] to dims actually applied. Firing-state machine, cooldown, quiet hours, dual thresholds untouched—evaluators stay pure (session, rule, now) -> AlertFinding | None.

Config validation (schemas_alerts.py): AlertRuleIn.config now typed as AlertConfigIn (extra='forbid') with filters: AlertFiltersIn (extra='forbid', five list[str] fields). Unknown config keys AND unknown filter keys -> 422. api/alerts.py stores payload.config.model_dump() into free-form JSON column. No migration needed (config is JSONType; existing rows have {} which parses to empty filters).

Tests (test_alert_filters.py, 11 cases): config parsing + empty cases; burn_rate provider-scoped window (unfiltered fires, scoped-below-threshold does not); scoped_dimensions recorded on fire; unknown_model filtered by environment and by source; unfiltered counts all; limit_pct provider filter (absent provider never fires, present records scoped_dimensions=['provider']); API persists filters + rejects unknown filter/config keys. Full alert suite 39 passed (28 existing regression + 11 new). ruff clean, mypy strict clean, no dep/secret changes.
</info added on 2026-07-20T11:09:47.536Z>

subtasks.title: Stale-source alert kind
subtasks.description: Alert when a reporting source stops ingesting.
subtasks.details:
Add evaluator stale_source: fires when a non-revoked source has no successful ingest for longer than warn or crit minutes (thresholds per rule, defaults from source-type thresholds in Task 63.2), one alert per source with per-source firing state so multiple stale sources notify independently (FR-ALERT-003, FR-SOURCE-007, AC-010); context includes source identity, last successful ingest, and staleness duration; respects the new dimension filters (alert only on selected source types or names). The existing collector_stale machine-based rule keeps working unchanged; seed no new default rules (user opt-in). Test strategy: unit tests with clock injection for warn and crit transitions, per-source state isolation, revoked-source exclusion, and resolve-on-recovery; integration test through the engine loop into alert_events with an ntfy stub.
<info added on 2026-07-20T15:19:12.457Z>
Implemented. Per-entity firing-state machine (FR-ALERT-003, FR-SOURCE-007, AC-010) via nullable JSONB alert_rules.entity_state column (migration 0025) storing {source_id: {state, last_fired_at}}; single-finding rules leave NULL and use existing state/last_fired_at. EntityFinding(key, finding) dataclass; evaluate_stale_sources scans non-revoked sources respecting config.filters.source name filter (other dims ignored, no source-row attribute), staleness = now - (last_successful_ingest or first_seen for never-ingested), warn defaults to per-source-type threshold from 63.2 (collector 30m, gateway 10m), crit = 4x warn (mirrors collector_stale), explicit overrides supported. Split registry: EVALUATORS (single), GROUPED_EVALUATORS (per-entity), ALL_EVALUATOR_KINDS for API validation; is_grouped_kind(). Engine _run_grouped drives per-source state machine with independent cooldown and resolve-on-recovery; revoked/removed source clears state silently. Wired stale_thresholds/default_stale_seconds from settings. API accepts stale_source via ALL_EVALUATOR_KINDS. No seeded default rule. docs/alerting.md updated. test_stale_source.py 15 cases: type/explicit thresholds with clock injection, gateway-shorter, per-source isolation, revoked exclusion, source-name filter, never-ingested-from-first-seen, content assertion, engine per-source fire+record, independent cooldown, resolve-on-recovery, revoke-clears-without-notice, API accepts kind+filter. Full suite 644 passed / 43 skipped (Postgres) / 0 failed; ruff+mypy strict clean, trivy no HIGH/CRITICAL.
</info added on 2026-07-20T15:19:12.457Z>

subtasks.title: Unpriced-event and unknown-model alert kinds

subtasks.title: Failure-rate, latency, and fallback-rate alert kinds
subtasks.description: Operational reliability alerts from attempt outcomes and routing.
subtasks.details:
Add three evaluators over final attempt events in a sliding window: failure_rate (failed outcome share versus threshold, minimum sample size guard to avoid firing on tiny volumes), latency_p95 (p95 of latency_ms versus threshold milliseconds), and fallback_rate (share of logical requests with fallback_count > 0 versus threshold) per FR-ALERT-007/008; each scoped by the dimension filters so per-provider or per-model reliability rules are expressible (US-006 operational side); context carries the measured value, sample size, and scope. Window length and minimum samples configurable per rule via config. Test strategy: unit tests for each metric computation including empty and below-minimum windows, filter scoping, warn and crit transitions, and resolve behavior; property test that fallback_rate uses logical requests not attempts as denominator (no double counting); integration smoke through the engine.
<info added on 2026-07-20T15:49:59.233Z>
Implemented the three sliding-window reliability alert kinds (FR-ALERT-007/008).

failure_rate: over final attempts in the window, 100*failed/total as a percent (failed = success IS FALSE via func.sum(case(...))), warn/crit percent thresholds (defaults 10/25). Silent below min_samples. Honors all five dimension filters (apply_ledger_filters). Context: failure_rate_pct, failed, sample_size, window_minutes, scoped_dimensions.

latency_p95: p95 of latency_ms over final attempts in the window that recorded a latency (latency_ms IS NOT NULL). Values fetched and p95 computed in Python (_percentile, nearest-rank: rank=ceil(pct/100*n)); no portable SQL percentile. warn/crit in ms (defaults 10000/30000). Silent below min_samples. All five filters. Context: latency_p95_ms, sample_size, window_minutes, scoped_dimensions.

fallback_rate: denominator is LOGICAL REQUESTS not attempts (FR-ALERT-008) - counts logical_requests with fallback_count>0 over total in window (ts_last within window), so a multi-attempt fallback counts once. warn/crit percent (defaults 10/25). Only provider (lr.provider) and model (lr.requested_model) filters apply - a logical request carries no source/project/environment - recorded in scoped_dimensions as only the applied dims. Context: fallback_rate_pct, fell_back, sample_size, window_minutes, scoped_dimensions.

Config: window length and min samples are per-rule via config.window_minutes / config.min_samples (defaults 60m / 20 samples). Extended AlertConfigIn (schemas_alerts.py) with window_minutes/min_samples (int|None, ge=1); other kinds ignore them. _config_int(rule, key, default) reads them defensively (rejects bool/non-int/<1). All three registered in EVALUATORS (auto-valid via ALL_EVALUATOR_KINDS).

Tests (test_reliability_alerts.py, 17): failure warn/crit/empty/below-min/provider-filter/min_samples-override; latency above-threshold, percentile-not-max property (19x1000 + 1x100000 -> p95=1000), null-latency+min-samples; fallback denominator-is-requests property, warn, below-min, provider-filter, out-of-window; engine smoke fires+records; API accepts window config + rejects window_minutes=0 (422). Reliability build UsageEventV2 rows directly since make_v1_event hardcodes success=True.

Gates: reliability+api+filters 36 passed; remaining alert suites 49 passed; ruff clean, mypy strict clean (7 files). No dep/lockfile/secret changes (trivy unchanged; full scan at 68.6).
</info added on 2026-07-20T15:49:59.233Z>

subtasks.title: Exporter schema-drift alert kind
subtasks.description: Alert when a source reports an unexpected schema version or malformed batches.
subtasks.details:
Add evaluator schema_drift: fires when a source reports a batch schema_version outside the server-supported set or when a source accumulates validation rejections above threshold within the window (FR-ALERT-006, US-012), using source health data from Task 63.2 and schema_drift data quality events from 61.4; context includes source identity, reported versus supported versions, and rejection counts. This catches a proxy or collector upgraded ahead of the server (or lagging behind a deprecation) before data silently degrades. Test strategy: unit tests for version-set comparison, rejection-threshold behavior, and per-source state; integration test simulating a source posting schema_version 3 batches and asserting one alert with correct context; resolve test after the source returns to a supported version.
<info added on 2026-07-20T16:02:17.497Z>
Implemented the schema_drift alert kind (FR-ALERT-006, US-012) as a per-source grouped evaluator reusing the 68.2 entity-state machine. The evaluate_schema_drift function scans non-revoked sources (honoring config.filters.source name filter) and detects two independent drift conditions: version drift (when reported_schema_version is not None and not in SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSIONS frozenset{2}, mirroring the api/v2/ingest.py schema_version==2 check) triggers critical severity; rejection drift (when recent_error_count from 63.2 source health crosses configurable warn/crit thresholds, defaults 5/20) triggers warn or critical severity, with version drift taking precedence. NULL reported version indicates not-yet-reported and is not considered drift. Context includes source identity, reported_schema_version, supported_schema_versions (sorted list), version_drift boolean, recent_error_count, open schema_drift DQ event count with link, and scoped_dimensions. The evaluator has no own time window since rejection count is already windowed by 63.2's error_window; the now parameter is kept for grouped signature compatibility but unused.

Engine generalization: _evaluate_grouped now dispatches by kind (schema_drift calls evaluate_schema_drift, else evaluate_stale_sources); _resolved_entity produces kind-specific recovery wording (schema_drift: 'back on a supported schema with no recent rejections', stale_source unchanged); GROUPED_EVALUATORS dict replaced with GROUPED_EVALUATOR_KINDS frozenset{stale_source, schema_drift} since values were never invoked through the dict and the two grouped evaluators have different signatures; ALL_EVALUATOR_KINDS and is_grouped_kind updated making schema_drift auto-valid in the CRUD API.

Test coverage (test_schema_drift.py, 12 tests): unsupported version triggers critical; supported version with no rejections remains silent; null version not treated as drift; rejection counts trigger warn/crit at thresholds; below-threshold rejections remain silent; explicit threshold overrides work; per-source isolation verified; revoked sources excluded; source-name filter honored; open DQ event link included in context; engine fires once with correct context then resolves after source returns to supported version with rejections cleared (entity_state normal); API and grouped evaluator guards validated.

Quality gates: schema_drift 12 tests passed; full alert regression suite (stale_source, schema_drift, engine, rules, filters, accounting, reliability, api, seed) 97 tests passed; ruff clean; mypy strict clean (6 files). No dependency, lockfile, or secret changes (trivy scan unchanged; full security scan deferred to 68.6).
</info added on 2026-07-20T16:02:17.497Z>

tasks.title: TOK-12: Multi-Provider Limit Sources
tasks.description: Provider-neutral limit tracking: window registry with labels and period semantics, limit snapshot dimensions for account, organization, and source, new OpenAI/Codex and Z.ai coding-plan limit sources in the collector, gateway-observed rate-limit snapshots, and confidence-labeled forecasting. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.9; Epic TOK-12; FR-LIMIT-001..013; decision D-008.
tasks.details:
Objective: subscription and rate-limit visibility for Anthropic, OpenAI/Codex, and Z.ai on one neutral schema (new epic added by decision D-008).

Design: provider window registry describing window kinds, display labels, and period semantics per provider (FR-LIMIT-012), exposed through the providers API so dashboards and alerts stop hardcoding five_hour and seven_day labels; window kinds remain provider-defined opaque identifiers in storage (FR-LIMIT-001). limit_snapshots gains account, organization, and source dimensions plus limit_amount, remaining, and unit columns (FR-LIMIT-002/003); multiple account or subscription streams are never merged without an explicit grouping rule (FR-LIMIT-005); official versus estimated provenance remains distinguishable (FR-LIMIT-004). New collector limit sources following the AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource pattern (apps/collector, registered through the existing builder registry): an OpenAI/Codex subscription limits source and a Z.ai coding-plan limits source; both are undocumented endpoints, so every failure degrades gracefully to LimitsUnavailableError plus a data_quality_events record instead of failing collection (FR-LIMIT-013); credentials are read locally and never uploaded, matching the Anthropic source. Gateway-observed rate-limit headers ingestible as optional snapshots through POST /api/v2/ingest/limits (FR-LIMIT-010). Forecasting identifies its source data and confidence (FR-LIMIT-008); unknown windows never require schema migrations (FR-LIMIT-009).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-12): window registry describes kinds, labels, and periods; snapshots support account, organization, and source dimensions; the existing Anthropic OAuth source keeps working unchanged; OpenAI/Codex and Z.ai sources report subscription windows; gateway rate-limit headers ingestible; dashboards and alerts read window metadata from the registry.

Test strategy: unit tests for the registry and grouping rules; collector unit tests with recorded endpoint fixtures for both new sources including failure degradation; integration test for gateway snapshot ingest; migration tests for the new limit_snapshots columns; regression test that existing Anthropic window history and alerts remain intact.

subtasks.title: Provider window registry
subtasks.description: Window kinds, labels, and period semantics per provider, exposed via the API.
subtasks.details:
Add window metadata to the provider registry (packages/core descriptor plus providers table supported_dimensions or a dedicated windows JSON column): each provider declares its window kinds with display label, period semantics (rolling duration, calendar reset, or opaque), and sort order; Anthropic seeds five_hour, seven_day, seven_day_opus, seven_day_sonnet with the labels currently hardcoded in the dashboard; storage keeps window_kind fully opaque (FR-LIMIT-001) and unknown kinds never require migrations (FR-LIMIT-009), rendering with the raw kind as fallback label. Expose through GET /api/v2/providers (extending 61.5) so dashboards and alerts resolve labels dynamically (FR-LIMIT-012). Test strategy: unit tests for registry seed content and fallback labeling of unknown kinds; API test asserting window metadata in provider responses; contract test that the dashboard label helper (Task 67.9 consumer) resolves all seeded Anthropic kinds to the exact current labels, guaranteeing zero visual change for existing deployments.
<info added on 2026-07-19T14:05:21.389Z>
Provider window registry (FR-LIMIT-012) is now implemented and validated. Providers declare their limit-window kinds with display label, period semantics (rolling/calendar/opaque), period_seconds, and sort_order. Core packages/core introduces WindowDescriptor and ProviderDescriptor.windows; ANTHROPIC_DESCRIPTOR seeds five_hour, seven_day, seven_day_opus, seven_day_sonnet with exact labels previously hardcoded in the dashboard (5-hour block, Weekly, Weekly (Opus), Weekly (Sonnet)) as rolling windows; OpenAI and Z.ai descriptors remain empty until their limit sources land. Server migration 0022 adds Provider.windows JSON column (defaults []); _provider_row copies windows from the descriptor; seed_default_providers backfills windows onto existing rows only when the column is empty (safe upgrade path, never overwrites user edits). WindowOut schema and ProviderOut.windows added; list_providers returns the registry. Dashboard defines WindowV2 and ProviderV2.windows; lib/windows.windowLabelsFrom flattens the registry into a kind-to-label map; LimitsView passes it to windowLabel (67.2 seam). Contract test confirms the registry resolves every Anthropic kind to the exact prior hardcoded label, guaranteeing zero visual change (FR-UI-014). Test strategy: migration tests both engines with existing-data preservation; grouping-rule unit tests (two accounts same window produce two series); ingest round-trip with full dimensions; v1 limits ingest regression (dimension-less snapshots keep working, golden suite green).

subtasks.title: OpenAI/Codex limits source in the collector
subtasks.description: Subscription-window polling for Codex plans with graceful degradation.
subtasks.details:
Add apps/collector limits_openai.py implementing a LimitsSource for OpenAI/Codex subscription windows, modeled on AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource: read local Codex CLI credentials (auth.json under the Codex home directory, access token only, never uploaded), call the usage or rate-limit endpoint the Codex client uses, map the response to LimitSnapshot rows with provider openai, provider-defined window kinds (primary and secondary rolling windows as reported), utilization, resets_at, account label from the local account id, provenance official; every failure path (missing credentials, endpoint change, auth expiry, network) raises LimitsUnavailableError so collection degrades gracefully and emits a limit_source_failure data quality event server-side via the standard limits upload (FR-LIMIT-011/013). Register under sources key openai_codex in the collector builder registry with enabled default false. Because the endpoint is undocumented, isolate all endpoint specifics in one module with recorded fixtures so drift is a one-file fix (R-008 pattern). Window kinds get registry entries (69.1) with labels. Test strategy: unit tests against recorded response fixtures covering healthy, malformed, expired-auth, and missing-credential cases; degradation test asserting the collector loop continues and other sources still upload; no-token-leak test asserting uploaded payloads never contain the access token.
<info added on 2026-07-19T14:49:31.822Z>
Implemented OpenAICodexLimitsSource in apps/collector/limits_openai.py following the AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource pattern. The source reads the Codex access token and account ID from auth.json located via defensive recursive search in $CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex (token never uploaded). It GETs the undocumented usage endpoint with all endpoint specifics isolated in this module for one-file drift fixes per R-008 pattern. Response rate_limits.primary and secondary windows are mapped to LimitSnapshot records with provider=openai, corresponding window_kind, utilization, resets_at, and provenance=official. All failure paths including missing credentials, 401/403 expired auth, non-2xx responses, network errors, unparseable JSON, and malformed response shapes raise LimitsUnavailableError for graceful degradation; the collector runner already catches per-source exceptions and continues, with limit_source_failure data quality events recorded server-side per FR-LIMIT-013.

Registered as openai_codex in the builder registry with enabled default false, only instantiated when explicitly enabled in configuration.

Extended OPENAI_DESCRIPTOR in the core registry with primary and secondary rolling window entries including display labels per 69.1 registry requirements.

Test coverage in test_limits_openai includes 15 cases: auth.json reading with missing/corrupt/no-token scenarios, healthy response mapping, expired authentication, malformed responses, unparseable JSON, network failures, and critical no-token-leak assertion verifying the access token is absent from serialized snapshots. Full collector test suite passes (53 tests), registry API/services/acceptance tests green with updated openai empty-windows assertions, ruff and mypy clean.

Deferred to Task 76: collector currently uploads limits via /api/v1/ingest/limits using core LimitSnapshot schema without dimension columns, so account label remains in raw JSON and does not populate the v2 account column. Threading dimensions end-to-end requires migrating the collector to the v2 limits upload endpoint.

subtasks.title: Z.ai coding-plan limits source in the collector

Source GETs the undocumented coding-plan quota endpoint (specifics isolated in one module), maps quota.prompt_5h to LimitSnapshot(provider=zai, window_kind=prompt_5h, utilization, resets_at, provenance=official). subtasks.title: Gateway rate-limit header snapshots
subtasks.description: Proxy-observed provider rate limits as optional estimated snapshots.
subtasks.details:
Support gateways submitting observed rate-limit headers as limit snapshots (FR-LIMIT-010): document the mapping in the proxy integration contract (anthropic-ratelimit-* and x-ratelimit-* header families to window kinds like requests_per_minute, tokens_per_minute with limit_amount, remaining, resets_at), provenance estimated (headers are point-in-time observations, FR-LIMIT-004), source identity attached so gateway-observed and collector-official streams stay separate under the no-merge rule; per-window-kind rate limiting of snapshot ingest (accept at most one snapshot per source per window kind per configurable interval) so high-traffic gateways do not flood the table. Registry entries for the API rate-limit window kinds with labels. Extend the conformance fixtures (65.7) with a limits batch. Test strategy: integration test posting header-derived snapshots through /api/v2/ingest/limits with an ingest:limits token asserting provenance, dimensions, and flood-control behavior; documentation review of the header mapping table; no-merge verification with simultaneous collector and gateway streams for the same provider.
<info added on 2026-07-19T15:08:11.943Z>
Implemented: source resolution via SourceRegistryService ensures gateway-observed and collector-official streams for the same provider/window remain distinct (FR-LIMIT-005); flood control accepts at most one snapshot per resolved source per window kind per limit_snapshot_min_interval_seconds (new config, default 0 = off, gateways typically 60s) with rapid repeats silently dropped and keyed on (source_id, provider, window_kind); provenance estimated preserved with promoted dimension columns persisting limit_amount/remaining/unit. Added anthropic requests_per_minute and tokens_per_minute window kinds to registry (labels 'Requests / min', 'Tokens / min') for anthropic-ratelimit-*/x-ratelimit-* header families (FR-LIMIT-012). Documented header-to-window-kind mapping table, utilization derivation, example snapshot, source identity, and flood-control contract in docs/api/gateway-limits.md (linked from ingest-v2.md). test_limit_ingest_flood verifies source resolution to source_id, dimensions, estimated provenance, flood-drop behavior, off-by-default passthrough, and no-merge guarantee for gateway+collector distinct source_ids; targeted server tests and core normalization green, ruff+mypy clean. Conformance-fixture limits batch (65.7) deferred pending Task 65.
</info added on 2026-07-19T15:08:11.943Z>

subtasks.title: Limit forecasting with source and confidence labeling
subtasks.description: Exhaustion prediction across providers with explicit data provenance.
subtasks.details:
Generalize the existing burn-rate and exhaustion prediction (services/analytics.py) to any provider window with period semantics from the registry: prediction identifies its source data (which limit stream and which usage series) and a confidence level derived from sample density and window age (FR-LIMIT-008); subscription windows without utilization history yield unavailable rather than a guess (FR-DIM-010 semantics); multi-account streams predict independently (FR-LIMIT-005). The /summary/now v1 response keeps its current Anthropic-shaped fields (golden suite) while the v2 limits endpoint (66.6) gains forecast objects per stream. Test strategy: unit tests for period-semantics math across rolling and calendar windows, confidence derivation, insufficient-data behavior, and per-account independence; regression test that Anthropic five-hour predictions match current outputs on identical fixtures; integration test with all three providers seeded.
<info added on 2026-07-19T15:37:12.803Z>
Implementation now includes services/limit_forecast.py with LimitForecast dataclass and pure functions forecast_stream and forecast_streams operating on pre-grouped snapshots without database access. Each forecast carries SOURCE (the LimitStreamKey identifying provider, window_kind, account, organization, source_id), CONFIDENCE tier (high/medium/low/unavailable) computed from sample count and reading span, and PERIOD-AWARE logic setting will_reset_first when the window resets before extrapolated exhaustion. Streams with fewer than 2 samples or zero span return 'unavailable' confidence as a real value rather than a guess (FR-DIM-010). forecast_streams groups by (provider, window_kind, account, organization, source_id) ensuring per-account and per-gateway independence (FR-LIMIT-005). Slope calculation reproduces existing analytics.predict_exhaustion behavior on identical fixtures (regression-tested). resource_queries.forecast_limits loads snapshots within a bounded time range, groups them, and invokes forecast_streams. New endpoint GET /api/v2/limits/forecast with query parameters from, to, provider, window_kind (scope query:read) returns LimitForecastOut per stream containing stream identity, utilization, slope, predicted_exhaustion_at, resets_at, will_reset_first, sample_count, and confidence. v1 /summary/now endpoint and analytics.predict_exhaustion remain unchanged, preserving golden suite compatibility. Test coverage includes test_limit_forecast with 7 cases: slope-to-exhaustion calculation, flat/falling usage never predicting exhaustion, insufficient-data returning unavailable, confidence scaling with sample count and span, period reset-before-exhaustion flagging, per-account independence, and Anthropic five-hour regression against existing fixtures. Integration test seeds a rising limit stream and verifies forecast with confidence plus range-bound 400 validation. OpenAPI snapshot updated (additive changes only); ruff and mypy pass cleanly. Dashboard forecast display deferred as additive LimitsView enhancement; v2 endpoint delivery is the milestone.
</info added on 2026-07-19T15:37:12.803Z>
