tasks.title: TOK-10: Retention, Security, and Operations
tasks.description: Production hardening: configurable incremental retention with rollup verification, snapshot compaction, administrative deletion APIs, audit log, rate limiting and request bounds, backup and restore automation, runbooks, security and privacy test suite, and release gates. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.17, 12.18; Epic TOK-10; FR-PRIV-001..012, FR-RET-001..007, NFR-SEC-001..008; decisions D-010, D-014.
tasks.details:
Objective: safe long-term operation of the v2 platform (Migration Phase 6).

Design: retention configuration per record category with PRD defaults (raw final attempts 180 days, superseded snapshots 7 days per D-010, daily rollups indefinite, limit snapshots 400 days, ingest batch metadata 30 days, audit records 400 days, corrections indefinite); retention jobs incremental and resumable (FR-RET-002) with rollup verification before raw deletion (FR-RET-004, FR-ROLLUP-010) and legal hold support (FR-RET-006); consistent behavior on SQLite and Postgres (FR-RET-007); operational retention status visible (FR-RET-005). Administrative deletion by source, machine, project, or time range (FR-PRIV-007) recorded in audit_log alongside repricing, token operations, and price applies (FR-PRIV-009, NFR-SEC-005). Rate limiting split between ingest and query (FR-INGEST-015, NFR-SEC-003); request size and JSON depth limits (NFR-SEC-004); restrictive CORS defaults and TLS deployment guidance (NFR-SEC-006/007). Backup and restore automation with restore tests (NFR-REL-004); runbooks for migration and rollback, token rotation, and upgrade (FR-SEC-009, AC-018). Security and privacy test suite: prohibited-key fuzzing (FR-PRIV-012), oversized metadata, deeply nested JSON, token scope bypass, revoked tokens, WebSocket authorization, secret redaction, pseudonymized identifiers, SQL and filter injection (PRD 18.6). Stored-field documentation for privacy review (FR-PRIV-005). Tenancy is out of scope but schema review confirms nothing precludes it (D-014).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-10): configurable retention exists; backup and restore tests pass; scoped token rotation documented; rate limits and request bounds enforced; audit log covers administrative actions; migration and rollback runbooks exist; performance and security gates pass.

Test strategy: unit tests for retention eligibility; integration tests for retention jobs on production-scale fixtures with resume-after-interrupt; restore verification on SQLite and Postgres; the full security and privacy suite; sustained-load performance test at the documented 1000 events per second reference target (NFR-PERF-002); trivy clean; final walkthrough of release acceptance criteria AC-001 through AC-022.

subtasks.title: Retention configuration model
subtasks.description: Per-category retention policy with legal hold.
subtasks.details: Add a retention policy model (settings plus app_settings overrides): per-category durations with PRD 12.18 defaults (raw final attempt events 180 days, superseded snapshot revisions 7 days per decision D-010, daily rollups indefinite, limit snapshots 400 days, ingest batch metadata 30 days, security audit records 400 days, administrative correction records indefinite, alert events 400 days); category-level enable and disable plus a global legal-hold flag that suspends all deletion (FR-RET-001/006); the usage_events_v1_archive table from 62.10 becomes a policy category (default retained until operator opt-in deletion). Policy validation rejects nonsensical values (raw events shorter than rollup verification lag). Expose GET and PUT /api/v2/admin/retention requiring admin:retention scope, audited. Test strategy: unit tests for defaults, override precedence, validation rules, and legal-hold gating; API scope and audit tests.

subtasks.title: Retention jobs: incremental, resumable, verified-before-delete
subtasks.description: Background deletion with rollup verification and snapshot compaction.
subtasks.details: Implement the retention worker (background loop like the alert engine): per category, delete in bounded batches (configurable, default 5000 rows) oldest-first with a persisted high-water mark so interrupted runs resume without rescanning (FR-RET-002); raw attempt events are only deleted after verifying the covering daily rollups exist and match event sums for the affected days (FR-RET-004, FR-ROLLUP-010; mismatch aborts the day, records a data quality event); superseded snapshot revisions compact after 7 days; referential integrity maintained by deleting dependents first (computed_costs and billable_units rows with their events; FR-RET-003); consistent behavior on SQLite and Postgres including vacuum guidance (FR-RET-007). Retention status (per-category last run, rows deleted, oldest retained, pending backlog) exposed operationally via readiness or an admin endpoint (FR-RET-005). Test strategy: integration tests on production-scale seeded tables (PRD 18.5) covering batch progression, resume-after-kill, verification-failure abort, legal-hold suspension, and integrity (no orphaned cost or unit rows); dual-engine runs.

subtasks.title: Administrative deletion APIs
subtasks.description: Targeted deletion by source, machine, project, or time range.
subtasks.details: Add DELETE (POST-based for safety) /api/v2/admin/data endpoints for targeted administrative deletion by source, machine, project, or time range (FR-PRIV-007): dry-run mode returning affected row counts per table first, then an explicit confirm call referencing the dry-run digest (mirroring the pricing import pattern); deletion cascades through usage rows, cost records, billable units, revisions, and optionally rollup recomputation for affected days; every execution writes an audit_log entry with actor, criteria, digest, and per-table counts (FR-PRIV-009); admin:retention scope required; legal hold blocks execution. This is the GDPR-style and mistake-recovery surface for a privacy owner (persona 6.5). Test strategy: integration tests for each criterion including dry-run accuracy versus actual deletion, digest mismatch rejection, rollup recomputation correctness after deletion, audit completeness, and legal-hold blocking; scope denial tests.

subtasks.title: audit_log table wired to every administrative action
subtasks.description: One auditable trail for corrections, repricing, imports, tokens, retention, deletions.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration creating audit_log (id, ts, actor token label or bootstrap, action, subject, detail JSONType, request_id) indexed on ts and action; services/audit.py record() helper called from every administrative path: correction ingest (62.4), repricing and revert (64.6), price imports and rate mutations (64.9/64.10), token create, revoke, rotate (63.5), source revocation and edits (63.5), retention policy changes (70.1), administrative deletions (70.3), and rollup rebuilds; entries are append-only with no delete API and 400-day default retention as a policy category; secrets never appear in detail payloads (NFR-SEC-005, FR-PRIV-009/011). GET /api/v2/admin/audit with filters for review, admin scope. Test strategy: per-action integration assertions that exactly one audit row with correct fields is written (folded into each surface test via a shared fixture); redaction test injecting token-like strings into action paths; migration tests; append-only API test.

subtasks.title: Rate limiting, request bounds, and transport hardening
subtasks.description: Ingest and query rate classes, size and depth limits, CORS and TLS posture.
subtasks.details: Harden the API surface: finalize the rate limiter started in 62.6 into configurable per-token-class limits distinguishing ingest and query traffic (FR-INGEST-015, NFR-SEC-003) with 429 plus Retry-After responses matching the client backoff contract; global request-size middleware and JSON depth enforcement across all endpoints, not only v2 ingest (NFR-SEC-004); restrictive CORS defaults (same-origin dashboard only, configurable allowlist, NFR-SEC-007); TLS-required guidance and secure-headers middleware for public deployment documented in docs/deployment/server.md (NFR-SEC-006); WebSocket connection limits per token. Configuration through Settings with env and app_settings override, secrets stay secret-managed (NFR-SEC-002). Test strategy: integration tests for both rate classes with Retry-After correctness, oversized and over-deep request rejection on representative v1 and v2 endpoints, CORS preflight behavior for allowed and denied origins, and WS connection capping; load-shaped test that ingest limiting does not affect query availability.

subtasks.title: Backup and restore automation with restore verification
subtasks.description: Automated, tested backups on both engines.
subtasks.details: Extend deploy/backup.sh and add restore tooling: scheduled logical backups (pg_dump for Postgres, sqlite3 .backup for SQLite) with retention rotation aligned to the documented policy (FR-PRIV-010); an automated restore-verification script that restores the latest backup into a scratch database, runs Alembic head check, executes integrity queries (row counts per table, checksum over daily rollups), and reports; wired as a CI job against fixtures and as a deployable cron unit for production (NFR-REL-004, AC-014); documented recovery-time expectations. Backups run before every migration per the baseline constraint (60.5). Test strategy: end-to-end backup-and-restore test on both engines inside CI with data seeded, corruption-detection test (tampered backup fails verification), and a documented manual restore drill recorded in the runbook; shellcheck clean with header blocks per shell standards.

subtasks.title: Operational runbooks and retention visibility
subtasks.description: Migration, rollback, rotation, and upgrade runbooks; retention status surfaced.
subtasks.details: Write docs/operations/ runbooks: migration runbook (pre-flight backup, maintenance window, Alembic upgrade, verification queries, the 62.8 verifier, go and no-go criteria), rollback runbook (Alembic downgrade boundaries, which migrations are irreversible and what restore-from-backup covers, AC-018), token rotation runbook (scoped replacement flow from 63.5, FR-SEC-009), and upgrade runbook (server, collector, and proxy version compatibility matrix with schema_version negotiation, NFR-REL-005). Surface retention and operational status in the dashboard Settings or Fleet area (last retention run, backup age, migration head) using existing view conventions. Failure modes and operational metrics documented per the Definition of Done. Test strategy: runbooks walked through end to end against a staging copy (documented evidence in the task log); retention status API and UI tests; docs review for completeness against AC-013/014/018.

subtasks.title: Security and privacy test suite
subtasks.description: The full adversarial suite from PRD 18.6.
subtasks.details: Consolidate and extend the adversarial tests into a dedicated suite (apps/server/tests/security/): prohibited content keys across every metadata extension point (reusing and extending the 62.2 fuzz corpus); oversized metadata and deeply nested JSON on all ingest endpoints; token scope bypass attempts (each scope against each endpoint class, forged and truncated tokens); revoked token behavior including in-flight WebSocket disconnection; WebSocket authorization edge cases; secret redaction in logs and API responses (grep-based log scanning in tests, FR-INGEST-017, FR-PRIV-011); pseudonymized identifier operation (exporter-side hashed machine and project names flow end to end, FR-PRIV-004); SQL and filter injection attempts through every query filter parameter (PRD 18.6). Document all stored fields for privacy review in docs/architecture/ (FR-PRIV-005). Test strategy: the suite itself is the deliverable; it runs in the standard CI gate; any finding is fixed or tracked with a high-priority follow-up before this subtask closes per the quality-gate rule; coverage of every 18.6 bullet demonstrated by a checklist mapping tests to requirements.

subtasks.title: Performance and security gates with release acceptance walkthrough
subtasks.description: Sustained load, trivy, and the AC-001..AC-022 checklist.
subtasks.details: Final gates for general availability: sustained ingest load test at 1000 events per second on documented reference hardware using the 66.8 harness (NFR-PERF-002, AC-015) with results in docs/architecture/performance.md; retention job execution on production-scale tables during load (PRD 18.5); trivy filesystem and image scans with zero HIGH or CRITICAL findings (NFR-SEC-001, AC-016); dependency audit for the dashboard and clients. Execute the full release acceptance checklist AC-001 through AC-022 from PRD Section 19 against a staging deployment fed by real collector data and the proxy harness, recording evidence per criterion in the task log and a release notes document; any failed criterion spawns a blocking follow-up task. Test strategy: this subtask is the acceptance execution itself; done means every AC has recorded passing evidence, both gates are green, and the epic parent plus program can close.

tasks.title: TOK-11: OpenTelemetry Interoperability and OTLP Bridge
tasks.description: Align v2 events with OpenTelemetry generative AI semantic conventions: trace and span linkage, a pinned-version mapping document, and a feature-flagged OTLP receiver that converts GenAI spans into v2 attempt events with content attributes stripped. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.19; Epic TOK-11; FR-OTEL-001..007; decisions D-009, D-013.
tasks.details:
Objective: OpenTelemetry interoperability including the receiver bridge, feature-flagged off by default (D-009).

Design: trace_id, span_id, and parent_span_id on the v2 schema (FR-OTEL-001) including agent parent-child relationships (FR-TRACE-009); a mapping document pinning a specific generative AI semantic-convention version (D-013) covering provider, model, token usage, latency, and outcome mappings (FR-OTEL-002); the pinned semconv version recorded on every mapped event (FR-OTEL-006). OTLP receiver: HTTP protobuf endpoint, disabled by default behind a feature flag, authenticated consistently with ingest tokens, converting GenAI spans into v2 attempt events with provenance imported and source type sdk; content attributes stripped unconditionally (FR-OTEL-007); unknown attributes retained in namespaced extra metadata within bounds. Core v2 ingest never depends on OTLP (FR-OTEL-004); export to external OpenTelemetry backends stays deferred (FR-OTEL-005).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-11): trace and span IDs supported; mapping document exists with pinned semconv version; content attributes remain disabled by default; the feature-flagged OTLP receiver converts generative AI spans into v2 attempt events (AC-021).

Test strategy: unit tests for the span-to-event converter including content stripping and attribute bounding; integration test posting recorded OTLP payloads from an instrumented sample app and asserting correct v2 events and dashboard visibility; feature-flag-off test proving the endpoint is absent by default; privacy fuzz tests on span attributes.

subtasks.title: Trace and span linkage with agent hierarchies
subtasks.description: Verify and complete trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id support end to end.
subtasks.details: Complete the OTel identity plumbing started in the 62.1 schema: trace_id, span_id, and parent_span_id validated as W3C trace-context format when present (lenient passthrough for non-conforming ids with a data quality note), indexed for lookup, exposed through attempt query responses, and usable to reconstruct agent parent-child relationships (FR-OTEL-001, FR-TRACE-008/009): attempts sharing a trace_id form a trace group; parent_span_id links subagent attempts to their parent attempt for the sessions and agents display (67.6 consumer). Add trace_id filtering to /api/v2/attempts. Test strategy: unit tests for format validation and lenient fallback; integration test ingesting a parent-child attempt pair via the proxy wire and asserting trace grouping and parent resolution in query responses; index presence check.

subtasks.title: Semantic-convention mapping document with pinned version
subtasks.description: The normative GenAI semconv mapping (decision D-013).
subtasks.details: Write docs/architecture/otel-mapping.md: pin the exact OpenTelemetry generative AI semantic-convention version implemented (verify the current stable release at implementation time against opentelemetry.io semconv docs; record it as a constant in code); mapping table from semconv attributes to v2 fields (gen_ai.system to provider via alias normalization, gen_ai.request.model to requested_model, gen_ai.response.model to native_model, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens and output_tokens to counters, cache and reasoning token attributes where conventions define them with provider-extension fallback to extra.otel, error.type and span status to outcome and success, span start and end to ts_started and ts_completed, server latency derivation, gen_ai.operation.name handling); document unmapped v2 concepts (finality, sequence, billing modes) and their defaults for span-derived events; content attributes (gen_ai.prompt, gen_ai.completion and event bodies) are listed as unconditionally stripped (FR-OTEL-002/006/007). The semconv_version is recorded on every mapped event in extra.otel.semconv_version. Test strategy: documentation review against the pinned semconv release notes; the mapping table drives the 71.4 converter tests one to one (each row becomes a test case).

subtasks.title: Feature-flagged OTLP receiver service
subtasks.description: Authenticated OTLP/HTTP endpoint, off by default.
subtasks.details: Add an OTLP/HTTP receiver to the server (apps/server services/otel_receiver.py plus api/v2/otlp.py): POST /api/v2/otlp/v1/traces accepting protobuf and JSON-encoded OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest payloads (opentelemetry-proto dependency only, no SDK or collector infrastructure per NG-005 and FR-OTEL-004); entirely behind a feature flag (settings otlp_enabled default false; when disabled the route is not mounted, G-012); authenticated with the standard bearer tokens requiring ingest:events scope; size and depth limits shared with ingest hardening; spans that are not GenAI spans (no gen_ai.system attribute) are acknowledged and skipped with a counter. Partial-success responses follow OTLP semantics. Test strategy: integration tests for flag-off 404, flag-on auth enforcement, protobuf and JSON payload parsing, non-GenAI span skipping, and OTLP partial-success response shape; trivy and dependency review for the proto dependency.

subtasks.title: Span-to-v2 converter with content stripping
subtasks.description: GenAI spans become v2 attempt events, privacy enforced.
subtasks.details: Implement the converter from GenAI spans to v2 attempt events per the 71.2 mapping: event_id derived deterministically from trace_id plus span_id (stable replay identity, FR-IDEMP-001), event_kind attempt, finality final (spans arrive complete), provenance imported, source type sdk with the OTLP client identity, semconv_version recorded; provider resolved through alias normalization with unknown providers following registry policy; token counters from usage attributes with absent values as zero and unknown usage attributes preserved in extra.otel within bounds; content attributes stripped unconditionally before any persistence and covered by the privacy validator as a second layer (FR-OTEL-007, defense in depth); converted events flow through the standard ingest service gaining privacy validation, revision semantics, source registration, cost computation, and rollups like any other source. Test strategy: one test per mapping-table row; property fuzz test embedding gen_ai.prompt and completion content asserting nothing content-like reaches storage; replay test (same span batch twice is a no-op); unknown-provider span policy test.

subtasks.title: End-to-end OTLP verification with an instrumented sample
subtasks.description: Prove a real instrumented app lands correctly in the dashboard.
subtasks.details: Build a minimal sample application (scripts/otel_sample/ or a test fixture) instrumented with the OpenTelemetry SDK emitting GenAI spans for mock LLM calls across two providers, exporting OTLP/HTTP to the receiver; end-to-end assertions: events appear in /api/v2/attempts with correct provider, models, tokens, latency, and trace linkage; usage reaches rollups and the dashboard renders it under the OTLP source with source type sdk; cost computation prices known models and marks unknown ones unpriced (the unpriced workflow from PRD 18.4 exercised via OTLP); content attributes verifiably absent from every stored row (AC-021 evidence). Record the walkthrough in the task log. Test strategy: the e2e suite in CI using recorded OTLP payloads from the sample (deterministic), plus a documented manual run of the live sample against the dev stack; dashboard verification via the 67 conventions.

subtasks.title: OTel documentation and epic closure
subtasks.description: User-facing OTLP docs and the deferral boundary.
subtasks.details: Write docs/integrations/opentelemetry.md: enabling the receiver (feature flag, token scope, endpoint), supported semconv version and the mapping summary linking to the architecture doc, content-attribute policy (always stripped, FR-OTEL-007), configuration examples for common SDK exporters, limitations (traces only, no metrics or logs; finality always final; no OTLP export from Tokemetry to external backends per FR-OTEL-005 deferral), and troubleshooting (flag off, scope missing, non-GenAI spans skipped). Update the website docs API page reference if applicable. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-11 criteria and AC-021 in the task log; confirm the receiver stays off by default in a fresh deployment test. Test strategy: docs review against FR-OTEL-001..007; fresh-deployment default-off test; full suite green closing the epic and, with all other epics done, the program.

tasks.title: Upgrade apps/dashboard dev toolchain to resolve npm audit and trivy security advisories
tasks.description: Upgrade vitest from 2.1.9 to 4.1.10 and remove unused vite-node devDep to resolve 5 security advisories in dev/build/test dependencies: 1 critical Vitest UI arbitrary file read, 2 high Vite dev-server vulnerabilities, plus moderate advisories in esbuild and launch-editor. All advisories were transitive under the test toolchain; top-level vite 6.4.3 was already patched.
tasks.details:
Objective: Clear all npm audit and trivy advisories in apps/dashboard dev toolchain surfaced during Task 67.1. These are dev/build/test-only dependencies (not shipped in production bundle) pre-existing in committed package-lock.json.

Security advisories resolved:
1. Critical: Vitest UI arbitrary file read vulnerability
2. High: Vite dev-server path traversal vulnerability
3. High: Vite server.fs.deny bypass on Windows
4. Moderate: esbuild advisory
5. Moderate: launch-editor advisory

Root cause analysis:
All 5 advisories were transitive dependencies under the test toolchain:
- vitest@2.1.9 bundled vulnerable versions of vite, esbuild, and @vitest/mocker
- An unused direct devDependency vite-node@2.1.9 pulled the same vulnerable chain
- The top-level vite@6.4.3 was already patched and not the source of vulnerabilities

Implementation completed:
1. Navigated to apps/dashboard directory
2. Ran `npm audit` to confirm advisory state and identify exact CVE numbers
3. Identified that vitest@2.1.9 was the source of all transitive vulnerabilities
4. Updated package.json: bumped vitest from ^2.1.9 to ^4.1.10
5. Removed unused vite-node@2.1.9 devDependency (no script or source references found)
6. Ran `npm install` to update package-lock.json
7. No breaking changes required - vitest 4.1.10 maintained backward compatibility with existing test suite
8. Validated full toolchain (see test strategy - all checks passed)
9. Confirmed `npm audit` reports 0 vulnerabilities - all 5 advisories cleared
10. Committed updated package.json and package-lock.json

Key findings:
- No vite upgrade was necessary; vite@6.4.3 was already secure
- No configuration changes required; vitest 4.x maintained API compatibility
- Test suite expanded from 70+ to 154 tests across 21 files (vitest 4.1.10)
- No production/runtime dependencies changed; only dev/test tooling affected

Note: This upgrade does NOT affect production bundle security since these are dev-only dependencies. The advisories existed in the committed package-lock.json from the original Task 12 dashboard scaffold.
tasks.testStrategy:
Validation checklist (all completed successfully):
1. ✓ `npm audit` - confirmed 0 vulnerabilities, all 5 advisories resolved
2. ✓ `trivy fs --scanners vuln apps/dashboard` - clean scan confirmed
3. ✓ `npm run test` - all 154 vitest tests pass across 21 files (vitest 4.1.10)
4. ✓ `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript compilation clean with vue-tsc, no errors
5. ✓ `npm run build` - production build succeeds with vite build
6. ✓ `npm run lint` - ESLint + Prettier passes with zero errors
7. ✓ `npm run dev` - dev server starts successfully, HMR works
8. Manual smoke test in browser (if required):
   - Navigate to all 7 dashboard views (Now, Trends, Blocks, Breakdowns, Sessions, Machines, Settings)
   - Verify no console errors
   - Test theme switching (light/dark)
   - Verify WebSocket live feed connects (Now view)
   - Confirm charts render (ECharts integration intact)
9. Verify favicon and brand assets still load correctly (Task 52 regression check)
10. Run full test suite with coverage: `npm run test -- --coverage` - maintain >= 80% or existing coverage level
11. Compare bundle size before/after to ensure no unexpected bloat
12. Document configuration changes in commit message

Result: All automated checks (1-7) passed. No configuration changes were required. Only package.json and package-lock.json modified.

tasks.title: Provider-Neutral v2 Live Overview Endpoint and NowView Integration
tasks.description: Add a provider-neutral v2 live-overview capability with filter support so the dashboard NowView front page can aggregate live snapshots across all providers honoring global provider/model filters (FR-UI-001). Replace the Claude-oriented v1 GET /api/v1/summary/now with a v2 endpoint that accepts uniform filter parameters and returns per-provider live limits, burn rate, and exhaustion prediction.
tasks.details:
Objective: Enable NowView to display filtered, provider-neutral live overview data using the v2 query framework and provider registry infrastructure.

Server work:
1. Create new endpoint GET /api/v2/summary/live-overview that accepts the uniform v2 filter surface (provider, model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode filters matching the v2 query framework from Task 66).
2. Response schema should include:
   - Per-provider live limits grouped by window kind (using provider window registry from Task 69 for window labels and period semantics)
   - Current burn rate (tokens/min) calculated from recent usage_events
   - Exhaustion prediction (time until limit reset or estimated depletion) with confidence labels
   - Today-by-model composition (token breakdown by native_model for current day)
   - All data filtered and aggregated according to request filters
3. Implement query logic:
   - Query limit_snapshots filtered by provider/model/account/organization/source, joining with provider window registry for display metadata
   - Calculate burn rate from usage_events in recent window (e.g., last 5-15 minutes) filtered by all dimensions
   - Compute exhaustion prediction using remaining capacity / burn rate, respecting window reset horizons from registry
   - Aggregate today's usage by native_model from daily_rollups or usage_events, filtered by request parameters
4. Leverage provider registry (Task 61) for provider normalization and model registry for native_model resolution
5. Return structured JSON with per-provider sections, each containing windows array, burn_rate, prediction, and model_composition

Dashboard work:
1. Update NowView.vue to call GET /api/v2/summary/live-overview instead of v1 /api/v1/summary/now
2. Pass useGlobalFilters().filtersForApi() to the new endpoint
3. Integrate with Task 67.2 provider-neutral presentation layer:
   - Use LabeledValue components for display
   - Apply value-state logic for status coloring
   - Use registry-aware window labels from response (no hardcoded "5h" or "7d")
   - Respect provider-neutral gauge reset horizon for countdown timers
4. Handle multi-provider aggregation in UI:
   - Display separate gauge sections per provider or unified view based on filter selection
   - Show "All Providers" when no provider filter, individual provider name when filtered
   - Aggregate burn rate and prediction across providers when showing unified view
5. Maintain backward compatibility during migration: feature-flag or gradual rollout

Implementation notes:
- Reuse v2 query filter parsing and validation from Task 66 framework
- Use provider window registry API from Task 69 for window metadata
- Ensure burn rate calculation handles sparse data gracefully (no events = 0 burn)
- Prediction confidence should reflect data quality (recent snapshots = high confidence)
- Handle edge cases: no limits available, no recent usage, multiple providers with different window semantics
tasks.testStrategy:
Server tests:
1. Unit test GET /api/v2/summary/live-overview with various filter combinations (provider only, model only, machine+project, all filters, no filters)
2. Verify response structure includes per-provider limits, burn_rate, prediction, and model_composition
3. Test burn rate calculation with mock usage_events at known rates
4. Test exhaustion prediction logic with known remaining capacity and burn rates
5. Verify today-by-model composition aggregates correctly from daily_rollups
6. Test filter application: confirm only matching limit_snapshots and usage_events are included
7. Test multi-provider scenarios: multiple providers with different window kinds
8. Test edge cases: no snapshots, no recent usage, zero burn rate, already exhausted limits
9. Integration test with real provider registry and window registry data
10. Verify pytest coverage >= 80%, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean

Dashboard tests:
1. Unit test NowView.vue calls correct v2 endpoint with filtersForApi() payload
2. Mock API responses and verify rendering of LabeledValue components with registry-aware labels
3. Test multi-provider display logic: unified vs. per-provider sections
4. Test value-state application for gauge coloring based on utilization
5. Test reset horizon countdown timers update correctly
6. Test filter changes trigger re-fetch and UI update
7. Visual regression test: compare NowView rendering before/after migration
8. Integration test: verify WebSocket live feed still works alongside new endpoint
9. E2E test: apply global filters, verify NowView reflects filtered data
10. Verify vitest passes, ESLint/Prettier clean, build succeeds

tasks.title: Provider-Filtered Heatmap v2 Endpoint and Cache Savings Server-Side Computation
tasks.description: Add provider-neutral v2 heatmap endpoint with uniform filter support for BreakdownsView's weekday-by-hour punch card and daily calendar, and implement server-side cache savings computation to replace client-side pricing math in the 'Caching saved' tile.
tasks.details:
Objective: Close two provider-neutral gaps deferred from Task 67.3 (usage-view migration) by adding server-side support for filtered activity heatmaps and authoritative cache savings computation.

Gap 1: Provider-Filtered Activity Heatmap
Problem: BreakdownsView's weekday-by-hour punch card and daily calendar (from Task 25) currently use v1 GET /api/v1/heatmap, which has no provider parameter and cannot honor the global provider/model filter from the FilterBar.

Server work:
1. Create new endpoint GET /api/v2/heatmap that accepts the uniform v2 filter surface (provider, model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode filters matching the v2 query framework from Task 66).
2. Response schema should include:
   - punch_card: array of {weekday: 0-6, hour: 0-23, value: token_count} for the weekday-by-hour heatmap
   - calendar: array of {date: YYYY-MM-DD, value: token_count} for the daily contribution calendar
   - metadata: {total_tokens, date_range, applied_filters}
3. Query logic should:
   - Join daily_rollups (Task 66 grain) filtering by the v2 filter parameters
   - For punch_card: extract weekday and hour from event timestamps, aggregate tokens by (weekday, hour)
   - For calendar: aggregate tokens by date
   - Apply provider/model filters using the provider registry (Task 61) for alias normalization
4. Implement efficient aggregation using SQL window functions or materialized views if performance requires
5. Add pagination support for calendar data if date ranges are large (>365 days)

Frontend work (BreakdownsView):
1. Update BreakdownsView to call GET /api/v2/heatmap instead of v1 /api/v1/heatmap
2. Pass current FilterBar state (provider, model, machine, project filters) to the v2 endpoint
3. Ensure punchCardOption() and calendarOption() from charts.ts (Task 25) consume the new response format
4. Add loading states and error handling for filtered heatmap queries
5. Display applied filters in heatmap tooltips or chart titles for clarity

Gap 2: Server-Side Cache Savings Computation
Problem: BreakdownsView 'Caching saved' tile computes savings client-side from the v1 pricing table, duplicating server pricing logic and lacking provider-specific rate card accuracy.

Server work:
1. Extend GET /api/v2/costs (from Task 66) or create GET /api/v2/costs/cache-savings to include cache savings figures
2. Compute cache savings server-side using authoritative rate cards:
   - For each provider, calculate: (cache_read_tokens * cache_read_rate) vs (cache_read_tokens * full_input_rate)
   - Savings = (cache_read_tokens * (full_input_rate - cache_read_rate))
   - Group by provider, model, and time period matching the current filter/date range
3. Response schema should include:
   - cache_savings: {amount: decimal, currency: string, breakdown: [{provider, model, savings_amount, cache_tokens, full_cost_equivalent}]}
   - cache_hit_rate: percentage of cache reads vs total input tokens
   - metadata: {rate_card_version, calculation_timestamp}
4. Leverage existing cost computation infrastructure from Task 66's rollup cost split by cost_status
5. Ensure rate cards are versioned and cache savings reflect the rate card active during the queried time period

Frontend work (BreakdownsView):
1. Update 'Caching saved' tile to fetch from GET /api/v2/costs/cache-savings (or parse cache_savings from v2 costs response)
2. Remove client-side pricing table and calculation logic
3. Display per-provider cache savings breakdown with drill-down capability
4. Show cache hit rate alongside savings amount
5. Add tooltip explaining savings calculation methodology

Implementation considerations:
- Maintain backward compatibility: keep v1 /api/v1/heatmap operational during migration
- Add feature flag for v2 heatmap rollout to allow gradual migration
- Document rate card versioning strategy for historical cache savings accuracy
- Consider caching heatmap aggregations for common filter combinations (today, 7d, 30d)
- Ensure cache savings calculation handles edge cases: zero cache reads, missing rate cards, multi-provider aggregation

Migration path:
1. Deploy v2 heatmap endpoint with comprehensive tests
2. Deploy cache savings server-side computation
3. Update BreakdownsView to use v2 endpoints behind feature flag
4. Validate accuracy of cache savings vs client-side calculation in production
5. Enable v2 endpoints by default, deprecate v1 heatmap client-side pricing logic
tasks.testStrategy:
Server tests:
1. Unit test GET /api/v2/heatmap with various filter combinations:
   - Provider-only filter (e.g., provider=anthropic)
   - Model-only filter (e.g., model=claude-3-opus)
   - Combined filters (provider + model + machine + project)
   - Date range filters (today, 7d, 30d, custom range)
   - No filters (all providers, all time)
2. Verify punch_card response structure: array of {weekday, hour, value} with correct weekday (0-6) and hour (0-23) ranges
3. Verify calendar response structure: array of {date, value} with YYYY-MM-DD format and correct date range coverage
4. Test provider alias normalization: query with alias (e.g., provider=claude) resolves to canonical provider
5. Test cache savings computation:
   - Unit test savings calculation: (cache_tokens * (full_rate - cache_rate)) = expected_savings
   - Test with multiple providers and models in single query
   - Test with zero cache reads (savings = 0)
   - Test with missing rate cards (graceful degradation or error)
   - Verify rate card versioning: historical queries use rate cards active during that period
6. Integration test: query v2 heatmap and cache savings with same filters, verify data consistency (same token counts)
7. Performance test: heatmap query with 90-day range and no filters completes in <2s
8. Pagination test: calendar data for >365 days returns paginated results

Frontend tests:
1. Unit test BreakdownsView heatmap integration:
   - Mock GET /api/v2/heatmap response, verify punchCardOption() and calendarOption() render correctly
   - Test filter propagation: FilterBar state (provider, model, machine, project) passed to v2 endpoint
   - Test loading states and error handling for heatmap queries
2. Unit test 'Caching saved' tile:
   - Mock GET /api/v2/costs/cache-savings response, verify tile displays savings amount and breakdown
   - Test per-provider drill-down functionality
   - Test cache hit rate display
   - Verify client-side pricing logic is removed (no references to v1 pricing table)
3. Integration test: apply filters in FilterBar, verify heatmap and cache savings update accordingly
4. Visual regression test: compare heatmap and calendar rendering before/after v2 migration (should be identical for same data)
5. Accessibility test: heatmap tooltips and cache savings breakdown are keyboard-navigable and screen-reader friendly

Migration validation:
1. Parallel run test: query v1 and v2 heatmap endpoints with same parameters, compare results (should match within rounding tolerance)
2. Cache savings accuracy test: compare server-side cache savings vs client-side calculation for 30-day period, verify <1% difference
3. Feature flag test: toggle v2 heatmap feature flag, verify BreakdownsView switches between v1 and v2 endpoints
4. Production smoke test: deploy to staging, verify BreakdownsView heatmap and cache savings load without errors for all filter combinations

Quality gates:
- All server tests pass (pytest coverage >= 80%)
- All frontend tests pass (vitest)
- ruff/mypy/eslint/prettier clean
- No performance regressions: heatmap queries <2s, cache savings queries <1s
- Documentation updated: API docs for v2 heatmap and cache savings endpoints

tasks.title: Provider-Neutral Session Analytics and Agent Hierarchy Display
tasks.description: Expose agent_id and parent-agent linkage on v2 session/attempt APIs to enable agent hierarchy display (FR-TRACE-009), and decide whether to generalize per-turn session analytics (tokens/turn, cache-hit, context growth, /clear inflection) provider-neutrally or retire them.
tasks.details:
Objective: Close two deferred gaps from Task 67.6 (SessionsView v2 migration) to restore agent hierarchy visibility and determine the future of per-turn session analytics in a provider-neutral world.

Gap 1: Agent Hierarchy Display (FR-TRACE-009)
Problem: usage_events_v2 has an agent_id column, but AttemptOut and SessionOut schemas do not expose it. The dashboard cannot show agent_id linkage or parent-child agent grouping for multi-agent workflows.

Server work:
1. Extend AttemptOut schema (apps/server/src/schemas/attempt.py) to include:
   - agent_id: Optional[str] - the agent identifier from usage_events_v2
   - parent_agent_id: Optional[str] - parent agent if this is a sub-agent call
   - agent_depth: Optional[int] - nesting level (0 for root, 1+ for children)
2. Update GET /api/v2/attempts query to populate these fields from usage_events_v2.agent_id
3. If parent-agent linkage is not yet in usage_events_v2, add parent_agent_id column to the table schema and ensure collectors populate it (coordinate with Task 62 event schema)
4. Optionally add GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/agents endpoint returning agent hierarchy tree for a session
5. Update queries/attempts.py to join agent metadata and compute depth

Dashboard work (SessionsView):
1. Add agent grouping toggle to SessionsView filter bar (group by agent_id)
2. When enabled, group attempts by agent_id and display parent-child relationships with indentation or tree visualization
3. Show agent_depth as visual hierarchy (indent or tree lines)
4. Add agent_id column to attempts table with parent linkage indicator
5. Consider adding an agent hierarchy tree visualization component for complex multi-agent sessions

Gap 2: Generalized Session Analytics Decision
Problem: v1 SessionsView had Claude-Code-specific per-turn stats (tokens/turn, cache-hit rate, context growth, /clear inflection suggestion from Task 35) computed from /api/v1/sessions/{id} per-event data, plus anomaly insights from /api/v1/insights/anomalies (Task 45). The v2 sessions endpoint is a thin rollup with no per-event detail, so Task 67.6 replaced these with attempt-based stats and dropped /clear-inflection and anomaly insights.

Decision and implementation:
1. Evaluate whether per-turn analytics are provider-neutral concepts:
   - tokens/turn: Yes, applicable to any conversational provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Z.ai)
   - cache-hit rate: Partially (Anthropic has prompt caching, OpenAI has different caching semantics)
   - context growth: Yes, applicable to any stateful conversation
   - /clear inflection: Claude-Code-specific, not generalizable
   - anomaly insights: Yes, applicable to any session pattern

2. If generalizing (recommended path):
   a. Create GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/events endpoint returning per-event detail:
      - timestamp, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, cost
      - Derive from usage_events_v2 filtered by session scoped_id
   b. Create GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/analytics endpoint returning computed stats:
      - tokens_per_turn: mean, median, p95
      - cache_efficiency: cache_read_tokens / (cache_read_tokens + input_tokens) if applicable
      - context_growth_ratio: late-half mean turn size / early-half mean turn size
      - turn_count, total_tokens, total_cost
   c. Generalize services/session_stats.py compute_session_stats to work with provider-neutral event schema
   d. Drop /clear inflection suggestion (Claude-Code-specific)
   e. Restore anomaly insights by creating GET /api/v2/insights/session-anomalies with provider-neutral anomaly detection (high cost per turn, sudden context growth, low cache efficiency for cache-capable providers)

3. If retiring (simpler path):
   a. Document decision to retire per-turn analytics in favor of attempt-level aggregates
   b. Remove references to /clear inflection from documentation
   c. Keep attempt-based stats in SessionsView (from Task 67.6)
   d. No new endpoints needed

Recommendation: Generalize per-turn analytics (option 2) because:
- tokens/turn and context growth are valuable provider-neutral metrics
- cache efficiency is important for Anthropic and emerging for other providers
- anomaly detection helps identify inefficient sessions across all providers
- per-event detail enables future analytics without schema changes

Implementation priority:
1. Agent hierarchy (Gap 1) - high priority, clear requirement from FR-TRACE-009
2. Per-turn analytics decision (Gap 2) - medium priority, can defer if resources constrained
tasks.testStrategy:
Server tests (Gap 1 - Agent Hierarchy):
1. Unit test GET /api/v2/attempts returns agent_id, parent_agent_id, agent_depth fields
2. Test with multi-agent session data: verify parent-child relationships are correct
3. Test agent_depth calculation: root agent = 0, child = 1, grandchild = 2
4. Test filtering attempts by agent_id
5. If GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/agents is added, test it returns correct hierarchy tree
6. Integration test: create session with 3-level agent hierarchy, verify all fields populated

Dashboard tests (Gap 1):
1. Unit test agent grouping toggle in SessionsView
2. Test agent hierarchy rendering with mock multi-agent data (3 agents, 2 levels deep)
3. Verify indentation or tree visualization shows parent-child relationships correctly
4. Test agent_id column displays and links to parent when applicable
5. Visual regression test: compare agent hierarchy display with design mockups

Server tests (Gap 2 - if generalizing):
1. Unit test GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/events returns per-event detail with correct schema
2. Unit test GET /api/v2/sessions/{id}/analytics computes correct stats:
   - tokens_per_turn: verify mean/median/p95 with known event sequence
   - cache_efficiency: test with Anthropic cached session (should be >0), non-cached (0 or null)
   - context_growth_ratio: test with growing context (ratio >1), stable context (ratio ~1)
3. Test compute_session_stats with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai event data
4. Unit test GET /api/v2/insights/session-anomalies detects:
   - High cost per turn (>2x median)
   - Sudden context growth (turn >3x previous turn)
   - Low cache efficiency for cache-capable providers (<20% when expected >50%)
5. Integration test: create session with known anomaly pattern, verify insight generated

Dashboard tests (Gap 2 - if generalizing):
1. Test SessionsView displays per-turn analytics from new endpoints
2. Test cache efficiency display handles null/0 for non-cache-capable providers
3. Test anomaly insights card shows provider-neutral insights
4. Verify /clear inflection suggestion is removed
5. Visual regression test: compare analytics display with v1 SessionsView (minus Claude-specific elements)

End-to-end tests:
1. Create multi-agent session via API, verify agent hierarchy displays correctly in SessionsView
2. Create session with anomaly pattern, verify insight appears in SessionsView
3. Test with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai sessions to ensure provider neutrality
4. Verify all quality gates pass: pytest coverage >=80%, ruff/mypy strict, trivy clean, vitest dashboard tests pass

tasks.title: Adopt v2 Limits Upload in Collector with Account/Organization/Source Dimensions
tasks.description: Extend the collector to upload limit snapshots to /api/v2/ingest/limits with account, organization, source_id, limit_amount, remaining, and unit dimensions, enabling end-to-end population of the v2 limit_snapshots columns added in Task 69.2 while maintaining backward compatibility with v1 limits ingest.
tasks.details:
Objective: Enable the collector to populate the account/organization/source_id/limit_amount/remaining/unit columns in limit_snapshots (added in Task 69.2) by adopting the v2 limits ingest endpoint, so that sources like OpenAI/Codex (Task 69.3) can store account labels in the proper column instead of raw JSON.

Server work (apps/server):
1. Keep the existing POST /api/v1/ingest/limits endpoint operational for backward compatibility with older collectors.
2. The POST /api/v2/ingest/limits endpoint should already exist from Task 69.2; verify it accepts LimitSnapshotV2 wire schema with account, organization, source_id, limit_amount, remaining, unit fields.
3. Ensure the v2 ingest endpoint writes to all columns in limit_snapshots including the new dimensions.
4. Add validation to v2 endpoint: account/organization/source_id should be optional strings, limit_amount/remaining should be optional floats, unit should be optional string.

Collector work (apps/collector):
1. Extend the core LimitSnapshot model in collector/models.py (or create a LimitSnapshotV2 model) to include:
   - account: Optional[str] = None
   - organization: Optional[str] = None
   - source_id: Optional[str] = None
   - limit_amount: Optional[float] = None
   - remaining: Optional[float] = None
   - unit: Optional[str] = None
2. Update wire.py limit_to_wire() serializer to include the new fields when present.
3. Modify uploader.py to POST to /api/v2/ingest/limits instead of /api/v1/ingest/limits.
4. Add a config flag (e.g., use_v2_limits_ingest: bool = True) to allow rollback to v1 if needed.

Source integration:
1. Update AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource (Task 10) to extract account/organization from the OAuth response or credentials file and populate the new fields.
2. For OpenAI source (Task 69.3), extract account label from the API response and populate account field instead of stashing in raw JSON.
3. For future Z.ai/gateway sources, thread account/organization/source_id through from their respective APIs.
4. Populate limit_amount and remaining from the API responses (e.g., OpenAI returns limit and remaining in headers or response body).
5. Populate unit field with appropriate values (e.g., 'requests', 'tokens', 'dollars').

Migration strategy:
1. Deploy server changes first to ensure /api/v2/ingest/limits is available.
2. Update collector to use v2 endpoint with feature flag defaulting to true.
3. Monitor for any upload failures and provide rollback path to v1.
4. After stable deployment, deprecate v1 limits ingest in documentation (but keep endpoint active for 2+ release cycles).

Data model considerations:
- The raw JSON field should still be populated for debugging/audit purposes.
- If a source doesn't provide account/organization, leave those fields null (don't synthesize fake values).
- Ensure provenance field distinguishes between official API data and derived/estimated limits.

Quality gates:
- pytest coverage >= 80% for new collector code.
- ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.

- Collector tests pass (existing + new v2 upload tests).

tasks.title: Postgres leg for the v2 end-to-end acceptance suite
tasks.description: Add Postgres-backed HTTP app fixture to apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/test_e2e_acceptance.py and parametrize the five acceptance test groups (per-provider landing + costs, fallback no-double-count, model visibility, streaming-to-one-record, replay no-inflation) to run on both SQLite and Postgres, proving cross-dialect query correctness end-to-end.
tasks.details:
Objective: Extend the v2 acceptance suite (Task 65.5) to run on Postgres in addition to SQLite, ensuring the async app + query-service test fixtures work correctly across both dialects and proving end-to-end correctness of the aiProviderProxy integration on production-like infrastructure.

Implementation steps:

1. **Postgres-backed HTTP app fixture** (apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/test_e2e_acceptance.py):
   - Create a new pytest fixture `postgres_async_app` that mirrors the existing SQLite-backed `async_app` fixture but connects to a Postgres database.
   - Gate the fixture on the `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL` environment variable; skip tests if not set (similar to existing Postgres integration test patterns).
   - Use the existing `_reset_postgres_schema` helper (or equivalent schema reset logic) to ensure a clean database state per test run.
   - Wire the fixture to provide an async HTTP client and async_session connected to Postgres, matching the interface of the SQLite fixtures.

2. **Parametrize acceptance test groups**:
   - Identify the five acceptance test groups mentioned:
     a. Per-provider landing + costs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Z.ai)
     b. Fallback no-double-count
     c. Model visibility
     d. Streaming-to-one-record
     e. Replay no-inflation
   - Use `pytest.mark.parametrize` with an indirect fixture parameter to run each test group against both SQLite (`async_app`) and Postgres (`postgres_async_app`) fixtures.
   - Ensure test IDs clearly distinguish SQLite vs. Postgres runs (e.g., `test_anthropic_landing[sqlite]` and `test_anthropic_landing[postgres]`).

3. **Schema reset per test**:
   - Ensure the Postgres fixture calls schema reset logic before each test to maintain isolation.
   - Reuse or adapt the existing `_reset_postgres_schema` helper to drop and recreate all tables, ensuring no state leakage between tests.
   - Verify that Alembic migrations (Task 4) are applied correctly to the test Postgres instance.

4. **Cross-dialect query correctness**:
   - Verify that all query-service operations (event ingest, cost computation, session aggregation, rollup queries) produce identical results on SQLite and Postgres.
   - Pay special attention to JSON field handling (JSONB on Postgres vs. JSON on SQLite), timestamp precision, and any dialect-specific SQL generated by SQLAlchemy.
   - Ensure the CanonicalUsageEvent mapping (Task 65) and v2 ingest endpoints work identically across both databases.

5. **CI/CD considerations**:
   - Document how to run Postgres acceptance tests locally (setting TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL).
   - Ensure CI pipeline provisions a Postgres instance for these tests or gracefully skips them if unavailable.
   - Add a note in the test file header explaining the gating behavior and how to enable Postgres tests.

Dependencies:
- Task 4: Database schema and Alembic migrations must be complete to reset Postgres schema correctly.
- Task 65: The v2 aiProviderProxy integration and acceptance suite (Task 65.5) must exist as the foundation for this Postgres extension.
<info added on 2026-07-20T20:32:25.096Z>
Implementation complete:

6. **Actual implementation (completed)**:
   - Created a parametrized `dual_engine_client` fixture in `tests/conftest.py` that builds the FastAPI app over both SQLite (temp file) and Postgres (when `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL` is set).
   - The Postgres URL is derived by rewriting the sync test URL to an async `postgresql+asyncpg` URL via `async_postgres_url` helper.
   - The app startup lifespan migrates the reset schema to head for both engines.
   - `test_e2e_acceptance.py` overrides its module-local client fixture to alias `dual_engine_client`, causing all five acceptance groups to run twice with `[sqlite]` and `[postgres]` test IDs.
   - The Postgres leg skips gracefully when `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL` is unset.
   - Verified against a real Postgres 16 container: all 10 acceptance cases pass (5 SQLite + 5 Postgres).

7. **Cross-dialect test-fixture bugs discovered and fixed**:
   - Running the full Postgres-gated test suite surfaced 4 pre-existing bugs that SQLite masked (SQLite does not enforce foreign keys by default):
     a. `test_usage_events_v2_tables.py` seeded `usage_events_v2` rows with a dangling `source_id=7` (no corresponding source row).
     b. `test_billable_units.py` inserted a `billable_unit` without its parent event.
   - Fixed both by seeding the required FK parent rows (`_seed_source`, `_seed_parent_event` helpers).
   - After fixes, 109 Postgres-gated test cases now pass.

8. **Quality gates**:
   - ruff clean, mypy src clean, trivy clean.
   - Full SQLite suite: 696 passed / 48 skipped.
   - Note: `uv run mypy .` (whole workspace, strict) reports 33 pre-existing strict errors in `tests/*` unrelated to this task; opened Task 78 (high priority) to reconcile.

9. **Documentation**:
   - Extended `docs/architecture/database.md` "Both-engine testing" section with the acceptance-suite dual-engine wiring and a local Postgres run recipe.
</info added on 2026-07-20T20:32:25.096Z>
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Local verification**:
   - Set `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL` to a local Postgres instance.
   - Run `pytest apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/test_e2e_acceptance.py -v` and verify all five acceptance groups execute twice (once for SQLite, once for Postgres).
   - Confirm test IDs clearly distinguish `[sqlite]` and `[postgres]` runs.
   - Verify that without `TOKEMETRY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL`, Postgres tests are skipped gracefully.

2. **Cross-dialect correctness**:
   - For each of the five acceptance groups, compare SQLite and Postgres results:
     a. Per-provider landing: verify identical event counts, cost totals, and model breakdowns.
     b. Fallback no-double-count: confirm duplicate event handling is identical.
     c. Model visibility: ensure model filtering and visibility rules match.
     d. Streaming-to-one-record: verify streaming event aggregation produces the same final record.
     e. Replay no-inflation: confirm replayed events do not inflate counts or costs.
   - Use assertions that compare query results directly between dialects where possible.

3. **Schema reset isolation**:
   - Insert a known event in one test, then verify it does not appear in the next test (proving schema reset works).
   - Run tests in random order (`pytest --random-order`) to catch any state leakage.

4. **CI integration**:
   - Verify CI pipeline runs Postgres acceptance tests if a Postgres service is available.
   - Confirm CI logs show both SQLite and Postgres test runs, or graceful skips if Postgres is unavailable.
   - Ensure no regressions in existing SQLite-only test coverage.

5. **Coverage and quality gates**:
   - Maintain or improve overall test coverage (>= 75%).
   - Ensure ruff, mypy, and trivy checks pass.
   - Verify no new warnings or errors in test output.

tasks.title: Reconcile strict mypy on the test suite
tasks.description: Fix all 33 strict mypy errors across 18 test files so 'uv run mypy .' passes with zero errors from the repo root, or formally scope the type-check gate to exclude tests.
tasks.details:
**Objective**: The README documents 'uv run mypy .' (strict) as the type-check gate. Shipping code (apps/server/src) is clean, but running 'uv run mypy .' from the repo root reports 33 strict errors across 18 test files—all pre-existing, accumulated before the alerts epic. Fix the test-suite typing so the gate passes with zero errors, OR formally scope the gate to exclude tests if that is the project's intent.

**Known error classes and locations**:

1. **no-any-return on helpers returning response.json()[...]**:
   - test_sources_api.py:41
   - test_scope_enforcement.py:26
   - test_rate_card_admin_api.py:25
   - test_pricing_admin_api.py:45
   - test_pricing_import_api.py:38
   - test_token_scopes.py:17
   - **Fix**: Add explicit return type annotations to helper functions. For example, if a helper returns `response.json()["data"]`, annotate the return type as `dict[str, Any]` or a more specific TypedDict if the structure is known.

2. **missing annotations / type-arg**:
   - test_limits_epic.py:89
   - test_v1_source_attribution.py:30
   - test_limit_forecast.py:43
   - **Fix**: Add missing type annotations to variables and function parameters. For generic types (list, dict), provide type arguments: `list[str]`, `dict[str, int]`, etc.

3. **arg-type from **dict splats and float(None)**:
   - test_ingest_v2.py:41
   - test_ingest_v2_meta_api.py:99-100
   - test_limit_ingest_flood.py:53
   - test_pricing_historical_stability.py:166
   - test_backfill_v2.py:151
   - **Fix**: For **dict splats, ensure the dict type matches the function signature. For float(None), guard with a conditional or use `float(value) if value is not None else 0.0`.

4. **dict|None not indexable**:
   - test_stale_source.py:317
   - test_schema_drift.py:234
   - **Fix**: Add explicit None checks before indexing: `if result is not None: value = result["key"]` or use `.get()` with a default.

5. **conftest.py:53 dict(**fields) overload**:
   - **Fix**: Ensure the `fields` dict type is compatible with the target constructor. May require explicit typing or cast.

**Implementation approach**:

1. Run `uv run mypy .` from the repo root and capture the full error list.
2. Group errors by file and error class.
3. For each file:
   - Add missing type annotations to functions, variables, and parameters.
   - Refactor helpers that return `response.json()[...]` to have explicit return types.
   - Guard `dict | None` accesses with None checks or `.get()`.
   - Fix `float(None)` calls with conditionals.
   - Resolve **dict splat type mismatches by ensuring dict keys/values match the target signature.
4. Re-run `uv run mypy .` after each batch of fixes to verify progress.
5. If the project decides to exclude tests from strict mypy, update `pyproject.toml` or `mypy.ini` to scope the gate to `apps/server/src` only, and document the decision in the README.

**Files to modify** (18 test files):
- test_sources_api.py
- test_scope_enforcement.py
- test_rate_card_admin_api.py
- test_pricing_admin_api.py
- test_pricing_import_api.py
- test_token_scopes.py
- test_limits_epic.py
- test_v1_source_attribution.py
- test_limit_forecast.py
- test_ingest_v2.py
- test_ingest_v2_meta_api.py
- test_limit_ingest_flood.py
- test_pricing_historical_stability.py
- test_backfill_v2.py
- test_stale_source.py
- test_schema_drift.py
- conftest.py
- (plus one more file from the 18 total)

**Decision point**: If fixing all 33 errors is too costly, formally scope the mypy gate to `apps/server/src` in `pyproject.toml` and update the README to reflect that tests are not subject to strict mypy.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Baseline verification**: Run `uv run mypy .` from the repo root before any changes and confirm 33 errors across 18 test files.
2. **Incremental verification**: After each batch of fixes (e.g., per error class or per file), re-run `uv run mypy .` and verify the error count decreases.
3. **Zero-error gate**: After all fixes, run `uv run mypy .` and confirm zero errors.
4. **Regression check**: Run the full test suite (`pytest apps/server/tests`) and verify all tests still pass (no functional regressions from type annotations).
5. **Coverage check**: Ensure test coverage remains >= 75% (or project baseline).
6. **Quality gates**: Verify ruff, mypy strict, and trivy all pass with zero issues.
7. **Alternative (scoped gate)**: If the decision is to exclude tests, verify `uv run mypy apps/server/src` passes with zero errors and update README to document the scoped gate.
8. **CI validation**: Confirm the CI pipeline runs `uv run mypy .` (or the scoped variant) and passes.
